faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for PGIMER Recruitment 2026?

faq.question: Q2. What are the different posts available in the PGIMER Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The PGIMER Recruitment 2026is for various posts including Optometrist, Data Entry Operator, and Helper. Each post has specific eligibility criteria and age limits.

faq.answer: The minimum qualification required for the Helper post in the PGIMER Recruitment 2026 is Class 10 pass. This is the basic educational requirement for this position.

faq.answer: The salary offered for the Optometrist post in the PGIMER Recruitment 2026 is Rs 15000/- per month. This is a project-based position.

faq.answer: Candidates should carry all original documents, including ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if any), and category certificates (if applicable), along with one set of self-attested photocopies.

basic_details.short_information: Andaman & Nicobar Police has released the official Group-B (Non-Gazetted) & Group-C Recruitment Notification 2026 in April 2026 at its official website. A total of 495 (tentative) vacancies have been announced under the A&N Police Group-B (NG) & Group-C Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 30/33 years (varies by post) with qualifications ranging from 10th, 12th, to Graduation can apply online from 27 April 2026 to 26 May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

important_dates.physical measurement & endurance test (p: To be scheduled by A&N Police
important_dates.trade test (wherever applicable): To be conducted by A&N Police

application_fee.details: Candidates (except Female/ST candidates who are exempted from payment of fee) are required to pay a fee of Rs. 100/- (Rupees one hundred only) No Fee for ST/Women candidates of any community. No "fee exemption" is available to Gen/OBC/EWS male candidates and they are required to pay the full prescribed fee. After completing payment: candidate has to upload the transaction slip in the portal in image format.

age_limit.age_details: Varies by post (18-30 years for most SI posts; 18-25/33 years for Constable/Follower posts) as on 17/05/2026
age_limit.details: Crucial date: 17/05/2026 | Age Relaxation: | ST: 5 years OBC: 3 years Govt. Servants / Departmental candidates: Additional relaxation as per rules Ex-Servicemen: As per Govt. orders DRM/Contractual staff: As per A&N Administration circular

qualification.details: Nationality: Candidate must be a citizen of India. | Essential Educational Qualification (as on 17/05/2026) (Varies by post – Graduate / Higher Secondary / Matric Level) | Graduate Level Posts (e.g., Sub-Inspector (Executive), Radio Operator (ASI), etc.): Bachelor’s Degree in relevant discipline + valid driving license (LMV) in most cases. Higher Secondary Level Posts (e.g., Constable (Executive), Sub-Inspector (Master), etc.): 12th Pass + specific technical/skill requirements. Matric Level Posts (e.g., Fitter Electrician, Diesel Mechanic, Follower posts): 10th Pass + ITI/NTC/NAC in relevant trade + experience + driving license where applicable. | Desirable Qualifications mentioned for some posts (e.g., Fire Service experience, Sub-Officer Course).

salary_details.details: Additional Benefits: DA, HRA, Medical, Pension, and other allowances as per rules.

selection_process: Physical Measurement and Endurance Test (PM&ET): Qualifying in nature (Height
Chest for males
Race
Long Jump
High Jump

Swimming for some posts). Written Examination: Objective type (Graduate / Higher Secondary / Matric Level) – Common + Additional papers as per post. Negative marking of 0.25. Trade Test / Skill Test (wherever applicable): Qualifying in nature for technical/driver/bandsmen/marine posts. Document Verification Medical Examination Final merit based on performance in Written Exam + Trade Test (subject to qualifying PM&ET and other conditions).
how_to_apply: Apply online only via official A&N Police website.
Fill details, upload documents/photo/signature, submit.
Print application for records.
Departmental candidates must route through proper channel with NOC.
Keep email/mobile active.
Last date: As notified (closing date 26/05/2026 mentioned in document)
important_instructions: Read notification carefully before applying. Ensure eligibility (age, education, physical standards, driving license, trade skills) as on 17/05/2026. Some posts are open only for Male candidates. PM&ET and Trade Test are qualifying in nature. Govt. strives for gender balance; women candidates are encouraged to apply. No TA/DA for tests. SSC / A&N Police reserves right to modify/cancel the recruitment. Check official website regularly for updates on exam dates, admit cards, etc.

basic_details.job_title: Konkan Railway Recruitment 2026 Notification Out – Walk in for 132 Helper, Technician and Other Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL), A Government of India Undertaking under Ministry of Railways
basic_details.post_name: VDPD Workshop, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh (Selected candidates can be posted and transferred anywhere to KRCL establishment during contract period)
basic_details.short_information: Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL) Recruitment 2026: Get the latest KRCL recruitment notification 2026 for 132 vacancies across 9 posts in the Mechanical/Project Department on a fixed term contract basis under Notification No. CO/P-R/7C/2026. The Walk-in Interview process starts on 8th June 2026 and the last walk-in interview date is 12th June 2026 at Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. Find KRCL vacancies on the official website www.konkanrailway.com. You can also get Free Job Alert of “Upcoming KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026” notifications for freshers and experienced candidat

age_limit.age_details: Fixed Term Contract – Initial period of 1 year, extendable up to 5 years based on satisfactory performance and requirement of KRCL
age_limit.details: The upper age limit is 50 years for all posts as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is provided to reserved category candidates as per Government guidelines. Age relaxation for Ex-servicemen is discretionary.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 01-05-2026. Candidates must ensure they fulfill the criteria before attending the walk-in interview for a particular post. Incomplete or vague educational qualification will be invalid. Post-wise eligibility details are as follows: | Educational Qualification & Experience (Post-wise)

salary_details.details: The consolidated monthly remuneration is inclusive of Basic, DA, HRA, Conveyance, and Mobile Allowance. Remuneration is classified as per A Class, B Class, and C Class cities. Annual remuneration will increase by 4% of the consolidated remuneration after completion of every year, subject to requirement of KRCL and at the discretion of KRCL. No accommodation and no Food/Mess Allowance will be provided by KRCL. | Other Allowances/Facilities: Insurance, Payment of Gratuity, and Bonus as per provisions of New Labour Code-2026 (as applicable). TA as per extant KRCL policy for official travel beyond the project area. Complementary Railway Pass for travel on duty as per extant KRCL policy. Rest House of KRCL if available while on duty; otherwise hotel charge reimbursement as per KRCL rates on production of vouchers. 15 days fully earned leave on completion of every 6 months (2.5 leaves per month); 8 days Casual Leave during the year. Weekly off and public holidays as applicable at place of posting.

selection_process: Stage Details
Preliminary Screening Verification of applications and documents submitted in person. Only provisionally eligible candidates allowed to proceed.
Elimination Round (if required) Group Discussion (GD) or Written Test may be introduced depending on number of candidates.
Interview By nominated Committee of KRCL. Merit list drawn based on performance in interview, qualification, and post-qualification experience.

The selection process for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026 is through Walk-in Interview. Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria may report directly for the walk-in interview at the specified venue with all original certificates and one copy of application in prescribed format (Annexure-A) along with one set of self-attested copies of all required certificates. Registration will be done between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs only on the date of walk-in interview.
After preliminary screening of applications submitted in person

only provisionally eligible candidates will be interviewed. Group Discussion (GD) or any other elimination round like written test may be introduced depending on the number of candidates; only those who clear the elimination round will be called for further selection. The nominated Committee’s decision shall be final and binding. No train/bus fare/TA/DA shall be payable by KRCL for attending the walk-in interview. Candidates should come prepared to stay for a minimum of 2 days if required
at their own cost.
how_to_apply: Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria may report directly for the walk-in interview at the venue on the specified date and time for their respective post. Candidates must fill and bring one copy of the application in the prescribed format (Annexure-A attached with the notification), available on the KRCL website at www.konkanrailway.com under Recruitment > Current Notifications. No online or offline application submission by post/courier is required – candidates must appear in person.

Registration time is strictly 09:00 hrs to 12:00 hrs only on the date of walk-in interview. Applications not conforming to the prescribed format or having illegible/ambiguous certificates or without certificates or incomplete will be summarily rejected. Any kind of canvassing will result in disqualification of the candidate at any stage of selection without assigning any reason.
Documents (Self-attested copies) to be attached with the filled application form:
Copies of certificate in proof of qualification (as per the qualification specified in the Notification Annexure)
Copy of proof of Date of Birth (SSLC/SSC Certificate/Birth Certificate)
Copy of Caste certificate in support of claims, if any (OBC-NCL certificate in prescribed Central Government format; certificate not indicating validity will not be accepted)
Copy of service certificate in support of claims for Ex-servicemen, if any
Experience Certificate issued by previous employer
Two passport size recent photographs
Character certificate from Gazetted Officer/Executive Officer certifying good moral character

All original certificates must also be brought for verification at the time of the walk-in interview. Selected candidates will be required to pass a prescribed medical examination before the contract is entered into. Selected candidates must be able to join within a maximum of 30 days after issuance of offer of appointment. Selected candidates will be required to execute an agreement and indemnity bond of Rs. 50,000/- in favour of KRCL on stamp paper of Rs. 100/- or more.
Walk-in Interview Venue: Administrative Building, Wagon Repair Workshop, Autonagar, Vadlapudi, Gajuwaka, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
important_instructions: Candidates must ensure they fulfill the eligibility criteria before attending the walk-in interview for a particular post. Registration time is strictly 09:00 hrs to 12:00 hrs only on the date of the walk-in interview; late arrivals will not be registered. Candidate can appear for interview at this one venue only; no TA/DA will be paid for attending the walk-in interview. This engagement is on a fixed term contract basis and shall not result in regular/permanent absorption in KRCL service. Candidates must give an undertaking in writing to KRCL to this effect. Selected candidate can be posted and transferred at any time, anywhere to the KRCL establishment during the contract period. Contract may be terminated by giving one month’s notice from either side or payment in lieu thereof. Contract can be terminated without notice if performance is not satisfactory. No accommodation will be provided and no Food/Mess Allowance will be paid by KRCL. Even after contractual agreement, if any document/certificate/information is found incorrect or false, contractual service will be immediately terminated without prior notice, besides legal action. Candidates who are already working in KRCL on contract basis and selected against this notification do not require to follow the condition of notice period for joining the new post; however, a relieving letter from the concerned Konkan Railway authority will be required at the time of joining. The Corporation reserves the right to cancel/restrict/curtail/enlarge the contract engagement process without any prior notice and without assigning any reason. Any kind of canvassing will result in disqualification of the candidate at any stage of selection without assigning any reason. Statutory Income Tax, Professional Tax, and other applicable taxes will be deducted at source. The information on KRCL’s website www.konkanrailway.com at its link Recruitment > Current Notifications should be checked for updates.

faq.question: Q1. What is KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026 is a fixed term contract recruitment by Konkan Railway Corporation Limited to engage 132 candidates across 9 posts in the Mechanical/Project Department for VDPD Workshop, Visakhapatnam, under Notification No. CO/P‑R/7C/2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under KRCL Recruitment 2026 and for which posts?
faq.answer: A total of 132 vacancies are announced for Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical, Quality Inspector, Technician (Welder), Technician (Fitter), Technician (EOT Operator), Vehicle Driver, Lister/Forklift Operator, Hydra Operator, and Office Boy/Helper/Khalasi posts in the Mechanical/Project Department.

faq.question: Q3. What are the walk‑in interview dates and venue for KRCL Mechanical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Walk‑in interviews will be held from 08 June 2026 to 12 June 2026 (post‑wise scheduled dates) between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs at the Administrative Building, Wagon Repair Workshop, Autonagar, Vadlapudi, Gajuwaka, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: Q4. What is the nature and duration of engagement in KRCL Mechanical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The engagement is purely on fixed term contract for an initial period of 1 year, which can be extended up to a maximum of 5 years based on satisfactory performance, conduct, and the requirement of KRCL, without any claim for regular absorption.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility and educational qualification for Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical in KRCL?
faq.answer: For Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical, candidates must have a full‑time Degree or Diploma in Mechanical Engineering or related branches (Production & Industrial, Automobile, Manufacturing, Mechatronic, Material Sciences, Metallurgical) from an AICTE‑recognized institute, along with at least 1 year post‑qualification experience, preferably in repair workshops or manufacturing units.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For all posts, the upper age limit is 50 years as on 01 May 2026, with relaxations for OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer) and SC/ST candidates as per government rules, while Ex‑servicemen age relaxation is as per government guidelines.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary structure for KRCL Mechanical contract posts?
faq.answer: KRCL offers consolidated monthly remuneration varying by post and city class; for example, Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical gets around Rs. 47,220 in A‑Class cities (including Basic, DA, HRA, conveyance and mobile allowance), with slightly lower consolidated pay for B and C class city postings, and annual 4% increment on completion of each year, subject to KRCL’s discretion.

faq.question: Q8. Is accommodation or food provided to KRCL Mechanical Department contract staff?
faq.answer: No, KRCL does not provide accommodation or food/mess allowance to contract employees; however, certain benefits like insurance, gratuity and bonus as per applicable labour codes, TA for duty travel, railway pass for official journeys, and leave benefits are provided as per KRCL rules.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a walk‑in process involving preliminary screening of documents, and based on the number of applicants, an elimination round such as Group Discussion or written test may be conducted, followed by an interview by a nominated committee that prepares a merit list considering qualification and post‑qualification experience.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no online application; eligible candidates must download and fill the prescribed application form (Annexure‑A) from the KRCL website, bring the duly filled form along with original certificates and self‑attested photocopies to the walk‑in venue on the specified date and time for their post, and register between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs only.

basic_details.job_title: SECR Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1191 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: South East Central Railway (SECR), Personnel Department, Divisional Railway Manager Office, Bilaspur
basic_details.short_information: South East Central Railway (SECR), Personnel Department, Divisional Railway Manager Office, Bilaspur has officially announced the Act Apprentice (Trade Apprentice) - Various Trades Recruitment 2026 with 1191 openings. The detailed notification was published on 12 May 2026 at secr.indianrailways.gov.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 15 to 24 years (relaxation applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PWD/Ex-Servicemen) and hold Matriculation (10th) certificate and ITI in the relevant trade can now submit their online applications from 12 May 2026 to 11 June 2026. Direct links to download the notificat

age_limit.details: The candidate should have completed 15 years of age and should not have completed 24 years of age as on 11-06-2026. Age relaxation is subject to submission of latest caste certificate. Date of birth community-wise is as under: | Upper age limit is relaxable by 05 years for SC/ST candidates, 03 years for OBC, and 10 years for Ex-Servicemen and PWD.

qualification.education_qualification: Details
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of application, i.e. 11-06-2026. Only 10th and ITI marks should be filled in the qualification section on the portal — no other higher qualification should be filled, otherwise the application will be automatically rejected. If an applicant belongs to SC/ST/OBC community, they should upload a recent caste certificate issued by the competent authority on the portal. | Educational / Technical Qualification

salary_details.pay_scale: As per rules of State Government of Chhattisgarh (paid during training period)

selection_process: Merit Criteria Details
Basis Average of % marks in Matriculation (min. 50% aggregate) and ITI, with equal weightage to both

Medical Selected candidates may be asked to bring a medical certificate at the time of document verification (signed by Government authorized Doctor Gazetted, not below rank of Asstt. Surgeon of Central/State Hospital)
Contract Selected candidates (or guardian if minor) must enter into a contract of apprenticeship with the employer
Apprenticeship Duration 1 year for each trade
Stipend As per rules of State Government of Chhattisgarh (paid during training period)

Post-Training Obligation It is not obligatory for the employer to offer employment after training completion, nor for the apprentice to accept any employment, if offered. Training will be terminated after completion of apprenticeship.
Selection will be based on a merit list prepared by taking the average of the percentage marks obtained by candidates in both Matriculation (with minimum 50% aggregate marks) and ITI examination

giving equal weightage to both (Establishment Rule 201/2017). Candidates must fill their 10th and ITI marks on the portal in the qualification section; otherwise the application will be automatically rejected. No higher qualification should be filled. There is no written examination — selection is purely on merit basis.
Apprenticeship Period & Stipend
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the website https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in from 12-05-2026 to 11-06-2026 (till 23:59 hrs). No physical copy of the application is required to be sent to the office. Candidates must upload a soft/scanned copy of their present passport size color photograph and scanned signature along with necessary documents pertaining to educational/technical qualification on the website. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date.

If the option of EWS/Ex-Serviceman is not available on the portal, candidates should apply on the portal from their actual category (e.g. UR, OBC, SC, ST) and send/submit the EWS/Ex-Serviceman certificate physically to this office.

Note: Applicants whose father’s name and applicant’s name in their 10th mark sheet, ITI mark sheet, Aadhar card and PAN card do not match, their application will be rejected at the time of document verification. In case of any doubt/contradiction, only the English format of the notification will be valid.
Apply Online at: https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted online only on https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in; no physical copy is required to be sent to this office. Candidates must fill only their 10th and ITI marks on the portal in the qualification section — do not fill any other higher qualification, otherwise the application will be automatically rejected. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC community must upload a recent caste certificate issued by the competent authority on the portal. PWD candidates must produce a disability certificate issued by a competent authority with not less than 40% of disability (Medical Board duly constituted by Central or State Government). Ex-Servicemen candidates selected under the 10% reservation will be placed in the appropriate category (UR/SC/ST/OBC) to which they belong. Candidature will be cancelled if the candidate fails to produce required original testimonials for verification or any discrepancy is noticed. If wrong/fake/false certificates are noticed, the railway administration reserves the right to discharge the candidate at any stage without notice, even after selection. Canvassing in any form shall disqualify the candidate. No Travelling Allowance/Daily Allowance shall be paid to the candidates. It is not obligatory for the employer to offer any employment to any apprentice after completion of training. The decision of the Railway administration in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications and mode of selection shall be final. No correspondence in respect of the applications submitted shall be entertained or replied by this office to any individual or organization. For any query, contact: Mo. No. 9752876639 during office hours 10:00 am to 18:00 pm (Monday to Friday, working days only).

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are announced in SECR Act Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: South East Central Railway (SECR) Bilaspur has announced 1191 Act Apprentice vacancies across 19 trades including 115 Ex-SM and 45 PWD horizontal reservations.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for SECR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online applications is 11 June 2026 (till 11:59 PM) through apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for SECR Act Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 10th class (minimum 50% aggregate) and relevant ITI trade certificate from a recognized institution.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for SECR Apprentice Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: Age limit is 15 to 24 years as on 11 June 2026: SC/ST: 5 years relaxation (up to 29 years) OBC: 3 years relaxation (up to 27 years) PWD/Ex-SM: 10 years relaxation

faq.question: Q5. Which trades have maximum vacancies in SECR Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Fitter (223 vacancies), Electrician (206 vacancies), Wireman (125 vacancies), and COPA (350 vacancies) have the highest number of vacancies.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for SECR Act Apprentice?
faq.answer: Merit-based selection on average percentage of Matriculation + ITI marks (equal weightage, no written exam).

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for SECR Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is required. Applications are completely free on apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal.

faq.question: Q8. What is the apprenticeship duration and stipend for SECR 2026?
faq.answer: 1-year training period with stipend as per Chhattisgarh State Government rules (paid during training).

faq.question: Q9. How to apply for SECR Bilaspur Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Apply online only through apprenticeshipindia.gov.in from 12 May to 11 June 2026. No physical applications accepted.

faq.question: Q10. Will SECR provide jobs after apprenticeship training?
faq.answer: No guaranteed employment after training completion. Apprenticeship is purely for skill training under Apprentices Act 1961.

how_to_apply: The application process for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026 is strictly offline. Eligible candidates can apply to any of the Air Force Stations of their choice subject to the vacancies and qualifications. The application as per Annexure-I (prescribed proforma given in the advertisement), duly supported with required documents, must reach the concerned Air Force Station as per choice within 30 days from the date of publication of this advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar (i.e., by approximately 01-06-2026).

Step 2: Fill Application Form - Download and fill the Application Form as per the prescribed Proforma (Annexure-I). The application form must be filled/typed in English or Hindi only
Step 3: Affix Photograph - Affix a recent passport-size self-attested photograph on the application form. Also attach two additional passport-size photographs (same as affixed on the form)

Step 4: Prepare Required Documents - Attach self-attested copies of all documents: Educational qualification certificates (10th/12th/Diploma as applicable) Age proof certificate (10th certificate/birth certificate) Technical qualification certificates (if applicable) Valid Civil Driving Licence (for CMTD post - for light and heavy vehicles) Experience certificate (minimum 2 years for CMTD post) Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) certificate issued by competent authority (if applicable) Caste Certificate issued by competent civil authorities for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates OBC certificate stating non-creamy layer status (for OBC candidates seeking reservation) Discharge book (self-attested photocopy for ex-servicemen) EWS certificate valid for Financial Year 2026-2027 (issued on the basis of income for FY 2025-2026)

Step 6: Prepare Envelope for Posting - Put all documents in an envelope. Mention clearly on the envelope: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF AND CATEGORY "

Step 7: Send by Post - Send the complete application by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit's postal address (as mentioned in the official notification for the chosen station) within 30 days from the date of publication
Important Notes for Application:
Candidates intending to apply for more than one post must submit a separate application for each post
Applications received prior to the date of publication and after the closing date will not be entertained and will be rejected
IAF will not be held responsible for any postal delay

Note: Detailed station-wise postal addresses are available in the official Employment News notification PDF (Advt. No. 01/2026).

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies have been announced for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force has announced vacancies for Group C Civilian posts through Advertisement No. 01/2026 across various Air Force Stations/Units. The total number of vacancies is distributed across multiple commands: HQ Maintenance Command (14 posts), HQ Training Command (5 posts), HQ Central Air Command (3 posts), HQ Western Air Command (6 posts), HQ Eastern Air Command (6 posts), HQ South Western Air Command (5 posts), HQ Southern Air Command (1 post), and Independent Units (7 posts). The posts include Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD).

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: For Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Hindi Typist positions, candidates must have passed 12th Class from a recognised Board with typing speed of 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi on computer (corresponding to 10500 KDPH/9000 KDPH on an average of 5 key depressions for each word). For Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD), candidates must have Matriculation pass or equivalent qualification from a recognised board or university, hold a valid Civil Driving Licence for light and heavy vehicles, possess professional skill in driving and knowledge of motor mechanism, and have minimum two years' experience in driving motor vehicles.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for all posts is 18 to 25 years, calculated as on the last date of receipt of application. Age relaxation is provided for reserved categories: OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (up to 28 years), SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (up to 30 years), Persons with Disabilities (PwBD) get 10 years relaxation with additional 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC categories. Ex-Servicemen get relaxation for full period of continuous service in Armed Forces (minimum 6 months), and Departmental Employees can apply up to 40 years (45 years for SC/ST).

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned in the official notification for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026. However, candidates must attach a self-addressed envelope with stamps of Rs. 10/- pasted along with the application. The application process is completely offline and candidates need to send their applications by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: All the Group C Civilian posts including Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD) are placed at Level-2 as per Pay Matrix 7th CPC. This corresponds to a pay scale of Rs. 19,900 - Rs. 63,200 plus allowances as admissible. The exact salary depends on various allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other applicable benefits.

faq.question: Q6. How to apply for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is strictly offline. Candidates must download and fill the Application Form as per the prescribed Proforma (Annexure-I) given in the advertisement, affix a recent passport-size self-attested photograph, attach self-attested copies of all required documents (educational qualification, age, caste certificate, PwBD certificate if applicable, experience certificate), attach a self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamps, and send the complete application by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit's postal address. The envelope must be superscribed clearly with "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF AND CATEGORY ". Applications must reach within 30 days from the date of publication in Employment News (i.e., by approximately 01-06-2026).

faq.question: Q7. What is the last date to apply for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of application is 30 days from the date of publication of this advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar. Since the advertisement was published in Employment News dated 2-8 May 2026, the last date is approximately 01-06-2026. Applications received prior to the date of publication or after the closing date will not be entertained and will be rejected. IAF will not be held responsible for any postal delay.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process involves multiple stages: Stage 1 - Scrutiny of Applications based on age limit, minimum qualification, documents and certificates; Stage 2 - Written Test with 100% weightage (question cum answer paper in English and Hindi) covering General Intelligence, English Language, Numerical Aptitude & General Awareness for LDC/Hindi Typist, and additionally Trade/Post related questions for CMTD; Stage 3 - Skill/Practical/Physical Test which is qualifying in nature for shortlisted candidates based on merit in written test. Eligible candidates will be issued call letters for the written test, and there will be simultaneous conduct of test for all categories.

basic_details.job_title: THSTI Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Project Technical Support, Assistant Data Manager and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Translational Health Science and Technology Institute (THSTI)
basic_details.short_information: Translational Health Science and Technology Institute (THSTI) Recruitment 2026 Notification has been released on 11 May 2026 on the official website of THSTI. A total of 7 vacancies have been announced through the THSTI Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 28 Years Maximum Age Limit: 40 years. Age relaxation will be as per the Standard Government of India relaxation rules for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-servicemen etc.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate Degree in Life Sciences, MBBS/BDS/BVSc, Post Graduate degree in Computer Application/Computer Science/Data Science, 12th in Science with DMLT, 10th with DMLT
qualification.details: Project Research Scientist-II: Post Graduate Degree, including the integrated PG degree, in Life Sciences from a recognized university with at least three (3) years of post-qualification experience in the relevant field. Project Research Scientist-I: Post Graduate Degree, including the integrated PG degree, in Life Sciences from a recognized university. Project Technical Support-II: 12th in Science with DMLT or equivalent with five (5) years’ of experience in a research laboratory. OR Three-year graduate degree in a relevant subject/field with two (2) years’ experience in a research laboratory. Project Technical Support-I: Standard 10th with DMLT or equivalent with two (2) years’ of experience in a research laboratory. OR Three-year graduate degree in a relevant subject/field with one (1) year of experience in a research laboratory. Assistant Data Manager: Post Graduate degree in Computer Application/Computer Science/Data Science from a recognized university with at least two (2) years of post-qualification work experience in clinical data management. OR Graduate degree in Computer Science/Computer Application from a recognized university with at least four (4) years of post-qualification work experience in clinical data management. OR Graduate degree in any discipline with a computer diploma (duration of diploma should be at least 6 months) from a recognized Institute/University with at least five (5) years of post-qualification work experience in clinical data management. Project Research Scientist-I (Medical): MBBS/BDS/BVSc or equivalent degree from a recognised university with at least three (3) years of post-qualification experience working with Randomized Controlled Trials in cardiovascular health and diseases.

inter-alia
on verification of character & antecedents
medical fitness and verification of genuineness of sports certificates to the Bank's satisfaction

basic_details.job_title: CRPF Constable Tradesman Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 9195 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has officially released a recruitment notification for Constable Tradesman . For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

important_dates.raw: Notification Release Date: 08-04-2026 Starting Date for Apply Online: 20-04-2026 Last Date for Apply Online: 19-05-2026

age_limit.details: Age Limit for Constable (Driver): 21-27 Years Constable (Motor Mechanic Vehicle / Cobbler / Carpenter / Tailor / Brass Band / Pipe Band / Gardner / Bugler / Painter / Cook / Barber cum Hair Dresser / Washer Man & Women / Water Carrier / Safai Karamchari / Mason / Plumber / Electrician): 18-23 Years Age relaxation is applicable as per rules. | Detailed CRPF Tradesman Eligibility Criteria 2026

selection_process: The recruitment process will consist of Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
Physical Standards Test (PST)
Computer Based Test (CBT)
Trade/Skill Test
Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (DME/RME).
Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Qualifying in nature. For Constable (Driver
Motor Mechanic Vehicle
Gardner
Painter
Carpenter
Brass Band
Pipe Band
Cobbler
Tailor & Bugler): Male – 5 Kms race in 24 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 8.30 minutes. For Constable (Cook/Water Carrier
Barber & Hair Dresser
Washer man & Washer women

Safai Karmachari): Male – 1.6 Kms in 10 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 12 minutes. For Pioneer Wing (Mason / Electrician): Male – 1.6 Kms in 09 minutes. Pregnant women candidates will be declared temporarily unfit. Physical Standards Test (PST): Height
Chest (for males only) & Weight (proportionate to height & age). Height: Male – 170 Cms (minimum); Female – 157 Cms (minimum). Relaxations applicable for certain categories (ST
NE States
Gorkhas
etc.). Chest (Male only): Un-expanded – 80 Cms
Minimum expansion – 05 Cms. Relaxations applicable. Candidates failing PST will be eliminated. Computer Based Test (CBT): 100 marks (100 MCQs)

duration 02 hours. General Intelligence & Reasoning – 25 Qs / 25 Marks General Knowledge & General Awareness – 25 Qs / 25 Marks Elementary Mathematics – 25 Qs / 25 Marks English/Hindi – 25 Qs / 25 Marks Negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. Qualifying marks: UR – 30%
EWS/OBC – 25%
SC/ST & others – 20%. Marks will be normalized. Merit list prepared on CBT marks. Trade/Skill Test: Qualifying in nature (50 marks
minimum 20 marks required). Practical assessment of the applied trade/post. Document Verification (DV): Original documents (Matric certificate
Domicile/PRC
Trade certificates
Caste/EWS/ESM certificates

etc.) will be verified. Detailed Medical Examination (DME) / Review Medical Examination (RME): As per Uniform Guidelines issued by MHA. Temporary unfitness allowed only for pregnancy. Final selection subject to medical fitness. Final merit list prepared on the basis of marks obtained in CBT (subject to qualifying all stages). Separate merit lists for male/female and each State/UT (All India basis for Pioneer posts).
how_to_apply: Application Mode: Online only through CRPF website. No other mode will be accepted.
Steps to Apply:
Visit the official website www.rect.crpf.gov.in.
Click on the link for “Recruitment for the post of Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) 2026”.
Register by providing basic details and generate Provisional Registration Number & Password.
Fill the online application form carefully (personal details, educational qualification, trade/post applied, State/UT of domicile, etc.).
Upload scanned copies of recent colour passport size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per specifications.
Pay the application fee online (if applicable) through BHIM, UPI, Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or CSC Wallet.
Submit the application and take printout of the application form & e-receipt for future reference.
Candidates should bring two coloured printouts of the Admit Card at every stage of examination.

faq.question: Q1. Who is eligible for CRPF Constable Tradesmen Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates who are Indian citizens, passed Class 10, meet trade‑wise physical and technical standards, and are within 18–23 years (21–27 years for Driver posts) with applicable category relaxations are eligible.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released in CRPF Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 9,195 vacancies have been notified for CRPF Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) posts across various trades under this recruitment drive. These posts are open for eligible male and female candidates from different categories, with reservations applicable as per government rules for UR, OBC, SC, ST, and EWS candidates. This CRPF Tradesman recruitment is one of the biggest opportunities in recent years for aspirants seeking a secure defence and paramilitary government job at the Constable level.

faq.question: Q3. What are the online application dates for CRPF Constable Online Form 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window will be open from 20 April 2026 to 19 May 2026 on the official recruitment portal rect.crpf.gov.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for CRPF Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs 100 for General, OBC and EWS male candidates, while SC, ST, all female candidates and Ex‑servicemen are exempted from paying any fee; payment must be made online through debit/credit card, net banking, UPI or similar modes.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for CRPF Constable Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer posts?
faq.answer: For Constable (Driver), the age limit is 21 to 27 years, and for other Technical & Tradesmen including Pioneer trades it is 18 to 23 years as on 01 January 2026, with age relaxation for reserved categories as per Government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What educational qualifications are required for CRPF Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Class 10 (Matriculation) from a recognized board; additionally, Driver requires a valid Heavy Transport Vehicle licence, Motor Mechanic Vehicle requires ITI in the relevant trade plus one year experience, other trades require trade proficiency, and Pioneer Wing posts (Mason/Electrician) need Matric and one year experience (ITI preferred).

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process and exam pattern for CRPF Constable Technical & Tradesmen 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves PET, PST, Computer Based Test (100 questions, 2 hours, 0.25 negative marking), Trade/Skill Test, Document Verification and Medical Examination; CBT is of 100 marks with sections on General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge, Elementary Mathematics and English/Hindi.

faq.question: Q8. What are the PET and PST requirements for CRPF Constable Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For trades like Driver, MMV, Gardner, Painter, Carpenter, Brass/Pipe Band, Cobbler, Tailor and Bugler, male candidates must run 5 km in 24 minutes and females 1.6 km in 8.30 minutes; for Cook, Water Carrier, Barber, Washerman and Safai Karmchari, males must run 1.6 km in 10 minutes and females 1.6 km in 12 minutes; Pioneer (Mason/Electrician) males must run 1.6 km in 9 minutes.

faq.question: Q9. What is the pay scale for CRPF Constable Tradesmen & Pioneer posts?
faq.answer: All selected Constable (Technical, Tradesmen and Pioneer) candidates will receive salary in Pay Level‑3 of the 7th CPC with a basic pay range of Rs 21,700 to Rs 69,100 plus allowances as per Central Government norms.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for CRPF Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must visit rect.crpf.gov.in, register with basic details, log in to fill the application form with personal, educational and trade‑wise information, upload photo, signature, thumb impression and declaration, pay the fee (if applicable), submit the form and take a printout of the final application and e‑receipt for future reference.

basic_details.job_title: Indian Army CSBO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 190 Civilian Switch Board Operator Posts
basic_details.post_name: Civilian Switch Board Operator (CSBO) Grade-II, Group 'C'
basic_details.department: Ministry of Defence, Government of India
basic_details.total_vacancies: 190 (including 12 PwBD posts and 20 ESM posts - Horizontal Reservation)
basic_details.short_information: Indian Army HQ 2 Signal Training Centre, Panaji, Goa has released the official Civilian Switch Board Operator (CSBO) Grade-II, Group 'C' Notification 2026 on 02 May 2026 at the official website indianarmy.nic.in. A total of 190 vacancies have been announced under the CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 and 25 years with a qualification of Matriculation (10th) and minimum 6 months experience in handling Private Board Exchange (PBX) Board can apply via email from 02 May 2026 to 31 May 2026. The official notification PDF and email application instructions are avail

important_dates.interview_date: To be intimated to eligible candidates through call letters via registered email ID only

application_fee.details: The official notification does not specify any application fee for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026. No fee details have been mentioned in the Employment Notice. Candidates are advised to check the official notification carefully and contact the official email ID [email protected] for any fee-related clarifications. No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in Written Test, Practical Test, and Interview - candidates must make their own arrangements for boarding/lodging at 2 STC, Goa.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be between 18 and 25 years of age as on the last date of receipt of application (31-05-2026). Age relaxations are available for reserved categories and special groups as specified below. The crucial date for determining the age limit shall be the last date of receipt of application. No extra weightage will be given for additional/extra/higher qualifications for recruitment.

qualification.education_qualification: Details
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (31-05-2026). Only citizens of India are eligible to apply. Proficiency in handling Private Board Exchange (PBX) Board with a minimum experience of 6 months is a crucial and mandatory criterion - no application will be accepted without a valid Experience Certificate in handling PBX Board. The post is subject to All India Service Liability including Field Service. | Educational Qualification

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 21,700/- + allowances (Level-3, Cell-1 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix)

selection_process: Part Subject Type Marks
Part-I General Intelligence & Numerical Aptitude Objective Multiple Choice Type 25
Part-II General Awareness Objective Multiple Choice Type 25
Part-III General English Objective Multiple Choice Type 25
Part-IV Logic & Reasoning Objective Multiple Choice Type 25
Total 100
Practical Test To check candidate's proficiency in handling exchange operation - Qualifying in nature only (marks not counted for merit)
Interview Conducted along with Practical Test for qualifying purpose only - marks not counted for final merit
Medical Test Only shortlisted candidates based on performance in Written Test, Practical Test, and Interview will be directed to undergo Medical Test as per extant Government Orders
Probation Period 2 years probation followed by permanent absorption subject to satisfactory performance
Pension New entrants joining on or after 01 Jan 2004 are governed by the National Pension System
Retention Beyond 55 Years / 30 Years of Service Subject to screening committee approval; retirement from service on attaining age of 60 years if retained
Disqualification Candidate will be disqualified if found to have contracted a marriage while having a living spouse, or entered into another marriage
The selection process for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026 consists of 3 stages - Written Test (100 marks
merit-based)
Practical Test (qualifying only)
and Interview (qualifying only)
followed by a Medical Test for shortlisted candidates. Selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit in the Written Test
subject to the candidate qualifying in the Practical Test and Interview. To reduce the number of candidates for the Written Examination
screening of applications may be carried out based on percentage of marks obtained in Matriculation/10th Board examination.
Stage 1 - Written Test (100 Marks - Merit Based)
The question paper will be bilingual (English and Hindi). However
questions of General English subject will be in English only. No extra weightage will be given for additional/extra/higher qualifications.
Stage 2 - Practical Test and Interview (Qualifying Only)
Stage 3 - Medical Test
Terms of Engagement

syllabus.details: The Written Test consists of 4 parts - General Intelligence & Numerical Aptitude, General Awareness, General English, and Logic & Reasoning - each carrying 25 marks (total 100 marks), all Objective Multiple Choice Type. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi) except the General English section which is in English only. After the Written Test, shortlisted candidates undergo a Practical Test and Interview (both qualifying in nature) to assess proficiency in handling exchange/PBX Board operations.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official email ID [email protected]. Hard copy applications sent by post will not be accepted. The application window is open from 02-05-2026 (0001 Hrs) to 31-05-2026 (2359 Hrs). Candidates must open the PDF application form in Adobe Acrobat Reader ONLY - do NOT open in browser (Chrome/Edge) as fields may not work. All fields marked with * are mandatory and must not be left blank. All text must be typed in BLOCK LETTERS (CAPITALS).

Candidates should not send their application more than once. Incomplete or wrongly named applications will be rejected without notice. Applications with missing attachments, unsigned forms, or mismatched information will be summarily rejected. Candidates must carry originals of all documents for verification while appearing for examination along with two additional self-attested photographs. For difficulties in filling the application form, candidates may send a screenshot of the issue to [email protected].
Documents to be Submitted Along with Application (as self-attested attachments to email):
Birth Certificate (Matriculation Certificate/Mark Sheet can be produced in lieu)
Educational Certificate - Matriculation Passing Certificate and Mark Sheet
Experience Certificate in handling PBX/EPABX Board (minimum 6 months duration - mandatory)
Residential Certificate
Domicile Certificate
Aadhaar Card (mandatory); PAN Card (optional)
Caste/Category Certificate in prescribed format (for all reserved categories - SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD)
Discharge Certificate (mandatory for Ex-Servicemen candidates, in addition to category certificate)
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer (if already a Government servant)
Passport size photograph (JPG file - latest, not older than 3 months; saved as PHOTO_YourFullName.jpg)
Signed application form saved as CSBO_YourFullName.pdf
Applications must be emailed to: [email protected] with the subject line: CSBO Application – YOUR FULL NAME
Applications must be addressed to: The Commandant, Headquarters, 2 Signal Training Centre, Panaji (Goa) – 403001
important_instructions: Only citizens of India are eligible to apply; the post is subject to All India Service Liability including Field Service. PBX Board handling experience of minimum 6 months is mandatory - no application will be accepted without the Experience Certificate. No hard copy applications will be accepted - submission via official email only ([email protected]). No application fee is required; no TA/DA is admissible for Written Test, Practical Test, and Interview. Do not send your application more than once - incomplete or wrongly named applications will be rejected without notice. Shortlisted candidates will be intimated via call letters sent to their registered email ID only - department not responsible for non-receipt of call letters. Screening of applications may be done based on Matriculation/10th Board marks percentage to reduce the number of candidates for Written Examination. Merely fulfilling the basic selection criteria does not automatically entitle a person to be called for test/interview. Persons working in Central/State Govt/PSU must apply through proper channel with NOC from their establishment. Ex-Servicemen candidates must enclose self-attested photocopy of complete discharge book along with the undertaking format provided with the application. EWS candidates must provide income/asset certificate from the competent authority. Candidates must carry originals of all documents for verification while appearing for examination along with two additional self-attested photographs. Falsification of documents at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature. Attempts to influence the selection process or use of unfair means (forgery, impersonation, false information) may lead to termination of candidature or legal action. The Appointing Authority has the right to cancel/postpone/suspend/terminate/modify the notification without prior notice at any stage. Recruitment Board's decision will be final in all matters.

faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 25 years as on 31 May 2026 for general candidates, with upper age relaxations for OBC, SC/ST, PwBD, Ex‑Servicemen, and departmental candidates as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: What is the salary for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a pay scale of Rs. 21,700/- plus allowances in Pay Level‑3 (7th CPC), with All India Service Liability including field service.

faq.question: What is the selection process for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process comprises a Written Test of 100 marks, followed by a Practical Test and Interview (both qualifying in nature), and finally a Medical Examination for shortlisted candidates.

faq.question: Is there any application fee for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As per the recruitment details, there is no application fee (Nil) for applying to Indian Army CSBO Grade‑II 2026, and candidates must bear their own expenses for appearing in tests.

faq.question: How can I apply for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must download and fill the prescribed PDF form from the official notification, save it correctly, and email the filled form along with all self‑attested documents to the official email ID sunshine.rise@nic.in before 31 May 2026, 23:59 hrs.

faq.question: What is the exam pattern for Indian Army CSBO Grade-II Written Test?
faq.answer: The Written Test is objective (MCQ) type for 100 marks, divided into four sections: General Intelligence & Numerical Aptitude (25 marks), General Awareness (25 marks), General English (25 marks), and Logic & Reasoning (25 marks), with the paper set bilingually except for General English.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in SSB Constable GD Sports Quota Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Sashastra Seema Bal has announced a total of 404 vacancies for Constable (General Duty) posts under the Sports Quota. faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for SSB Constable GD Sports Quota 2026?

faq.question: Q4. What sports achievements are required for SSB Sports Quota Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants should have represented India in international competitions or won medals in National Championships, Khelo India Games, SGFI events, Inter-University tournaments, or National School Games. faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive salary under Pay Matrix Level-3 with pay scale of Rs. 21,700 to Rs. 69,100 along with other allowances admissible to SSB personnel. The selection process for FRI Group C Recruitment 2026 consists of 3 successive stages - Stage-I: Computer Based Test (CBT)
Stage-II: Descriptive Test

and Stage-III: Skill/Trade Test (for specific posts only). Final merit list will be prepared based on marks secured in the CBT for candidates who qualify both the Descriptive Test and Skill/Trade Test (wherever applicable). No interview will be conducted. Shortlisted candidates (05 times the number of vacancies for each advertised category of post) will be called for the Descriptive Test based on their CBT scores.
Stage-I: Computer Based Test (CBT)
Stage-II: Descriptive Test (Qualifying Nature Only)

Descriptive Test will be of qualifying nature only and is intended to test elementary language skills required for Group-C posts. It will be held for all posts in Dehradun only in Pen and Paper Mode. In case of failure to attain minimum qualifying marks (50% of maximum marks)
the candidature will not be considered for final selection/merit. Candidates must not write any personal identity (name
roll number
mobile number
address etc.) inside the Answer Book - failure will be treated as Unfair Means (UFM) and will result in zero marks and rejection of candidature.
Stage-III: Skill/Trade Test (Qualifying Nature Only - For Specific Posts)

Skill/Trade Test will be of qualifying nature only and will be held for candidates who successfully pass the Descriptive Test. Final merit list will be prepared based on marks secured in CBT (with tie-breaking rules as applicable) for the candidates who qualify in the Descriptive and Skill/Trade Test (wherever applicable).
Tie-Breaking Rules for CBT
CBT Tentative Schedule

Note: FRI will not entertain any request for change in test date/time/Centre in case of any clash in the test date/time for any two or more posts. Candidates need to decide the test they would like to take.
how_to_apply: Online submission of application can only be made from the link provided on the official FRI website at https://fri.icfre.gov.in. Candidates should read the instructions carefully before filling up the online application form. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates who desire to apply for more than one post should apply online separately for each post. However, FRI will not be responsible if candidates get the exam centre at long distances or on the same date/time.

Candidates are advised to submit online applications well before the closing date to avoid the possibility of disconnection/inability or failure to login to the FRI website due to heavy load during the closing date. After final submission, no request with respect to change in any data submitted in the online application form will be entertained. In case of any corrigendum/addendum pertaining to this advertisement, the same shall be published on the Institute's website only.
Documents / Upload Requirements:
Recent passport size colour photograph (taken against a light-coloured, preferably white, background; face clearly visible)
Signature (signed on white paper with Black/Blue ink pen - must be of applicant only)
Left hand thumb impression (scanned digital image)
All relevant certificates (educational qualification, age proof, category certificate, etc.) as per instructions given during filling the online application form
Documents to be Produced at the Time of Document Verification (Originals + Copies):
Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (from 10th onwards as applicable)
Age proof certificate
SC/ST Certificate in the prescribed format (as applicable for appointment to posts under Govt. of India)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format valid for FY 2025-26 (issued before last date of application submission)
EWS Income & Asset Certificate in prescribed format from a Competent Authority
PwD Certificate in the prescribed format (as applicable for appointment to posts under Govt. of India)
Ex-Serviceman discharge/service certificate and undertaking (in prescribed format)
NOC from employer (for candidates working in Government Departments, Public Sector Undertakings and Government Funded Research Agencies) - uploaded at the time of form filling
All documents uploaded during online application must be produced at the time of document verification
Apply Online at: https://fri.icfre.gov.in
important_instructions: Online applications only - no other mode of application will be accepted; applications incomplete in any respect will be summarily rejected. Candidates must fully satisfy themselves about their eligibility before applying - if ineligibility is detected at any stage before or after appointment, candidature/appointment shall be cancelled/terminated immediately. Examination fee once paid will not be refunded or re-adjusted under any circumstances - application fee will be refunded only in the event of a post becoming zero. All posts are temporary in nature but likely to continue further; candidates will be confirmed after successfully completing probationary period as per rules. Candidates who desire to apply for more than one post must apply online separately for each post. FRI will not entertain any request for change in test date/time/Centre in case of any clash - candidates need to decide the test they would like to take. Candidates working in Government Departments, PSUs, and Government Funded Research Agencies must send application through proper channel and submit NOC at the time of document verification. For OBC-NCL candidates, the certificate must be valid for FY 2025-26 and issued before the last date of submission of application. Candidates must not write personal identity (name, roll number, mobile number, address etc.) inside the Answer Book during Descriptive Test - failure will be treated as Unfair Means (UFM). Photograph must be a recent passport size colour photograph taken against a light-coloured preferably white background; if photograph and signature are not clearly visible, candidature will be liable to be rejected. Canvassing in any form and/or bringing any influence, political or otherwise, will definitely be treated as a disqualification for the post applied for. All future correspondences regarding the selection process, shortlisting of candidates and important dates shall be uploaded on the Institute's website https://fri.icfre.gov.in - candidates are responsible to check the FRI website regularly. Appointment is subject to candidates being found medically fit as per the requirements of the Institute. Legal disputes, if any, shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the competent court in the state of Uttarakhand.

faq.question: Q1. What is FRI Dehradun Group C Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: FRI Dehradun Group C Recruitment 2026 is a Central Government recruitment drive by ICFRE–Forest Research Institute, Dehradun for various Group C posts like MTS, LDC, Forest Guard, Technician, Technical Assistant, Store Keeper, Stenographer, and Library Information Assistant, with a total of 91 vacancies.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in FRI Dehradun Group C Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 91 Group C vacancies have been notified, including posts such as Library Information Assistant, Technical Assistant, Stenographer Grade II, Technician (Field/Lab Research and Maintenance), Store Keeper, LDC, Forest Guard, and Multi Tasking Staff (MTS).

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for FRI Dehradun Group C Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts from 04 May 2026, and the last date to apply online is 03 June 2026 as per the recruitment details for FRI Group C posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for FRI Dehradun Group C posts?
faq.answer: Eligibility is post‑wise: 10th pass candidates can apply for MTS, Technician, and Forest Guard; 12th pass candidates can apply for LDC and Stenographer; ITI holders can apply for Technician trades; and graduates can apply for Library Information Assistant and Technical Assistant posts, along with fulfilling required physical and skill standards wherever applicable.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for FRI Dehradun Group C Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for FRI Dehradun Group C posts is generally 18 to 30 years, varying slightly post‑wise as per the notification, with applicable relaxations for reserved categories as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary or pay level for FRI Dehradun Group C posts?
faq.answer: The salary is as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix from Level‑1 to Level‑6: MTS is Level‑1; LDC, Forest Guard, and Technician are Level‑2; Technician (Field/Lab Research) and Store Keeper are Level‑3; Stenographer Grade II is Level‑4; Technical Assistant is Level‑5; and Library Information Assistant is Level‑6.

faq.answer: Candidates must visit the official FRI website [fri.icfre.gov.in], open the Group C Recruitment 2026 section, complete registration with a valid email ID and mobile number, fill the online application form, upload required documents, photograph and signature, pay the application fee if applicable, and finally submit and download/print the application form.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are required for FRI Dehradun Group C online application?
faq.answer: Applicants typically need to upload scanned copies of their educational certificates (10th/12th/ITI/Graduation as applicable), caste/category certificate (if claiming reservation), photo and signature, and any other documents prescribed in the detailed notification such as disability certificate for PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q1. What is Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026 is an offline recruitment drive by the Office of the District Commandant, Homeguards Dehradun, to fill 152 posts of Male Homeguard Volunteer for May–June 2026 under the Uttarakhand Homeguards & Civil Security Department.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 152 vacancies have been notified for Male Homeguard Volunteers, out of an approved strength of 1,038 posts, with 886 already filled and 152 posts lying vacant.

faq.question: Q3. What are the application dates for Dehradun Homeguard Volunteer 2026?
faq.answer: The application process starts on 01 May 2026 and the last date for submitting duly filled application forms in person or by post to the District Commandant Homeguards, Dehradun, is 22 May 2026 up to 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Q4. Who can apply for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only permanent residents of Dehradun district are eligible to apply, and they must possess a valid domicile certificate issued by a competent authority of Dehradun as per the applicable Government Orders.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for Dehradun Male Homeguard posts?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualification is 10th pass from any recognized board or institution approved by the Uttarakhand State Government; possessing NCC/NSS/Scout Guide certificates and knowledge of the local language is desirable.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 40 years of age as on 01 January 2026, with a relaxation of up to 5 years for SC, ST, OBC, Freedom Fighter dependants and other reserved categories, as per Uttarakhand Government norms.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No recruitment or application fee has been prescribed in the notification; candidates can submit their applications free of cost, either personally or by post to the designated address.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Dehradun Homeguard Volunteer 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Physical Measurement (qualifying), Physical Efficiency Test (PET) carrying 30 marks, and preparation of a final merit list out of 60 marks (including marks for PET, education, NCC/NSS/Scout, Homeguard family, sports and vehicle driving), followed by a medical examination for medically fit candidates.

faq.question: Q9. What are the physical standards and PET details for Dehradun Homeguard posts?
faq.answer: Male candidates must meet the minimum height and chest measurements as per category and area (General/OBC/SC, ST, and mountainous region), including at least 5 cm chest expansion, and must qualify the PET comprising a 2 km run/walk, cricket ball throw and long jump, each evaluated on a 10‑mark scale.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must download or collect the prescribed application form from the Dehradun Homeguards Facebook page or office notice board, fill it completely, attach self‑attested copies of required documents (educational, domicile, caste, EWS, hill area, character certificate, NOC, etc.) and submit it in person or by post to the District Commandant, Homeguards, Dehradun, Nanda Ki Chowki, Chakrata Road, Dehradun – 248007, on or before 22 May 2026.

faq.question: What is Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026 is an offline recruitment drive by the Office of the District Commandant, Homeguards Dehradun, to fill 152 posts of Male Homeguard Volunteer for May–June 2026 under the Uttarakhand Homeguards & Civil Security Department.

faq.question: How many vacancies are there in Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 152 vacancies have been notified for Male Homeguard Volunteers, out of an approved strength of 1,038 posts, with 886 already filled and 152 posts lying vacant.

faq.question: What are the application dates for Dehradun Homeguard Volunteer 2026?
faq.answer: The application process starts on 01 May 2026 and the last date for submitting duly filled application forms in person or by post to the District Commandant Homeguards, Dehradun, is 22 May 2026 up to 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Who can apply for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only permanent residents of Dehradun district are eligible to apply, and they must possess a valid domicile certificate issued by a competent authority of Dehradun as per the applicable Government Orders.

faq.question: What is the educational qualification required for Dehradun Male Homeguard posts?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualification is 10th pass from any recognized board or institution approved by the Uttarakhand State Government; possessing NCC/NSS/Scout Guide certificates and knowledge of the local language is desirable.

faq.question: What is the age limit for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 40 years of age as on 01 January 2026, with a relaxation of up to 5 years for SC, ST, OBC, Freedom Fighter dependants and other reserved categories, as per Uttarakhand Government norms.

faq.question: Is there any application fee for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No recruitment or application fee has been prescribed in the notification; candidates can submit their applications free of cost, either personally or by post to the designated address.

faq.question: What is the selection process for Dehradun Homeguard Volunteer 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Physical Measurement (qualifying), Physical Efficiency Test (PET) carrying 30 marks, and preparation of a final merit list out of 60 marks (including marks for PET, education, NCC/NSS/Scout, Homeguard family, sports and vehicle driving), followed by a medical examination for medically fit candidates.

faq.question: What are the physical standards and PET details for Dehradun Homeguard posts?
faq.answer: Male candidates must meet the minimum height and chest measurements as per category and area (General/OBC/SC, ST, and mountainous region), including at least 5 cm chest expansion, and must qualify the PET comprising a 2 km run/walk, cricket ball throw and long jump, each evaluated on a 10‑mark scale.

faq.question: How can I apply for Dehradun Homeguard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must download or collect the prescribed application form from the Dehradun Homeguards Facebook page or office notice board, fill it completely, attach self‑attested copies of required documents (educational, domicile, caste, EWS, hill area, character certificate, NOC, etc.) and submit it in person or by post to the District Commandant, Homeguards, Dehradun, Nanda Ki Chowki, Chakrata Road, Dehradun – 248007, on or before 22 May 2026.

basic_details.job_title: MPESB Hospital Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1200 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (MPESB / MPPEB), Bhopal
basic_details.post_name: Hospital Assistant / Aspatal Sahayak (Non-Executive, Fourth Grade)
basic_details.department: Directorate of Public Health and Medical Education, Govt. of Madhya Pradesh, Bhopal
basic_details.short_information: Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board has released the official Hospital Assistant Notification 2026 at esb.mp.gov.in. A total of 1200 vacancies have been announced under the MP ESB Hospital Assistant Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 40 years with a qualification of High School / Class 10th Pass can apply online from 07 May 2026 to 21 May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for Hospital Assistant posts are required to pay a non-refundable examination fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the MP Online Portal (www.mponline.gov.in) via Credit/Debit Card or Internet Banking, or through authorized MP Online Kiosks. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. | Candidates submitting incomplete applications or applications without fee will have their candidature rejected. No fee is payable for Direct Recruitment Backlog vacancy posts. Candidates applying through Kiosk must pay additional portal charges. Fee concession for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD is applicable only to domicile residents of Madhya Pradesh recognized as such by the Madhya Pradesh Government.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be at least 18 years and not more than 40 years of age as on 01-01-2026. The upper age limit is relaxed by 5 years (up to 45 years) for ST, SC, OBC, Women (reserved/unreserved), Government Employees (Govt./Corporation/Board/Autonomous Body/Home Guard), and Physically Handicapped candidates. EWS candidates are not eligible for age relaxation. Under no circumstances will the maximum age limit exceed 45 years for any category (except Sanvida employees and specific categories mentioned in Rule 13).

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the MP Online Portal at www.mponline.gov.in. No offline applications will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 07-05-2026 to 21-05-2026 (by 12:00 midnight). Candidates can apply either through self-login (using Credit/Debit Card or Net Banking for fee payment) or through authorized MP Online Kiosks (additional portal charge applicable).

Candidates must upload scanned copies of their color photograph (white background, front-facing, both ears visible, taken within 3 months), signature, and their own 2-line handwriting. Aadhar Card/Aadhar VID is mandatory to be filled in the application for biometric verification. Candidates must mention body identification marks and the photo ID details to be produced at the exam centre. Live employment office registration is mandatory. After fee payment, keep the application number safely as it will be required for downloading the Admit Card. A correction/amendment window is available from 07-05-2026 to 26-05-2026.
Documents to be Uploaded with Online Application:
Recent color passport-sized photograph (white background, front-facing, both ears visible; taken within 3 months)
Signature of the candidate (clear, full signature; not initials or capital letters)
Own handwriting (2 lines of Hindi/English text as declaration, self-written)
8th / 10th / 12th Marksheet (as proof of Date of Birth)
Caste/Category Certificate issued by competent authority (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates)
Disability Certificate issued by competent authority (for PwD candidates)
Guest Teacher Certificate issued by competent authority (if applicable)
Bonus/Priority Certificates (e.g., NCC Certificate and others, if applicable)
For any application-related queries, contact MP Online Helpdesk at: 0755-6720200
For exam-related complaints: [email protected] or Toll-Free: 18002337899
Apply Online at: https://www.mponline.gov.in
important_instructions: Aadhar registration is mandatory; only UIDAI-verified e-Aadhaar will be accepted. Multi-level biometric verification is compulsory at the examination centre. Candidates must bring their original photo ID proof (Voter ID, PAN Card, Aadhar Card, Driving Licence, or Passport) to the exam centre - e-Aadhaar printout is not accepted as photo ID. All electronic devices including mobile phones, calculators, digital watches, Bluetooth devices, recording devices, and scientific calculators are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall. Latecomers will not be allowed entry after the reporting time; no candidate may leave the examination hall after the exam begins until it is over. Special Primitive Tribal Communities (Baiga, Saharia/Sahariya, and Bhariya) candidates must submit separate offline hard copy applications directly to the concerned department (not to the Board) before the closing date. Women's reservation (35%) is applicable only to domicile residents of Madhya Pradesh; female candidates from other states will be considered under the unreserved/open category. Candidates from other states (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD) will be considered under the unreserved (open) category and will not get reservation, age relaxation, or fee concession benefits. OBC Creamy Layer candidates are not eligible for reservation, age relaxation, or any other OBC category benefits. A candidate can give preferences for multiple posts in one application - all preferred posts' eligibility documents must be uploaded. Candidates not marking preferences for all eligible posts will not be considered for those posts. No re-evaluation/re-checking of answer scripts is available after results are declared. No correspondence in this regard will be entertained. All disputes regarding exam conduct will be subject to the jurisdiction of the Madhya Pradesh High Court. For any suspicious activity or corrupt practices related to the examination, candidates must immediately report to the Board office with complete details.
