how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official website of the Kerala Public Service Commission at www.keralapsc.gov.in via the One Time Registration (OTR) system. Applications submitted in any other manner will be summarily rejected. New users must first complete One Time Registration on the KPSC website before applying. Candidates who have already registered can apply by logging into their profile using their User-ID and Password and clicking on the 'Apply Now' button of the respective post in the Notification Link.

Candidates must apply for the post in only one district and note the name of that district in the relevant column of the online application. Applying for more than one district in response to this notification is strictly prohibited - if found, the applications will be rejected and disciplinary action will be initiated. No application fee is required. Candidates are responsible for the correctness of personal information and secrecy of password. After final submission, no changes or deletions are permitted. Candidates are advised to keep a printout or soft copy of the online application for future reference.

If a Written/OMR/Online Test is conducted, candidates must submit a confirmation for writing the examination through their One Time Registration profile within the stipulated period - failing which the application will be rejected absolutely. Candidates who have AADHAAR card must add it as Identity Proof in their profile. Original documents to prove qualification, age, community etc. have to be produced as and when called for by the Commission.

Age proof - SSLC Book or equivalent certificate
SSLC Certificate/Marksheet and Pass Certificate
Government approved 2-year National/State Trade Certificate in Electrician/Wireman
Community/Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) if applicable
Medical Certificate in prescribed format (from a Medical Officer not below the rank of Assistant Surgeon) to prove physical fitness and vision
Gazette notification in case of difference in caste/community between application and SSLC book (along with Non-Creamy Layer/Community Certificate)
AADHAAR Card (for candidates who possess one)
Printout of online application
Apply Online at: https://www.keralapsc.gov.in
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted online only through the KPSC One Time Registration portal - applications in any other manner will be summarily rejected. Candidates must apply for only one district; applying for more than one district will lead to rejection of all applications and disciplinary action. Candidates appointed in Idukki, Wayanad, or Kasargod districts will not be eligible for working arrangement, deputation, transfer, or mutual transfer for at least ten years, and promotion will also be limited to those districts. No application fee is required for this post. Candidates already in Government service holding the same post in any district are prohibited from applying again for this post. 4% of vacancies are reserved for Differently Abled candidates as per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. Candidates must submit confirmation for the examination through their OTR profile within the stipulated period - failure to do so will result in absolute rejection of the application. Admission Tickets will be available for download only to candidates who have submitted confirmation, 15 days prior to the examination date. Candidates with AADHAAR card must add it as Identity Proof in their profile. Profile corrections made after the last date of application will not be reflected in the application - such corrections take effect only from the date on which they are made. Candidates must ensure that the photograph uploaded in the profile has the candidate's name and date of photograph printed legibly at the bottom portion; photograph is valid for 10 years from the date of taking. Disciplinary action as per Rule 22 of Kerala Public Service Commission Rules of Procedure 1976 shall be initiated against candidates who submit applications with bogus claims of qualifications or other details. Candidates are advised to visit www.keralapsc.gov.in regularly for updates on all stages of the recruitment process.

Download the application form from the official website:bhadrak.odisha.gov.in .
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.

basic_details.job_title: IISER Mohali Non Faculty Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 40 Attendant, Junior Engineer and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Superintending Engineer, Deputy Registrar, Deputy Librarian, Principal Technical Officer (Gr-II)(Lab), Senior Technical Officer (IT), Assistant Executive Engineer (Civil/Electrical), Senior Superintendent, Library Superintendent, Senior Technical Assistant, Physical Education Instructor, Counsellor, Nurse, Junior Engineer (Civil/Electrical), Junior Translator (Rajbhasha), Pharmacist, Junior Technical Assistant, Junior Office Assistant (MS) – Administration, Junior Office Assistant (MS) – Accounts, Attendant
basic_details.department: Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Mohali (Ministry of Education, Govt. of India)
basic_details.total_vacancies: 40 (SC-04, OBC-NCL-04, EWS-02, UR-30; including PwBD and ESM horizontal reservations)
basic_details.short_information: IISER Mohali Non-Faculty Recruitment 2026: Get the latest IISER Mohali recruitment notification 2026 for 40 Non-Faculty (Non-Teaching) posts across Group A, Group B, and Group C categories under the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research Mohali, an Institute of National Importance under the Ministry of Education, Government of India. The last date to apply online is 15th June 2026. Find IISER Mohali vacancies on the official website www.iisermohali.ac.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming IISER Mohali Non-Faculty Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienc

important_dates.age / qualification / experience calcula: 15/06/2026 (last date of receipt of online application)
important_dates.scheme of examination / syllabus: To be announced at a later date on IISER Mohali website
important_dates.interview_date: To be intimated via registered email and IISER Mohali website

application_fee.sc_fee: Exempted from payment of application fee
application_fee.female_fee: Exempted from payment of application fee
application_fee.details: The application fee must be paid online at the time of submission. Candidates applying for more than one post need to pay the application fee separately for each post. In case of cancellation of a post, GST will not be refunded.

age_limit.age_relaxation: Only the highest applicable relaxation shall apply - no cumulative relaxation
age_limit.details: Age limit shall be determined as on the last date for receipt of online applications (15/06/2026). Age relaxation in upper age limit will be as per Government of India norms. Candidates claiming reservation or age relaxation must attach supporting certificates. For Institute's own employees, there is no upper age limit, subject to at least one year of service remaining till superannuation on the last date of application submission. OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within 2 years of the last date of receipt of applications. EWS certificate must be issued for Financial Year 2025-26.

qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education/Sports Sciences with minimum 55% marks or equivalent grade with 3 years of relevant full-time experience after Bachelor's Degree
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (15/06/2026). All qualifications must be from recognized Universities/Institutions only. Mere possession of minimum essential qualifications does not entitle candidates to be called for written test/skill test/interview. Candidates should furnish all qualifications and experience over and above the minimum prescribed along with documentary evidence. The Institute reserves the right to relax experience for persons with a brilliant academic career or specialized skills in exceptional cases. | Post-wise Educational Qualification & Experience

salary_details.pay_scale: As per 7th CPC Pay Matrix - Level 1 to Level 13 depending on post

selection_process: Stage Description
Stage 1 Submission of online application forms before the last date
Stage 2 Evaluation of application forms by the Scrutiny Committee; remarks published on IISER Mohali website
Stage 3 Representation(s) by applicants (if any) w.r.t. Scrutiny Committee remarks - evaluation of representations by Scrutiny Committee
Stage 4 Publication of final remarks of Scrutiny Committee on Institute website; no reconsideration request entertained after this stage
Stage 5 Written Test / Skill Test / Interview - as applicable per post (venue decided by IISER Mohali)
1 Higher educational qualifications than the minimum prescribed
2 Higher experience in relevant field than the minimum prescribed
3 Experience before and/or after acquisition of essential qualifications
4 Experience in relevant posts under Central/State Govt./Central/State funded Educational Institutes/Govt. Universities
5 Rejection of incomplete applications

The selection process consists of multiple stages conducted by the Scrutiny/Selection Committee of IISER Mohali. The minimum qualifying marks for written/interview/skill tests will be as per standards fixed by the Institute at its discretion. The scheme of examination and syllabus for all posts will be announced at a later date on the Institute's website. Mere fulfilment of minimum eligibility criteria does not guarantee a call for test/interview.
Candidates selected to appear for the interview for Group A posts shall be reimbursed travel fare as follows - By Railways: AC II Tier; By Airways: up to 1000 km: Rs. 12
000/- (or actual
whichever is less); above 1500 km: Rs. 24
000/-. Air/Rail fare reimbursement is only on production of authentic tickets booked via authorized travel agents as per DoPT OM dated 29.08.2022.
Recruitment Process Steps
Shortlisting Criteria (may be adopted by Scrutiny Committee)
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only at erp.iisermohali.ac.in. Applications received in hard copy format or any other mode will be summarily rejected. The last date is 15/06/2026 up to 05:00 PM. Candidates are advised to submit well before the closing date to avoid connectivity issues due to heavy load on the website. Once submitted, the application cannot be withdrawn under any circumstances and no editing will be allowed. All correspondence including admit cards will be sent to the registered email ID only - no hard copy letters will be sent.

Candidates serving in Central Government / State Government / Semi-Government / PSUs / Autonomous Bodies must upload the NOC/Forwarding Letter (Annexure-A) and Experience Certificate (Annexure-B) from their current employer before the last date of application, or submit by email to [email protected] before the date of written exam/interview.
Documents to be Produced at Written Test / Skill Test / Interview:
Original documents of all educational qualifications with mark sheets
Experience certificates clearly mentioning date of joining/promotion, Pay Level/Grade Pay, period of employment, and nature of duties
Category certificate - SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD/ESM (if applicable)
NOC / Forwarding letter from current employer (Annexure-A) - for Govt./PSU employees
Self-declaration for OBC-NCL (Annexure-I format)
Any other documents supporting claims made in the application form
Apply Online at: https://erp.iisermohali.ac.in/Online Recruitment Application IISERM.action
For technical support: [email protected] | For other queries: [email protected]
important_instructions: The applicant must be a citizen of India. All posts are regular and permanent (except Lien vacancies). Selected candidates will be covered under the National Pension System (NPS). Candidates must satisfy themselves of the suitability for the position before applying - minimum essential qualifications are compulsory regardless of higher qualifications held. CGPA to percentage conversion will be done as per formula of the degree-awarding institution. OBC-NCL certificate must have been issued within 2 years of the last date of application and a self-declaration (Annexure-I) must also be submitted. EWS certificate must be valid for Financial Year 2025-26 and clearly state the same. If caste/category certificate is in a language other than Hindi or English, a certified translation must be produced at the time of interview/document verification. Candidates must disclose details of any close relatives employed at IISER Mohali, and any penalties/inquiry against them in the application. Canvassing in any form or bringing political or any other influence will be treated as disqualification. Candidates applying for more than one post must pay the application fee separately for each post. No hard copy/postal applications will be entertained - online mode only. Once submitted, the application cannot be edited or withdrawn under any circumstances. All correspondence will be made via registered email only - no separate hard copy letters will be sent. Candidates selected for Group A post interviews are eligible for travel reimbursement (AC II Tier by rail; air fare as per slab with authorized travel agent tickets only). Any corrigendum/addendum shall be published only on the Institute website. Candidates are advised to visit www.iisermohali.ac.in regularly. Any legal dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to jurisdiction of the Hon'ble Punjab and Haryana High Court, Chandigarh only. In case of ambiguity between Hindi and English versions of the advertisement, the English version shall prevail. All decisions of the Competent Authority, IISER Mohali regarding eligibility, acceptance/rejection, selection, and related matters are final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. What is IISER Mohali Non-Faculty Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: IISER Mohali Non‑Faculty Recruitment 2026 is a direct recruitment drive for 40 regular Non‑Teaching posts in Group A, Group B, and Group C at the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research Mohali, an Institute of National Importance under the Ministry of Education, Government of India.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under IISER Mohali Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: IISER Mohali has released a total of 40 vacancies for various Non‑Teaching posts such as Superintending Engineer, Deputy Registrar, Senior Technical Officer (IT), Junior Technical Assistant, Junior Office Assistant, Attendant and others across Group A, B and C categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply online for IISER Mohali Non-Faculty posts?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the IISER Mohali Non‑Faculty online application form is 15 June 2026 up to 05:00 PM, and candidates are advised to apply well before the closing time to avoid last‑minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q4. What are the eligibility qualifications for IISER Mohali Non-Teaching posts?
faq.answer: The educational qualification for IISER Mohali Non‑Teaching posts ranges from Matriculation (10th pass) and 3‑year Diploma to Bachelor’s degree, Master’s degree, B.E./B.Tech, M.Sc. or BS‑MS, along with post‑wise required experience, and all degrees must be from recognized Universities/Institutions as specified in the official notification.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for IISER Mohali Non-Faculty Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit varies post‑wise from 32 years to 56 years as on 15 June 2026, with applicable relaxations for SC, ST, OBC‑NCL, EWS, PwBD, Ex‑Servicemen and IISER Mohali employees as per Government of India norms mentioned in the advertisement.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary or pay scale for IISER Mohali Non-Teaching posts?
faq.answer: The IISER Mohali Non‑Teaching posts carry pay levels from Level 1 to Level 13 in the 7th CPC Pay Matrix depending on the post, offering attractive salary along with admissible allowances and coverage under the National Pension System (NPS) as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for IISER Mohali Non-Faculty Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 1,298 (Rs. 1,100 + 18% GST) for UR and OBC‑NCL candidates, Rs. 708 (Rs. 600 + 18% GST) for EWS candidates, while SC, ST, PwBD, Ex‑Servicemen and female candidates are fully exempted from paying the application fee, and the fee must be paid separately for each post applied.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for IISER Mohali Non-Teaching posts?
faq.answer: The selection process for IISER Mohali Non‑Teaching posts includes shortlisting by the Scrutiny Committee followed by Written Test, Skill Test and/or Interview, as applicable for each post, and the scheme of examination and syllabus will be notified later on the Institute’s official website.

application_fee.details: For Process Server Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 150/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Peon Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 200/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 100/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Copyist Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category-1 Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Stenographer Grade-III Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 150/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online

Upload required scanned documents and photograph.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years, and the maximum age varies by category as on the last date of application (31 May 2026). Additionally, there is a one-time age relaxation of 5 years (as per Government Order and High Court directions, valid up to 31.12.2027). faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for different posts in Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee varies by post and category. For Stenographer Grade-III: General candidates - Rs. 300/-, Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B - Rs. 150/-, SC/ST/Category-1/PH - Nil. faq.question: Q6. What is the pay scale for Mysuru District Court posts?
faq.answer: The pay scales for different posts are as follows: Stenographer Grade-III - Rs. 44,425 – 83,700/-, Process Server - Rs. 31,775 – 61,300/-, Copyist - Rs. 34,100 – 67,600/-, and Peon - Rs. 27,000 – 46,675/-. faq.answer: The selection process varies by post as per Karnataka Subordinate Courts (Recruitment) Rules. For Process Server and Peon, selection is merit-based on SSLC marks plus Interview (10 marks weightage). Selected candidates will be subject to verification of documents and medical fitness before final appointment.

faq.answer: Yes, knowledge of Kannada language is essential for all posts under Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026. For skill-based posts like Stenographer Grade-III, candidates must possess Senior Grade Typewriting & Shorthand in both Kannada and English. For Copyist, Junior Grade Typewriting in Kannada and English is required. Since the posts are in Karnataka state courts, proficiency in Kannada is desirable and may be tested during the selection process.

basic_details.job_title: Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 271 Forester, Forest Guard Posts
basic_details.short_information: Tripura Forest Department has released the official Forest Guard & Forester Notification 2026 on 28th April 2026 at forest.tripura.gov.in. A total of 271 vacancies have been announced under the Tripura Forest Guard & Forester Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 40 years with a qualification of Madhyamik (Class 10) for Forest Guard and Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) for Forester can apply online from 1st May 2026 to 31st May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

age_limit.details: Age Limit: 18-40 years as on 01 May 2026. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is admissible for SC/ST candidates and Government employees.

qualification.details: Before applying, candidates must ensure they fulfill the eligibility criteria. Applicants must possess Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC). | Educational Qualification: | Forest Guard: Madhyamik (Class 10) passed or equivalent. Forester: Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) passed or equivalent. | Desirable: Knowledge in Bengali or Kok-borok.

salary_details.pay_scale: Forest Guard: Level-6 (Rs. 5700-24000 + GP 2100) Forester: Level-7 (Rs. 5700-24000 + GP 2200)

selection_process: Selection will be done through:
Written Examination (85% marks) Interview (15% marks)
Only candidates securing cut-off marks in the written test will be called for interview. Final selection is subject to medical fitness.
Written Test Syllabus:
For Forest Guard: Mathematics (20)
English (30)
General Knowledge (120)
Interview (30). Bengali/Kokborok is qualifying in nature (Min 40%).
For Forester: Mathematics (20)
English (20)
General Knowledge (100)
Science (30)

physical_eligibility.details: Candidates should possess good physique and sound health. Persons with disabilities are not eligible. | Minimum Physical Standards: | Physical Test (Qualifying in nature): | Distance to be covered on foot in 4 hours: | Male: 25 Km Female: 16 Km | Only candidates who qualify in the Physical Test will proceed further.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted in online mode only through the official portal. The step-by-step process is as follows:
Visit the official recruitment portal: https://forestrecruitment.tripura.gov.in
Register and fill the online application form carefully.
Upload required documents and submit the form.
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.
Candidates applying for both Forest Guard and Forester posts need not appear twice for Physical Test.
important_instructions: Only online applications will be accepted. No physical applications are entertained. Candidates must have Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC). Women candidates must submit a self-declaration that they are not pregnant on the date of Physical Test. No TA/DA will be paid for attending any stage of recruitment. The vacancies are subject to increase or decrease. Candidates who have applied for both posts are not required to appear for physical test twice. For detailed syllabus and selection rules, refer to the official notification and Annexures.

faq.question: Q1. hat is the last date to apply for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026 is 31 May 2026 (up to 5:30 PM). The online application portal will open on 01 May 2026, giving candidates a full month to complete their registration through the official recruitment portal forestrecruitment.tripura.gov.in. All applications must be submitted online only - no physical applications will be accepted.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: A total of 271 vacancies have been announced under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Out of these, 167 posts are for Forest Guard (Group-C, Non-Gazetted) and 104 posts are for Forester (Group-C, Non-Gazetted). The category-wise distribution for Forest Guard includes UR-80 (including Ex-SM-02), SC-21, and ST-66 (including Ex-SM-01), while Forester posts include UR-39 (including Ex-SM-01), SC-21, and ST-44 (including Ex-SM-01). Additionally, 33% horizontal reservation for women is provided across both posts.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: For Forest Guard posts, candidates must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10) or equivalent examination. For Forester posts, candidates must have passed Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) or equivalent examination. Knowledge in Bengali or Kok-borok is desirable for both positions. Candidates must possess the prescribed educational qualification on or before the closing date of application.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 to 40 years of age as on 01 May 2026. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is admissible for SC/ST candidates and Government employees. The age calculation date is 01 May 2026 for all applicants. Candidates should verify their age eligibility carefully before applying, as this is a mandatory criterion.

faq.question: Q5. Is Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) mandatory for application?
faq.answer: Yes, possession of a valid Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) is absolutely mandatory for all applicants. Candidates without a valid PRTC are not eligible to apply for these posts under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Only bonafide residents of Tripura State with valid PRTC documentation can submit applications for Forest Guard and Forester positions.

faq.question: Q6. What are the physical standards required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: The minimum physical standards vary by category and gender. For Male (UR & SC): minimum height 163 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Male (ST): minimum height 152 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (UR & SC): minimum height 150 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (ST): minimum height 145 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion. Candidates should possess good physique and sound health, and persons with disabilities are not eligible.

faq.question: Q7. What is the Physical Efficiency Test for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Physical Efficiency Test (PET) is qualifying in nature and requires candidates to cover a specified distance on foot within 4 hours. Male candidates must cover 25 km within 4 hours, while female candidates must cover 16 km within 4 hours. Only candidates who qualify in the Physical Test will proceed to the next stage of selection. Women candidates must submit a self-declaration that they are not pregnant on the date of the Physical Test.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: Selection will be conducted through a Written Examination (carrying 85% marks weightage) followed by an Interview (carrying 15% marks weightage). Only candidates securing cut-off marks in the written test will be called for the interview. Final selection is subject to medical fitness. For Forest Guard, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (30 marks), General Knowledge (120 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok being qualifying in nature (minimum 40% required), plus Interview (30 marks). For Forester, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (20 marks), General Knowledge (100 marks), Science (30 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok qualifying (minimum 40%), plus Interview (30 marks).

basic_details.job_title: Tribal Development Department Maharashtra Watchman Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 416 Posts
basic_details.department: Commissioner, Tribal Development Department, Maharashtra State, Nashik
basic_details.short_information: The latest Tribal Development Department (TDC) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Chowkidar (Watchman) posts! Published on 27th April 2026 at tribal.maharashtra.gov.in, this new recruitment drive offers 416 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 18 to 40 years age group and possessing a Secondary School Certificate (SSC / 10th Pass) can apply online from 27th April 2026 to 18th May 2026. Find all details about the TDC Chowkidar new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

important_dates.online examination date: To be announced separately on https://tribal.maharashtra.gov.in

application_fee.payment_mode: Online only - Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, UPI, Mobile Wallet

age_limit.details: The age of candidates will be calculated on the closing date for online applications (18/05/2026). The general upper age limit and relaxations are governed by Government Resolution, General Administration Department No. SRV-2015/P.No.404/Work.12, dated 25 April 2016. | Age relaxation is available to candidates belonging to government-recognized backward categories, Divyang (PH), Ex-Servicemen, Sportspersons, and Part-time employees as per respective government orders. | Creamy Layer candidates in VJ-A, NT-B, NT-C, NT-D, Vimapra, Imav (SBC), SEBC, and EWS categories are not eligible for age relaxation. Candidates should verify their age eligibility from the official notification before applying.

qualification.education_qualification: SSC (Secondary School Certificate / 10th Pass)
qualification.domicile: Candidate must be a resident of Maharashtra. Candidates from 865 Marathi-speaking villages in the Maharashtra-Karnataka border area may also apply, subject to submission of valid domicile certificate issued by competent authority.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions specified below on the date of advertisement (27/04/2026) to apply for the post of Chowkidar. The minimum educational qualification is passing the Secondary School Certificate (SSC / 10th Standard) examination. | Merely possessing the minimum qualification does not entitle a candidate to be recommended - the final selection is strictly on the basis of merit in the computer-based examination. All service entry rules as per Maharashtra government norms and as amended from time to time will apply throughout the recruitment process. Candidates are advised to apply only after verifying their eligibility in full.

vacancy_details.Description: Persons displaced due to government projects
Persons with disabilities - Low Vision / Deaf / Locomotor etc. (min. 40% disability)
Orphan candidates as per GR dated 23 August 2021

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Orphan candidates as per GR dated 23 August 2021

selection_process: Post Name Marathi English General Knowledge Intellectual/Aptitude Test Total Marks Duration
Chowkidar (Watchman) 50 50 50 50 200 120 Minutes
Point Details
Exam Mode Computer-Based Online Examination (CBT)
Question Type Objective Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) - 2 marks per question
Medium of Examination Marathi only
Exam Centers District headquarters across Maharashtra (subject to change)
Minimum Qualifying Marks 45% of total marks
Banned Items Mobile phone, calculator, iPad, or any electronic/communication device strictly banned at exam center
Scribe (Lekhanik) Facility Available for visually impaired and candidates with permanent writing disability (must be declared in application form); 20 extra minutes per hour provided

Extra Time for PH Candidates Locomotor/Cerebral Palsy (min. 40% disability): 20 extra minutes per hour; Visually impaired (more than 40% disability): 20 extra minutes per hour + magnified font option available
Multiple Sessions If conducted in multiple sessions, scores will be normalized across sessions to ensure parity
Admit Card Available for download from https://tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in - will not be sent by post or any other means
Result Final result published on https://tribal.maharashtra.gov.in

Selection for the post of Chowkidar (Watchman) will be done entirely on the basis of a Computer-Based Online Examination (CBT) in Marathi medium. There is no physical or trade test for this post. The examination will be of 200 marks
comprising 100 objective multiple-choice questions of 2 marks each
to be completed in 120 minutes (2 hours).

The exam will be held at district headquarters across Maharashtra. Candidates must score a minimum of 45% marks to be eligible for inclusion in the merit list. Final appointment will be made on the basis of the merit list prepared from the online examination scores. In case of equal marks between two or more candidates
the tie-breaking priority order will be as per Government Resolution
GAD No. Pranimbh-1222/P.No.54/Ka.13-A
dated 4 May 2022.
Examination Pattern
Key Rules for the Online Examination:
how_to_apply: Applications will be accepted through online mode only at https://tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in. No offline or postal applications will be entertained. Candidates do not need to upload supporting certificates at the time of applying - however, all information entered in the online application must be accurate and complete.

The online application portal is open from 27/04/2026 to 18/05/2026 (till 11:55 PM). New candidates must first register, after which a temporary Registration Number and Password will be generated and sent to their registered mobile number/email.
Step-by-Step Online Application Process:
Visit https://tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in and click on "APPLY ONLINE".

Click "Click here for New Registration" - enter name, contact details, and email ID. A temporary Registration Number and Password will be displayed on screen and sent via SMS/email. Note these carefully.
Log in using the Registration Number and Password. Fill in all required details including personal information, address, category, Aadhaar number, and educational qualifications.
Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save progress. Verify all details carefully before final submission.

Upload the following scanned documents as per specifications: passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm x 3.5 cm), signature in black ink on white paper, left thumb impression in black/blue ink on white paper, handwritten declaration in English on white paper in black ink (in capital letters is NOT acceptable).
Select the post and ITDP project office for which you are applying.
Click "Proceed to Payment" and pay the examination fee online.
After successful payment, an E-Receipt will be generated - download and save it.
Take a printout of the completed application form and E-Receipt. Retain until the entire recruitment process is complete.
Documents to be Uploaded (Scanned, JPG/JPEG format):
Apply Online at: https://tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in/Candidate/EntryForm.aspx
Official Website: https://tribal.maharashtra.gov.in
important_instructions: Only online applications are accepted - no hard copy, postal, or offline applications will be entertained. Certificates need not be uploaded at the time of online application - however, all details entered must be truthful and accurate. Online application information cannot be modified after fee payment is completed. Candidates must have a valid personal email ID and active mobile number for the entire duration of the recruitment process. Admit cards will be available for self-download only from the official website - no other mode of delivery. Candidates must arrive at the examination center at least 1 hour before the scheduled start time to complete verification formalities. Mobile phones, calculators, iPads, and all electronic/communication devices are strictly banned inside the examination premises - violation will lead to disqualification and debarment from future exams. Candidates who wish to use a scribe (lekhanik) must declare this in the online application form - requests made later will not be entertained. The scribe must not be a candidate in the same examination; one scribe cannot serve more than one candidate. If false, tampered, or incomplete information is submitted at any stage, the candidature will be cancelled, and the candidate may face criminal prosecution. Candidates who share answers with others or adopt unfair means will have their candidature cancelled and results withheld. Reservation benefits (social, horizontal, or concession) are available only to candidates with valid certificates issued by competent authorities before the closing date of application. For Women reservation: a valid Maharashtra domicile certificate must be submitted. Non-Creamy Layer requirement for open category women's posts has been waived as per Women & Child Development Department GR dated 04 May 2023. EWS certificate must be produced at the time of document verification as per prescribed format issued by competent authority. Orphan candidates must submit a certificate issued by the Women & Child Development Department authority only - certificates from other agencies will not be accepted. Ex-Servicemen must clearly claim their status in the application; must have their name registered with the District Sainik Board and present original certificates at verification. The department reserves the right to cancel/modify/alter recruitment conditions - announcements will be published on the official website only. All examination-related disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of the competent court as per applicable law.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for TDC Chowkidar Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Tribal Development Department Chowkidar Recruitment 2026 is 18 May 2026 (till 11:55 PM). The online application portal opened on 27 April 2026 from 3:00 PM onwards, giving candidates approximately 21 days to complete their registration through the official website tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in. The examination fee payment must also be completed by 18 May 2026 till 11:55 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced for TDC Watchman Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 416 vacancies have been announced for the post of Chowkidar (Watchman), Group-D under Advertisement No. आस्था-पद भरती-2025/प्र.क्र.65/का.2(8)/नाशिक dated 27 April 2026. These vacancies are distributed across 29 Integrated Tribal Development Projects (ITDPs) in Government Ashramshala (residential schools) and Vastigruh (hostels) throughout Maharashtra State. The vacancies include category-wise and horizontal reservation for SC, ST, VJ-A, NT-B, NT-C, NT-D, SBC, EWS, Women, Sports Persons, Ex-Servicemen, Divyang (PH), and Orphan candidates.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for TDC Chowkidar posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC / 10th Standard) examination from any recognized board. The prescribed educational qualification must be acquired on or before the date of advertisement (27 April 2026). Merely possessing the minimum qualification does not automatically entitle candidates to selection, as final appointment is strictly based on merit in the computer-based online examination.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Tribal Development Chowkidar Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The general age limit for Open/Unreserved category candidates is 18 to 40 years, calculated as on the closing date of application (18 May 2026). Age relaxations are provided as per Maharashtra Government rules: Divyang (PH) candidates can apply up to 45 years maximum, Government-recognized Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) receive age relaxation as per state norms, Sportspersons (including reserved category) can apply up to 43 years maximum, and Ex-Servicemen receive relaxation equal to their military service period plus 3 years. However, Creamy Layer candidates in VJ-A, NT-B, NT-C, NT-D, Vimapra, Imav (SBC), SEBC, and EWS categories are not eligible for age relaxation.

faq.question: Q5. Is Permanent Resident Certificate of Maharashtra mandatory for application?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must be residents of Maharashtra to apply for this recruitment. Additionally, candidates from 865 Marathi-speaking villages in the Maharashtra-Karnataka border area may also apply, subject to submission of a valid domicile certificate issued by the competent authority. Reservation benefits are available only to candidates who are general residents of Maharashtra as defined under Section 20 of the Representation of the People Act, 1950.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for TDC Chowkidar Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee for all general category candidates is Rs. 1,000/-, while Backward Classes/OBC/Orphans/Disabled candidates need to pay Rs. 900/-. Ex-Servicemen and Disabled Ex-Servicemen candidates are exempted from paying any examination fee. The fee must be paid online through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, UPI, or Mobile Wallet, and it is non-refundable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for TDC Watchman posts?
faq.answer: Selection for the Chowkidar (Watchman) post will be done entirely on the basis of a Computer-Based Online Examination (CBT) in Marathi medium. There is no physical test or trade test for this recruitment. The examination comprises 100 objective multiple-choice questions of 2 marks each (total 200 marks) to be completed in 120 minutes (2 hours). The exam pattern includes four sections: Marathi (50 marks), English (50 marks), General Knowledge (50 marks), and Intellectual/Aptitude Test (50 marks). Candidates must score a minimum of 45% marks to qualify for inclusion in the merit list.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the Computer-Based Examination be conducted?
faq.answer: The Computer-Based Online Examination will be held at district headquarters across Maharashtra. However, the examination centers are subject to change, and the final exam date and venue details will be announced separately on the official website tribal.maharashtra.gov.in. Candidates must download their admit cards from tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in before the examination, as admit cards will not be sent by post or any other means.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for TDC Chowkidar Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Tribal Development Department Chowkidar Recruitment 2026 is 18 May 2026 (till 11:55 PM). The online application portal opened on 27 April 2026 from 3:00 PM onwards, giving candidates approximately 21 days to complete their registration through the official website tdcwatchman2026.cbtexam.in. The examination fee payment must also be completed by 18 May 2026 till 11:55 PM.

faq.question: How many vacancies are announced for TDC Watchman Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 416 vacancies have been announced for the post of Chowkidar (Watchman), Group-D under Advertisement No. आस्था-पद भरती-2025/प्र.क्र.65/का.2(8)/नाशिक dated 27 April 2026. These vacancies are distributed across 29 Integrated Tribal Development Projects (ITDPs) in Government Ashramshala (residential schools) and Vastigruh (hostels) throughout Maharashtra State. The vacancies include category-wise and horizontal reservation for SC, ST, VJ-A, NT-B, NT-C, NT-D, SBC, EWS, Women, Sports Persons, Ex-Servicemen, Divyang (PH), and Orphan candidates.

faq.question: What is the educational qualification required for TDC Chowkidar posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC / 10th Standard) examination from any recognized board. The prescribed educational qualification must be acquired on or before the date of advertisement (27 April 2026). Merely possessing the minimum qualification does not automatically entitle candidates to selection, as final appointment is strictly based on merit in the computer-based online examination.

faq.question: What is the age limit for Tribal Development Chowkidar Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The general age limit for Open/Unreserved category candidates is 18 to 40 years, calculated as on the closing date of application (18 May 2026). Age relaxations are provided as per Maharashtra Government rules: Divyang (PH) candidates can apply up to 45 years maximum, Government-recognized Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) receive age relaxation as per state norms, Sportspersons (including reserved category) can apply up to 43 years maximum, and Ex-Servicemen receive relaxation equal to their military service period plus 3 years. However, Creamy Layer candidates in VJ-A, NT-B, NT-C, NT-D, Vimapra, Imav (SBC), SEBC, and EWS categories are not eligible for age relaxation.

faq.question: Is Permanent Resident Certificate of Maharashtra mandatory for application?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must be residents of Maharashtra to apply for this recruitment. Additionally, candidates from 865 Marathi-speaking villages in the Maharashtra-Karnataka border area may also apply, subject to submission of a valid domicile certificate issued by the competent authority. Reservation benefits are available only to candidates who are general residents of Maharashtra as defined under Section 20 of the Representation of the People Act, 1950.

basic_details.job_title: Karnataka High Court Group D Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 93 Peon, Watchman and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Group-D posts (Peons, Watchman, Sweepers, Peons (House Keeping), Lift Attender, Assistant Binder, Tailor, Dhobi)
basic_details.job_location: High Court of Karnataka, Bengaluru (Residual Parent Cadre)
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Shortlisting based on qualifying examination marks and Interview
basic_details.short_information: The High Court of Karnataka (HCK) has officially announced the Group-D Recruitment 2026 with 93 openings. The detailed notification was published on 22 April 2026 at High Court of Karnataka judiciary.karnataka.gov.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 40 years (up to 45 years for reserved categories as per rules) and hold SSLC / 10th pass qualification (with relevant experience for certain posts) can now submit their online applications up to 21 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the following application fees through the State Bank of India (SBI) online payment gateway as per instructions provided in the online application portal. | General Merit / Category-II(A) / II(B) / III(A) / III(B): ₹200/- SC / ST / Category-I / PwBD: ₹100/- | The fee once paid will not be refunded or adjusted under any circumstances. Applications submitted without payment of prescribed fee (where applicable) will be rejected.

qualification.education_qualification: SSLC / 10th Standard Pass or equivalent (with relevant experience for some posts)
qualification.details: Candidates must satisfy the required educational qualification and age criteria as on the last date prescribed for submission of online applications. | Peons, Watchman, Sweepers, Peons (House Keeping): SSLC / 10th Standard Pass or equivalent. Lift Attender: SSLC / 10th Standard Pass and experience in Lift Mechanism. Assistant Binder: SSLC / 10th Standard Pass with a minimum of 2 years experience in binding work. Tailor: SSLC / 10th Standard Pass with a minimum of 3 years experience in tailoring work. Dhobi: SSLC / 10th Standard Pass with relevant experience as Dhobi. | Age shall be calculated as on the cut-off date mentioned in the notification. | Minimum Age (all categories): 18 years Maximum Age - General Merit (GM): 40 years Maximum Age - Category-II(A)/II(B)/III(A)/III(B): 43 years Maximum Age - SC/ST/Category-I: 45 years | Age relaxation applies as per Government orders including one-time relaxation up to 5 years, wherever applicable, subject to conditions laid down in the notification.

salary_details.details: The selected candidates will be paid salary in the following pay scales along with admissible allowances as per the High Court of Karnataka (Officers & Officials) Revised Pay Rules and Government norms. | Level-2 posts (Peon, Watchman, Sweeper, Peons (House Keeping), Assistant Binder, Dhobi): ₹19,900 - ₹63,200. Level-3 posts (Lift Attender, Tailor): ₹21,700 - ₹69,100. | In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates will be eligible for allowances as may be applicable from time to time. The candidates appointed will be on probation for a period of 2 years or as extended in accordance with the rules.

selection_process: The selection process for High Court of Karnataka Group-D posts will be carried out as per the High Court of Karnataka Service (Conditions of Service and Recruitment) Rules and relevant Government orders. The main stages are as follows.

Shortlisting of candidates based on the percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying examination (SSLC/10th Standard or equivalent). Interview carrying a maximum of 10 marks. Final selection based on merit
taking into account reservation policies and category-wise vacancies. Verification of original documents and certificates before appointment.
Candidates will be shortlisted in the order of merit as per category
and the number of candidates called for interview is expected to be multiple times the number of vacancies as per rules. In case of tie in interview marks
older candidates may be placed higher in merit as per the conditions in the rules/notification.
how_to_apply: Eligible and interested candidates can apply online for the High Court of Karnataka Group-D posts through the official website only. No other mode of application will be accepted.
Visit the official website of High Court of Karnataka at https://judiciary.karnataka.gov.in.
Go to the “Recruitment” section and find the notification for Group-D (Notification No. HCRB 8/2025 - GROUP-D - RPC).
Read the notification and instructions carefully to ensure eligibility for the post you wish to apply for.
Register yourself on the online recruitment portal, if you are a new user, or log in using existing credentials.
Fill the online application form with correct personal, educational and category details as required.

Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, SSLC/10th marks card, caste/category certificate, experience certificates (where required) and other documents in the prescribed format and size.
Pay the applicable application fee online through SBI payment gateway as per the instructions on the portal.
Verify all details entered in the application form and submit the form online.
Download and take a printout of the submitted application form for future reference.
Candidates intending to apply for more than one post should submit separate applications for each post as per the notification.
important_instructions: Read the complete notification carefully before applying and ensure that you fulfil all eligibility conditions as on the last date for submission of online applications. Only online applications submitted through the official website will be considered. Applications submitted by post, email, or any other mode will be rejected. Correct and valid details must be provided in the application form. Any discrepancy or suppression of facts may lead to rejection of candidature at any stage. Clear scanned copies of all required documents must be uploaded in the prescribed format. Submission of documents at a later stage, except when called for verification, may not be permitted. Candidates must provide an active mobile number and email ID, which shall remain valid for the entire recruitment process for receiving communications. Original certificates and documents must be produced at the time of interview and document verification; failure to do so may result in cancellation of candidature. Any false information or misrepresentation will lead to cancellation of candidature and may also attract legal action as per law. The decision of the High Court of Karnataka in all matters related to recruitment, eligibility, selection, and appointment will be final and binding on the candidates. Candidates are advised to visit the official website regularly for updates, corrigendum, and announcements related to this recruitment. This recruitment is subject to the outcome of pending writ petitions and other legal proceedings, if any, as mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies have been announced for High Court of Karnataka Group-D Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The High Court of Karnataka has announced a total of 93 vacancies under Notification No. HCRB 8/2025 (GROUP-D - RPC) dated 22 April 2026 for Residual Parent Cadre. These include 84 regular posts plus 1 backlog post for Peons/Watchman/Sweepers/Peons (House Keeping), 5 posts for Lift Attender, 1 post each for Assistant Binder, Tailor, and Dhobi. Additionally, there is a separate notification (HCRB 09/2025) for Kalyana Karnataka Region with 8 more vacancies, bringing the total to 101 posts across both notifications.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for Karnataka High Court Group-D posts?
faq.answer: For most Group-D posts including Peons, Watchman, Sweepers, and Peons (House Keeping), candidates must have passed SSLC/10th Standard or equivalent from a recognized board. For Lift Attender, SSLC/10th pass with experience in Lift Mechanism is required. For Assistant Binder, SSLC/10th pass with minimum 2 years experience in binding work is mandatory, while Tailor requires SSLC/10th pass with minimum 3 years experience in tailoring work. For Dhobi, SSLC/10th pass with relevant experience as Dhobi is required.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for High Court of Karnataka Group-D Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years for all categories. The maximum age varies by category: General Merit candidates can apply up to 40 years, Category-II(A)/II(B)/III(A)/III(B) candidates up to 43 years, and SC/ST/Category-I candidates up to 45 years. Age relaxation applies as per Government orders including one-time relaxation up to 5 years wherever applicable, subject to conditions laid down in the notification. Age is calculated as on the last date prescribed for submission of online applications (21-05-2026).

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for Karnataka High Court Group-D Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹200/- for General Merit, Category-II(A), II(B), III(A), and III(B) candidates. For SC/ST/Category-I and PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) candidates, the fee is reduced to ₹100/-. The fee must be paid through the State Bank of India (SBI) online payment gateway as per instructions provided in the online application portal. The fee once paid will not be refunded or adjusted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for Karnataka High Court Group-D posts?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of two main stages: Stage 1 - Shortlisting of candidates based on the percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying examination (SSLC/10th Standard or equivalent), and Stage 2 - Interview carrying a maximum of 10 marks. Final selection is based on merit, taking into account reservation policies and category-wise vacancies. The number of candidates called for interview is expected to be multiple times the number of vacancies as per rules. Verification of original documents and certificates is conducted before appointment.

faq.question: Q6. What is the pay scale for Karnataka High Court Group-D posts?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive salary in Level-2 pay scale (₹19,900 – ₹63,200) for posts like Peon, Watchman, Sweeper, Peons (House Keeping), Assistant Binder, and Dhobi. For Level-3 posts including Lift Attender and Tailor, the pay scale is ₹21,700 – ₹69,100. In addition to basic pay, selected candidates will be eligible for allowances as may be applicable from time to time as per the High Court of Karnataka (Officers & Officials) Revised Pay Rules and Government norms.

faq.question: Q7. How to apply for High Court of Karnataka Group-D Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is completely online. Candidates must visit the official website (judiciary.karnataka.gov.in), go to the "Recruitment" section, find the notification for Group-D (Notification No. HCRB 8/2025), read the notification carefully, register on the online recruitment portal, fill the online application form with correct details, upload scanned copies of required documents (photograph, signature, SSLC marks card, caste certificate, experience certificates, etc.) in prescribed format, pay the application fee through SBI payment gateway, verify all details, and submit the form online. A printout of the submitted application should be saved for future reference.

faq.question: Q8. What is the last date to apply for Karnataka High Court Group-D Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application is 21-05-2026. The notification was released on 22-04-2026, giving candidates approximately one month to complete the application process. Only online applications submitted through the official website (judiciary.karnataka.gov.in) before the deadline will be considered. Applications submitted by post, email, or any other mode will be rejected.

basic_details.job_title: TIFR Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 11 Engineer, Tradesman and More Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Engineer (D), Jr. Engineer (B), Tradesman (B), Project Scientific Officer (C), Library Trainee, Temporary Work Assistant
basic_details.short_information: Tata Institute of Fundamental Research has released the official Engineer, Tradesman & Various Posts Recruitment Notification 2026 (Advertisement No. 2026/5) in April 2026 at its official website. A total of 9 vacancies have been announced under the TIFR Recruitment 2026 for various technical, scientific, and support positions. Eligible candidates aged up to 28/35 years with qualifications such as SSC, ITI, Diploma, Graduation, B.Tech/M.Tech can apply online from April 2026 to 16 May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

important_dates.recruitment process: Written Test / Trade Test / Skill Test / Personal Interview (as applicable)

application_fee.details: Application fee details will be available on the official online application portal (if applicable). Pay online as per instructions on TIFR recruitment portal.

qualification.details: Nationality: Indian Citizens (as per Government of India rules). | Essential Educational Qualification & Experience (varies by post – refer detailed notification): | Engineer (D) [Mechanical]: M.E./M.Tech. or Ph.D. in Mechanical/Industrial/Production Engineering + minimum 2 years experience. Jr. Engineer (B) [Mechanical]: Diploma in Mechanical Engineering + 1-2 years experience in CNC machines. Tradesman (B) (Carpenter/Fitter/Machinist-Miller): NTC/NAC in relevant trade + 1-2 years experience. Project Scientific Officer (C): B.E./B.Tech. in Electronics/Electronics & Telecommunication + 1 year experience. Library Trainee: Graduate + B.Lib. (Science preferable). Temporary Work Assistant: SSC + minimum 1 year experience (basic science knowledge for one post). | Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026): Maximum 28 years (35 years for Engineer (D)). Age relaxation as per rules.

salary_details.details: Additional Benefits: As per TIFR rules (NPS applicable for regular posts).

selection_process: Mode of Recruitment varies by post: Personal Interview (for Engineer (D)

Jr. Engineer (B)) Written Test + Trade Test (for Tradesman posts) Written Test + Skill Test (for Library Trainee & Temporary Work Assistant) Written Test + Personal Interview OR Personal Interview only (for Project Scientific Officer) Document Verification and Medical Examination as per Institute rules. Final selection based on performance in test/interview.
how_to_apply: Apply online only through the official TIFR recruitment portal.
Fill details, upload required documents, and submit the application.
Applications from Government/Semi-Government/PSU/Autonomous bodies require NOC at the time of selection process.
Last date: 16 May 2026.
Incomplete applications shall not be considered.
Click Here to Apply Online for TIFR Advertisement 2026/5
important_instructions: Read the detailed advertisement carefully before applying. Ensure eligibility (qualification, experience & age as on 01.01.2026). Applications accepted ONLY ONLINE. Selected candidates liable to be transferred to other Centres/Field Stations of the Institute. Posts at Sr. No. 6 to 9 are purely temporary. Canvassing in any form shall disqualify the candidate. basic_details.job_title: UPSSSC Forest Guard Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 708 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Van Rakshak (Forest Guard) & Vanya Jeev Rakshak (Wildlife Guard)
basic_details.short_information: Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission has released the official Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard Notification 2026 on 23 April 2026 at upsssc.gov.in. A total of 708 vacancies have been announced under the UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 40 years with a qualification of Intermediate (10+2) can apply online from 30 June 2026 to 20 July 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

application_fee.details: For UPSSSC Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026, the application fee (as a direct charge) is nil for all categories. However, an online process fee of ₹25 is payable by all candidates. The fee can be paid online through Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or via SBI e-Challan as per the instructions given on the website. | The application form will be automatically submitted only after successful payment and reconciliation of the fee. Candidates are advised not to make any payment to any unauthorised individual or agency claiming to collect fees on behalf of UPSSSC.

age_limit.details: For UPSSSC Forest Guard Recruitment 2026, the candidate must have attained the age of 18 years and must not have crossed the age of 40 years as on the first day of July of the calendar year in which direct recruitment vacancies are advertised. Age will be determined on the basis of the date of birth recorded in the High School (10th) or equivalent certificate. Age relaxation is admissible to candidates of reserved categories as per the prevailing rules of the Government of Uttar Pradesh. The category-wise age limit is provided in the table below.

qualification.education_qualification: Intermediate (10+2) from Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad, UP or equivalent recognised board
qualification.details: Before applying for UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026, candidates must ensure that they fulfil all the prescribed eligibility conditions set by UPSSSC. The eligibility criteria include nationality, PET-2025 qualification, and educational qualification along with additional preferential qualifications. Candidates who did not appear in PET-2025 or have not been issued a Score Card by the Commission are not eligible to apply for this Main Examination. Applications found ineligible at any stage of the recruitment process will be rejected without any notice. | Nationality: The candidate must be a citizen of India. PET-2025 Qualification: The candidate must have appeared in the Preliminary Eligibility Test-2025 (PET-2025) and must have been issued a Score Card by UPSSSC. | Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard – Educational Qualification

selection_process: Stage Details

1. Shortlisting for Main Exam Based on PET-2025 Normalised Score; candidates up to 15 times the number of advertised posts (up to cut-off marks rounded to 2 decimal places) will be shortlisted for the Written Examination
2. Written Examination 100 Questions, 100 Marks, Duration: 120 Minutes (2 Hours); Objective and Multiple Choice type questions; Negative marking of ¼ (25%) of prescribed marks for each wrong answer
3. Physical Standard Test (PST) Measurement of height and chest as per prescribed standards; qualifying in nature
4. Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Male candidates: 25 km walk/run carrying 10 kg weight in 4 hours; Female candidates: 14 km walk/run in 4 hours; qualifying in nature
5. Medical Examination Conducted by a Medical Board constituted by the Chief Medical Officer at District Community Health Centres; qualifying in nature
6. Document Verification Verification of all original certificates and documents
Part Subject No. of Questions Total Marks Duration
Part-1 Subject Related Knowledge (Forest & Environment) 50 50 120 Minutes (2 Hours)
Part-2 Elementary Mathematics and Biology 15 15
Part-3 Knowledge of Computer & Information Technology and Contemporary Technological Development & Innovation 15 15
Part-4 General Information related to the State of Uttar Pradesh 20 20
Total 100 100
Gender Category Minimum Height Chest (Unexpanded / Expanded) Weight
Male General / OBC / SC 168 cm 84 cm / 89 cm (min. 5 cm expansion) –
Male ST 160 cm 82 cm / 87 cm (min. 5 cm expansion) –
Female General / OBC / SC 152 cm – 45 to 58 kg
Female ST 147 cm – 45 to 58 kg
The selection process for UPSSSC Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026 is based on a two-tier examination system. In the first stage
candidates will be shortlisted for the Main Written Examination on the basis of their PET-2025 Normalised Score.
Thereafter
candidates who qualify the written examination will be called for Physical Standard Test
Physical Efficiency Test

and Medical Examination — all of which are qualifying in nature. Clearing each stage is mandatory to proceed to the next. Horizontal reservation categories will be adjusted within their respective vertical categories. The detailed selection process is given below.
Written Examination Pattern
Physical Standard Test (PST) – Minimum Requirements
how_to_apply: The application process for UPSSSC Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026 is strictly online. Applications submitted through any other medium will not be accepted. Candidates must read the complete official notification carefully before filling out the application form. All steps of the application process can be completed in one sitting. The step-by-step process to apply online is given below.
Visit the official website upsssc.gov.in and click on the relevant advertisement under the Live Advertisement Segment on the Homepage.
Login using the OTP sent to the registered mobile number or email linked to your PET-2025 Registration Number.

After login, your personal details as entered in PET-2025 (name, father's/husband's name, mother's name, UP domicile status, category, EWS, horizontal reservation details, date of birth, gender, marital status, contact details) will be displayed automatically.

Fill in the mandatory educational qualification details as published in the advertisement, including board/university name, year of passing, certificate number/roll number, date of issue, and marks details.
Select post preference, additional/preferential qualification (if any), and provide permanent and correspondence address details.
The photograph and signature uploaded during PET-2025 will be displayed automatically; no changes can be made to these.
Read the Declaration carefully, select all checkboxes, enter the verification code, and click the "Save and Submit" button.
Pay the prescribed application fee through Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or SBI e-Challan as per instructions given on the website.
After successful fee payment and reconciliation, download and print your application form and keep it safe for future reference.
important_instructions: Candidates are advised to read all instructions issued by UPSSSC carefully before applying for Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026. Non-compliance with any of the instructions may result in outright rejection of the application or disqualification (DEBAR) from future examinations and selections conducted by UPSSSC. The key instructions are listed below.

All candidates belonging to reserved categories of Uttar Pradesh must mandatorily mention their category in the application form. Candidates claiming reservation/age relaxation under more than one reserved category will be given only one relaxation — whichever is more beneficial. Candidates who are not domiciles of Uttar Pradesh are not eligible for reservation benefits; such candidates will be treated as Unreserved (UR). EWS category candidates must possess a valid EWS certificate issued between 01-04-2026 and 20-07-2026 (based on income for FY 2025-26, valid for FY 2026-27). Divyang (Persons with Disability) candidates must update their disability category/sub-category in the application form as per the latest UP Government orders. Ex-Servicemen who will have been discharged from military service by 20-07-2026 are also eligible to avail reservation benefits under the applicable Act. Outstanding sportspersons of Uttar Pradesh must produce the certificate issued by the competent authority as per the prescribed format to avail reservation benefits. No individual correspondence will be made by the Commission with any candidate; all information is uploaded on the official website upsssc.gov.in only. Candidates who are in Central or State Government service must obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer before participating in the selection process. Any act of misconduct, use of unfair means, or malpractice during the examination will result in cancellation of candidature and may lead to DEBAR from all future examinations and selections by UPSSSC. Male candidates who have more than one living wife are not eligible to apply; similarly, a female candidate who has married a person who already has a living wife is not eligible, unless exempted by the Governor. UPSSSC reserves the right to amend, modify, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage without prior notice.

faq.answer: UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026 is a state‑level selection process conducted by Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UPSSSC) to fill 708 permanent posts of Van Rakshak (Forest Guard) and Vanya Jeev Rakshak (Wildlife Guard) in the Forest and Wildlife Department of Uttar Pradesh under Advertisement No. 12‑Pariksha/2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under UPSSSC Forest Guard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 708 vacancies have been announced for Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard posts combined. These include 647 posts for Forest Guard and 61 posts for Wildlife Guard, distributed across general and special selection categories.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard 2026?
faq.answer: The notification was released on 23 April 2026 and the online registration and fee payment window will remain open from 30 June 2026 to 20 July 2026. faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility and educational qualification for UPSSSC Forest Guard 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must have passed Intermediate (10+2) or an equivalent examination from a recognised Board such as Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad, Uttar Pradesh. In addition, candidates must be PET‑2025 qualified and possess a valid UPSSSC PET‑2025 score card, as this recruitment is being conducted for PET‑based shortlisted candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age for both Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard posts is 18 years and the maximum age is 40 years, calculated as on 01 July 2026. Upper age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories, UP State Government employees, Ex‑Servicemen and other eligible categories as per prevailing Uttar Pradesh Government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the pay scale for UPSSSC Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be appointed in Pay Level‑2 with a salary range of ₹19,900 to ₹63,200 plus applicable allowances as per the 7th CPC and UP Government norms. This makes the Forest Guard and Wildlife Guard posts a stable government job option with permanent status.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for UPSSSC Forest Guard Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no examination fee for any category; however, all candidates must pay an online processing fee of ₹25. faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for UPSSSC Forest Guard & Wildlife Guard 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes shortlisting based on PET‑2025 normalised scores, followed by a Main Written Examination, Physical Measurement Test (PMT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Document Verification and Medical Examination. Candidates must qualify each stage separately since PST/PMT, PET and Medical are of qualifying nature and mandatory for final selection.

faq.answer: The written exam will be objective type with 100 multiple‑choice questions for 100 marks and a duration of 120 minutes. The paper generally includes sections on subject‑related knowledge (forest and environment), elementary mathematics and biology, computer and information technology, and general knowledge about Uttar Pradesh, with a negative marking of ¼ marks for each wrong answer.

faq.answer: Eligible PET‑2025 qualified candidates can apply online through the official website upsssc.gov.in between 30 June and 20 July 2026 by logging in with their PET registration details. basic_details.job_title: Jharkhand Home Guard Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 284 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Home Guard / Grih Rakshak (Rural - Gramin, and Urban - Shahri)
basic_details.short_information: The Jharkhand Home Guard Vahini is a voluntary organisation functioning under the Home Department of Jharkhand Government. Home Guards are voluntary members who are deployed on duty as per necessity and paid allowances at the rate determined by the Government for the number of working days of deployment. This is not a permanent or salaried government job. The recruitment drive for Ramgarh district covers both Rural Home Guards (Gramin Grih Rakshak) across 6 blocks and Urban Home Guards (Shahri Grih Rakshak) across 2 urban areas (Technical and Non-Technical). The enrollment is done for a period of 4 years as per the Jharkhand Home Guard (Volunteer) Rules, 2014. Online applications are to be submitted at https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in, and the printed application along with documents must be submitted at the District Commandant Office, Ramgarh.

important_dates.date of physical/written test: To be communicated later through daily newspapers

application_fee.details: The application fee for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026 is ₹100/- (One Hundred Rupees) for all categories. The fee must be paid compulsorily through the link provided on the official website https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in. No other mode of payment is accepted. Candidates must ensure they pay the application fee while submitting the online application. The application fee payment is mandatory to complete the application process.

age_limit.age_details: Voluntary / Honorary (not a salaried government job); enrollment for 4 years
age_limit.details: The age limit for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026 is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The minimum age is 19 years and the maximum age is 40 years for both Rural and Urban Home Guard categories. Candidates must submit a self-attested photocopy of the date of birth proof certificate issued by a competent authority along with their application. No separate age relaxation has been mentioned in the notification for reserved categories; however, the age limit specified is for all candidates as on the cut-off date.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfil all the prescribed eligibility conditions before applying for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026. The eligibility criteria include domicile, age, educational qualification, physical standards, and technical proficiency (for Urban Technical candidates). | Domicile (Aawasiya): For Rural Home Guards - the applicant must be a permanent resident of the block of Ramgarh district from which they are applying. An online residential certificate issued by the competent authority of the State Government must be attached as a self-attested photocopy. For Urban Home Guards - the applicant must be a general resident of the urban area of Ramgarh district, supported by an online residential certificate issued by the competent authority. | Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026): Minimum 19 years and Maximum 40 years. Self-attested photocopy of the date of birth certificate issued by the competent authority must be attached. | Educational Qualification: For Rural Home Guards - minimum Class 7th Pass. For Urban Home Guards - minimum Matriculation (Class 10th) Pass. Self-attested photocopy of the educational certificate issued by the competent authority must be attached. | Physical Standards (same for both Rural and Urban candidates): | Technical Proficiency (only for Urban Technical Home Guard candidates): The applicant must be skilled in various technical fields and should be working/self-employed in a related office/establishment with technical knowledge. The following occupations are generally considered technically qualified - Doctor, Engineer, Advocate, Teacher, Nurse, Computer Operator, Typist, Licensed Driver (HMV/LMV), Cook, Carpenter, Cobbler, Washerman, Barber, Electrician, Gardner, Plumber, etc. Self-attested photocopy of the proof certificate issued by the competent authority must be attached.

salary_details.details: Home Guards (Grih Rakshak) are voluntary members of the Jharkhand Home Guard Vahini and are not entitled to a regular salary. They are paid allowances at the rate determined by the Government of Jharkhand for the number of working days on which they are deployed on duty as per requirement. | This is not a salaried government job or a means of regular livelihood. The number of days of deployment and the applicable allowance rate are determined by the organisational requirement and the Home Department of Jharkhand Government from time to time. Candidates must clearly understand this voluntary nature of service before applying.
