The selection process for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026 is based on the guidelines of the Home Department
Jharkhand Government
Ranchi vide Notification No. 5442 dated 20.10.2014. The selection consists of a Physical Test
a Hindi Writing Ability Test
and a Technical Proficiency Test (for Urban Technical candidates only).
After all tests are completed
a merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in all the tests combined
and selection will be made against the available vacancies. Selected candidates will be required to undergo medical examination and police character verification.
After being found medically fit and cleared in character verification
selected candidates will be given basic Home Guard training at Headquarters
Jharkhand Home Guard Vahini
Ranchi. On successful completion of training
they will be enrolled as Home Guards and issued an enrollment certificate as per provisions.
(क) Physical Test (Sharirik Janch Pariksha): Consists of 1 Mile Run
High Jump
Long Jump
and Shot Put. Marks are awarded as follows:
(i) For Rural Home Guards and Non-Technical Urban Home Guard candidates:
(ii) For Technical Urban Home Guard candidates:
Hindi Writing Ability Test (Hindi Lekhan Kshamata Pariksha): Candidates who pass the Physical Test will appear in the Hindi Writing Ability Test. For Rural Home Guards
writing ability will be assessed at the level of Class 7
and for Urban Home Guards at the level of Class 10. This is a qualifying test. Maximum marks: 100; Pass marks: 30.

Technical Proficiency Test (only for Urban Technical candidates): Candidates who pass the Physical Test and Hindi Writing Ability Test will appear in the Technical Proficiency Test. This is also a qualifying test. Maximum marks: 100; Pass marks: 30.
Sports Bonus Marks: Candidates who have been awarded at the National or State level in sports competitions will be given additional bonus marks as follows:

Bonus Marks for Government Servants: Government servants are eligible for enrollment as Urban Home Guards up to 10% of total Urban vacancies. They will be given bonus marks based on educational qualification as follows: Post-Graduate - 10 marks
Graduate - 8 marks
Intermediate - 6 marks
Matriculation - 4 marks.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply online only at https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in, which is made available by JAP IT, Ranchi, Jharkhand.

The online application window is open from 07/05/2026 to 04/06/2026. The application fee of ₹100/- must be paid through the link provided on the website. Rural Home Guards and Urban Home Guards must fill in separate prescribed application formats. In the online application, candidates must upload a self-attested proof certificate, a recent passport-size colour photograph, and their signature.

After successfully submitting the online application, the printout (along with all attachments) must be submitted at the District Commandant Office, Ramgarh from 08/05/2026 to 05/06/2026. Only one application per candidate will be accepted; in case of multiple applications, the candidature will be automatically cancelled. Candidates must provide two photo identity proofs (from Voter ID/Driving License/PAN Card/Aadhar Card) in the online application form.
Visit the official recruitment portal at https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in
Read all instructions carefully and ensure eligibility before applying
Fill in the online application form accurately with all required details
Upload self-attested proof certificate, passport-size colour photograph, and signature
Pay the application fee of ₹100/- through the prescribed online link on the website
Take a printout of the successfully submitted online application (6 copies for Rural / as required for Urban)
Submit the printout with all required document attachments at District Commandant Office, Ramgarh from 08/05/2026 to 05/06/2026
Submit only one application; multiple applications will lead to automatic cancellation
important_instructions: Home Guard service is voluntary and not a salaried government job. Enrollment is for 4 years as per Jharkhand Home Guard (Volunteer) Rules, 2014. 50% of posts are reserved for women in both Rural and Urban categories. 5% of women-reserved posts are for eligible divorced/deserted/widowed women candidates. 50% of Urban posts are for technically skilled candidates. Government servants can be enrolled up to 10% of total Urban Home Guard vacancies. One application per candidate only; multiple applications will be automatically cancelled. Any incorrect information in the online application will result in automatic cancellation. Physical/Written Test date and venue will be communicated through daily newspapers. SC/ST candidates are entitled to relaxation in physical measurements with valid caste certificate. All disputes regarding enrollment are governed by the Jharkhand Home Guard (Volunteer) Rules, 2014.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026 and how are they divided?
faq.answer: Jharkhand Home Guard Vahini, Ramgarh has released 284 Home Guard (Grih Rakshak) vacancies, comprising 213 Rural Home Guard posts across 6 blocks and 71 Urban Home Guard posts (Technical & Non‑Technical) across 2 urban areas, with 50% reservation for women in both Rural and Urban categories.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates and printout submission dates for Ramgarh Home Guard 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window is from 07 May 2026 to 04 June 2026 on the official recruitment portal recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in. After online submission, candidates must submit the printout with documents at the District Commandant Office, Ramgarh between 08 May 2026 and 05 June 2026; applications without printout submission will not be considered complete.

faq.question: Q3. What is the nature of engagement and salary/allowance for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh posts?
faq.answer: Home Guard is a voluntary, honorary service, not a permanent or salaried government job; enrolled Home Guards are paid duty‑based allowances at rates fixed by the Jharkhand Government only for the days they are deployed on duty, and there is no fixed monthly salary or regular livelihood guarantee.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for Rural and Urban Home Guards in Ramgarh?
faq.answer: For Rural Home Guard (Gramin Grih Rakshak), the minimum qualification is Class 7th pass, while for Urban Home Guard (Shahri Grih Rakshak), the minimum qualification is Matriculation/Class 10th pass from a recognised board.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As on 01 January 2026, the minimum age is 19 years and the maximum age is 40 years for both Rural and Urban Home Guards, similar to other district Home Guard notifications in the state.

faq.question: Q6. What are the domicile and residential requirements for Ramgarh Home Guard posts?
faq.answer: For Rural Home Guard, the applicant must be a permanent resident of the concerned block of Ramgarh district from which they apply. For Urban Home Guard, the applicant must be a general resident of the urban area of Ramgarh district. In both cases, an online residential certificate issued by the competent authority of Jharkhand must be uploaded and attached with the printout.

faq.question: Q7. What are the physical standards (height and chest) for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh recruitment?
faq.answer: Indicative state Home Guard norms apply: male candidates of General/OBC/BC categories must have a minimum height of 162 cm and chest 79 cm (unexpanded), while SC/ST males require 157 cm height and 76 cm chest; female candidates of all categories require a minimum height of 148 cm, with no chest measurement.

faq.question: Q8. How can candidates apply online and submit applications for Jharkhand Home Guard Ramgarh 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must visit recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in, select the Ramgarh Home Guard Recruitment 2026 link, register with a mobile number/email, fill the online form separately for Rural or Urban Home Guard, upload photograph, signature, domicile, educational, caste and other certificates, pay the ₹100 application fee online, submit the form, then take a printout and submit it with all enclosures at the District Commandant Office, Ramgarh between 08 May and 05 June 2026.

basic_details.job_title: UP Excise Constable Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 722 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UPSSSC) has officially released a recruitment notification for Excise Constable. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

important_dates.raw: Date of Advertisement: 06-04-2026 Starting Date for Apply Online: 04-06-2026 Last Date for Apply Online: 24-06-2026 Last Date for Fee Reconciliation & Application Modification: 01-07-2026

age_limit.details: Minimum Age Limit: 18 Years Maximum Age limit: 40 Years Age relaxation is admissible as per rules

selection_process: Selection will be made through Main Written Examination followed by Physical Standards Test and Physical Efficiency Test (qualifying in nature).

Shortlisting for Main Examination: Based on normalized score of Preliminary Eligibility Test-2025 (PET-2025). Only candidates who have qualified PET-2025 with valid Score Card can apply. Candidates securing zero or negative marks in PET-2025 will not be shortlisted. Main Written Examination: 100 Multiple Choice Questions (Objective Type)

2 Hours duration. Negative Marking: 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. Minimum qualifying marks will be decided by the Commission. Syllabus: Part-1 (85 Marks): History of India & Indian National Movement
Geography of India & World
Economic & Social Development of India
Indian Polity & Constitution
General Science & Technology
Environmental Ecology & Disaster Management
Current Events of National & International Importance
Data Interpretation

Important Legal Provisions & Policies related to Excise in Uttar Pradesh. Part-2 (15 Marks): Knowledge of concepts of Computer & Information Technology and contemporary technological development & innovation in this field. Physical Standards Test (Qualifying): Male: Height 167.6 cm
Chest 80 cm (unexpanded) / 85 cm (expanded). Female: Height 152 cm (General)
147 cm (SC/ST)

Weight 45 kg. Physical Efficiency Test (Qualifying): Male: 4.8 km run in 25 minutes. Female: 2.4 km run in 14 minutes. Final selection is based on marks obtained in the Main Written Examination. Physical tests are qualifying in nature. Result of Main Examination and final merit list will be declared on the official website.
how_to_apply: Application Mode: Online only through the official website https://upsssc.gov.in
Steps to Apply:
Visit the official website of UPSSSC and go to the Live Advertisement segment.
Login using your PET-2025 Registration Number + OTP received on registered mobile/email.
Fill the online application form with all mandatory details (personal information, educational qualification, category, horizontal reservation, etc.).
Upload/verify photo and signature (auto-populated from PET-2025).
Pay the online processing fee through Credit Card/Debit Card/Net Banking/UPI or SBI e-Challan.
Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.
After successful fee payment/reconciliation, download and print the final application form.
important_instructions: Only candidates who have appeared in and qualified PET-2025 with a valid Score Card are eligible to apply. The number of vacancies is tentative and may increase or decrease. Reservation benefits (vertical & horizontal) will be given as per latest UP Government orders. Candidates must possess the required educational qualification and other eligibility criteria as on the last date of submission of application. Age and experience will be counted as on the last date of application. Candidates belonging to reserved categories must produce original valid certificates in the prescribed format at the time of document verification. EWS candidates must submit the latest Income & Asset Certificate (issued between 01-04-2026 to 24-06-2026) based on financial year 2025-26. No change in category or any details will be allowed after the last date of modification. Mere submission of application does not guarantee eligibility or call for the Main Examination. All updates, admit cards, results, etc., will be available only on the official website https://upsssc.gov.in. Candidates are advised to visit the website regularly. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. Any dispute will be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts in Lucknow only. The decision of the Commission shall be final and binding.

important_dates.result_date: 31-08-2026 (Degree / Provisional Degree Certificate must be submitted by this date)

age_limit.details: Candidates must not exceed 30 years of age as on the closing date of online application (15-06-2026). selection_process: 1st Tie-Break Date of birth - the candidate born earlier shall be placed higher in the merit list
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of the first letter of the initial name (if date of birth is also same)
Bond Amount Rs. 5.00 Lakhs
Minimum Service Period 3 years from date of joining NHAI
Consequence of Early Resignation / Termination (within 3 years) Candidate shall be liable to pay Rs. 5.00 Lakhs to NHAI

Tie-Breaking Rules
Service Bond Conditions
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official NHAI website https://www.nhai.gov.in. Applications received through any other method will not be accepted and will be summarily rejected. Candidates are advised to submit their online application well in advance without waiting for the closing date. In case of multiple applications, only the last application submitted will be considered and all earlier applications will be summarily rejected. Once submitted, candidates will not be allowed to withdraw their candidature. All communication from NHAI will be made through email only - candidates must furnish a correct and active email address.
Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:
Scanned colour passport-size photograph (JPG / JPEG / PNG / GIF format, not exceeding 1 MB)
Scanned signature (JPG / JPEG / PNG / GIF format, not exceeding 1 MB)
Class X Certificate indicating date of birth (PDF format, not exceeding 2 MB)
Caste / Category Certificate - SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen as applicable, in prescribed format (PDF format, not exceeding 2 MB)

Certificate of passing Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering - Degree Certificate or Provisional Degree Certificate (PDF format, not exceeding 2 MB); candidates awaiting results should upload the same once declared, latest by 31-08-2026
GATE 2026 Score Card in Civil Engineering (PDF format, not exceeding 2 MB)
Apply Online at: https://www.nhai.gov.in → About Us → Recruitment → Vacancies → Current → Deputy Manager (Technical) → Online Application
For technical issues with the online application: Tel. No. 011-25074100 / 25074200 Extn. No. 1028 | Email: [email protected]
important_instructions: Selection is purely on the basis of GATE 2026 score in Civil Engineering - candidates must hold a valid GATE 2026 score card. The post carries All India service liability - only candidates willing to serve anywhere in India should apply. Candidates whose degree result is not declared by 15-06-2026 may apply but must submit the provisional/final degree certificate to NHAI by 31-08-2026, failing which candidature will be cancelled without notice. Candidates who have completed their degree by the closing date must mandatorily upload the Degree Certificate or Provisional Degree Certificate - mere uploading of marksheets of all semesters will not suffice. OBC candidates must submit an OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) certificate issued during Financial Year 2026–27 (issued on or after 01-04-2026). EWS candidates must possess an Income & Asset Certificate based on income for Financial Year 2025–26, issued on or after 01-04-2026. PwBD candidates must possess a Disability Certificate certifying a minimum 40% disability issued by a Competent Authority. No change in community/category status indicated in the online application will be allowed after submission. Selected candidates must execute a Service Bond of Rs. 5.00 Lakhs for a minimum service period of 3 years from date of joining NHAI. NHAI does not entertain transfer of service bonds for candidates already working in other Government organizations. Canvassing or bringing influence in any form will disqualify the candidature. In case of multiple applications, only the last application submitted will be considered; all earlier applications will be summarily rejected. Candidates will not be allowed to withdraw their candidature after submission of the application. Any dispute in regard to any matter shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Delhi Courts only. Corrigendum, addendum, or cancellation, if any, will be published only on the official NHAI website - candidates are advised to check regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for NHAI Deputy Manager Technical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The National Highways Authority of India has announced 60 vacancies for the post of Deputy Manager (Technical) under direct recruitment through GATE 2026 score in Civil Engineering discipline. The vacancies are distributed as: 26 UR, 09 SC, 04 ST, 16 OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and 05 EWS posts. Additionally, 04 posts out of the total 60 are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) across different sub-categories.

faq.question: Q2. What is the application period for NHAI Deputy Manager Technical posts?
faq.answer: The online application registration opens on 15th May 2026 at 10:00 AM and the last date for submission is 15th June 2026 up to 6:00 PM. Candidates are advised to apply well in advance without waiting for the closing date, as applications received through any mode other than online will be summarily rejected.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized University or Institute. Additionally, holding a valid GATE 2026 score in Civil Engineering discipline is mandatory for this recruitment. Candidates whose degree results are not declared by 15th June 2026 may apply provisionally, but must submit their Degree or Provisional Degree Certificate to NHAI by 31st August 2026, failing which their candidature will be cancelled without notice.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit and relaxation for NHAI Deputy Manager Technical post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 30 years as on 15th June 2026 (closing date of application). Age relaxation is provided as follows: SC/ST candidates get 5 years, OBC (NCL) get 3 years, PwBD-General get 10 years, PwBD-OBC get 13 years, PwBD-SC/ST get 15 years, and Ex-Servicemen (with at least 5 years military service) get 5 years relaxation. If a candidate is eligible for more than one relaxation, only the highest permissible relaxation will be granted.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary and pay scale for Deputy Manager Technical posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed in Level-10 of Pay Matrix (7th CPC) with a basic pay range of Rs. 56,100 to Rs. 1,77,500 per month along with Central Dearness Allowance (CDA). This pay scale reflects the responsibility of managing important national highway projects and offers long-term career growth opportunities in the infrastructure development sector.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for NHAI Deputy Manager Technical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection will be made purely on the basis of GATE 2026 score in Civil Engineering discipline. There is no separate written examination conducted by NHAI. However, in the event of a large number of applications, NHAI may adopt a shortlisting criteria and may call candidates for interaction/interview if required. The mere possession of minimum qualifications does not entitle candidates to be considered for appointment.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for NHAI Deputy Manager Technical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification. Candidates are advised to check the official NHAI website at www.nhai.gov.in for any fee-related updates before submitting their application.

faq.question: Q8. What is the service bond requirement for selected candidates?
faq.answer: All selected candidates must execute a Service Bond of Rs. 5.00 Lakhs for a minimum service period of 3 years from the date of joining NHAI. If a candidate resigns or their service is terminated within the 3-year period, they shall be liable to pay Rs. 5.00 Lakhs to NHAI. The post carries All India service liability, meaning only candidates willing to serve anywhere in India should apply.

faq.question: Q9. What documents need to be uploaded during online application?
faq.answer: Candidates must upload the following documents in prescribed formats: scanned colour passport-size photograph (JPG/JPEG/PNG/GIF, max 1 MB), scanned signature (JPG/JPEG/PNG/GIF, max 1 MB), Class X Certificate indicating date of birth (PDF, max 2 MB), Caste/Category Certificate for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen as applicable (PDF, max 2 MB), Bachelor's Degree or Provisional Degree Certificate in Civil Engineering (PDF, max 2 MB), and GATE 2026 Score Card in Civil Engineering (PDF, max 2 MB).

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates who have not yet received their degree results apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates whose Bachelor's Degree result in Civil Engineering is not declared by the closing date of application (15th June 2026) may apply provisionally. However, the offer of appointment will be issued only after submission of the Degree or Provisional Degree Certificate to NHAI not later than 31st August 2026. If the certificate is not submitted by this deadline, the candidature will be cancelled without any further notice. Candidates who have already completed their degree by the closing date must mandatorily upload the Degree Certificate or Provisional Degree Certificate, and mere uploading of marksheets of all semesters will not suffice.

basic_details.selection_process_brief: Application > Shortlisting > SSB > Medical > Merit List > Joining Letter
basic_details.short_information: Indian Army 10+2 Technical Entry Scheme (TES-56) Recruitment 2026: Get the latest Indian Army recruitment notification 2026 for 90 vacancies under the Technical Entry Scheme (TES-56) for a course commencing January 2027. The online application process starts on 13th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 12th June 2026. Find Indian Army TES vacancies on the official website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of “Upcoming Indian Army TES-56 Recruitment 2026” notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for Indian Army T

important_dates.interview_date: Upto five days SSB between August to September 2026

application_fee.details: There is no application fee mentioned in the official notification for the Indian Army TES-56 Recruitment 2026. Candidates are advised to visit the official website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in for any updates regarding fee. Applications must be submitted online only and no hard copy of the application is to be sent to the Directorate General of Recruiting.

age_limit.details: A candidate must not be below 16½ years and above 19½ years on the first day of the month in which the course is due to commence i.e. 01 January 2027. The candidate should not be born before 01 July 2007 and not after 01 July 2010 (both days inclusive). Only the date of birth as recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination (10th) certificate will be accepted; no other document relating to age will be accepted.

qualification.education_qualification: Details
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (12-06-2026). Only unmarried male candidates who have passed 10+2 with PCM subjects from a recognized education board and appeared in JEE (Mains) 2026 are eligible to apply. Knowledge of Bengali or Kokborok is not applicable for this entry. Candidates must possess the prescribed minimum qualification on the closing date of application submission; admission at all stages is provisional and subject to eligibility verification. | Educational Qualification

vacancy_details.Parent Arm/Service: Corps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers

salary_details.pay_scale: Level 10: Rs. 56,100–1,77,500/- (on commissioning as Lieutenant); CTC approx. Rs. 17–18 Lakh per annum

selection_process: Basis of Shortlisting Details
JEE Mains 2026 Common Rank List (CRL) Cut-off to be fixed by Integrated HQ of MoD (Army) without assigning any reason. Shortlisting result tentatively in first week of July 2026.
Stage Details
Stage I Psychological test, Group Testing. Candidates who fail Stage I are returned on the same day.
Stage II Group Testing Officer tests and Interview by Interviewing Officer. Only those clearing Stage I proceed to Stage II.
SSB Centres Prayagraj (UP), Bhopal (MP), Bengaluru (Karnataka), Jalandhar (Punjab)
Likely SSB Dates August to September 2026

Candidates recommended by SSB will undergo Medical Examination at the nearest Military Hospital. Medical standards as applicable for Officers Entry into Army. Proceedings of Medical Board are confidential.

Merit list prepared solely on the basis of marks obtained in SSB Interview. Higher educational qualifications, previous performances, NCC background etc. have no role in finalization of merit list. Joining Letters issued subject to vacancies and medical fitness.
Sl. No. Activity Minimum Standard (Officer Cadet - Male)
1 2.4 Km Run 10 Minutes 30 Seconds
2 Push Up 40
3 Pull Up 06
4 Sit Up 30
5 Squats Two Sets of 30 Repetitions
6 Lunges Two Sets of 10 Repetitions
7 Swimming Should know fundamentals of swimming
The selection process for Indian Army TES-56 Recruitment 2026 consists of multiple successive stages – shortlisting of applications based on JEE Mains CRL
SSB Interview (five days)
Medical Examination
Merit List
and finally issuance of Joining Letter. Candidates who qualify the SSB will be called for Medical Examination. The final merit list is based solely on marks obtained in the SSB Interview.
Merely qualifying at the SSB interview does not confirm final selection. If any candidate fails to report at the allotted CTW within 07 days from the allotted date of reporting
their candidature will be cancelled and the next candidate in merit will be upgraded to fill the vacancy.
Stage 1 - Shortlisting of Applications
Stage 2 - SSB Interview (Five Days)
Stage 3 - Medical Examination
Stage 4 - Merit List & Joining Letter
Minimum Physical Standards on Arrival for Training
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official Indian Army Recruiting website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in by clicking the ‘online application’ button. Hard copy applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal will be active from 13-05-2026 (1200 hrs) to 12-06-2026 (1200 hrs). Candidates must enter their particulars in the online application and read the terms and conditions before submitting the form.

Candidates are permitted to make changes to incorrectly filled data in the online application until the closing of online applications; the application must be re-submitted each time it is edited. After the closing date, no changes to the online application will be permitted and no representation in this regard will be entertained. Candidates must submit only one application; receipt of multiple applications from the same candidate will result in cancellation of candidature. DIGILOCKER certificates will not be accepted in lieu of original certificates.
Documents to be Carried to SSB Interview:
One self-attested printed copy of online application with Roll Number (for submission at Selection Centre)
Class 10th pass certificate and mark sheet in original showing date of birth
Class 12th pass certificate and mark sheet in original
ID proof in original
Copy of result of JEE (Mains) 2026
Two self-attested photocopies of all the above certificates
20 copies of self-attested passport size photographs

The second printed copy of the online application is to be retained by the candidate for his reference. There is no need to send any print copy of the application to the Directorate General of Recruiting.
Apply Online at: https://www.joinindianarmy.nic.in
important_instructions: Only unmarried male candidates who are citizens of India, subjects of Nepal, or persons of Indian origin from specified countries are eligible to apply. Candidates must possess the prescribed minimum qualification (10+2 with 60% PCM and JEE Mains 2026 appearance) on the closing date of application. No hard copy applications will be accepted – online submission only. Candidates must undertake not to marry until they complete full training; violation will lead to discharge and recovery of training cost. PCM percentage must be entered up to two decimals and must not be rounded off; any false entry will result in outright rejection. Candidate should not have been debarred from appearing in any examination by UPSC. Candidate should not have been arrested/convicted by a criminal court or involved in any court case. Candidate withdrawn from NDA, OTA, IMA, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy or any service training academy on disciplinary grounds is not eligible to apply. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are banned inside SSB/interview premises. No compensation will be paid in respect of any injury sustained during SSB tests. No request for change of SSB interview date/centre will be entertained or replied to. Candidates appearing for SSB interview for the first time are entitled to AC III Tier to-and-fro railway fare or bus fare including reservation-cum-sleeper charges within Indian geographical limits. Candidates are advised to visit https://www.joinindianarmy.nic.in regularly for updates. Queries will be replied only through the ‘Feedback/Queries’ option on the website. Queries received up to 03 days prior to closing of online application will only be entertained/replied. In case of any dispute between English and Hindi versions of the notification, the English version will prevail.

faq.question: Q1. What is Indian Army TES-56 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Indian Army TES-56 is a Technical Entry Scheme for unmarried male candidates who have completed 10+2 with PCM and appeared in JEE Mains 2026, leading to a Permanent Commission as Lieutenant.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in TES-56 2026?
faq.answer: There are 90 vacancies (provisional) announced for the TES-56 course commencing in January 2027.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for Indian Army TES-56 2026?
faq.answer: The online application starts on 13 May 2026 (12 PM) and the last date is 12 June 2026 (12 PM).

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for Indian Army TES-56 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 10+2 with at least 60% in PCM and must have appeared in JEE Mains 2026. Only unmarried male candidates are eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for TES-56 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 16½ to 19½ years as on 1 January 2027 (born between 01 July 2007 and 01 July 2010).

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Indian Army TES-56?
faq.answer: The selection includes shortlisting based on JEE Mains rank, SSB interview, medical examination, and final merit list.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for TES-56 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for applying to Indian Army TES-56.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary after selection in TES-56?
faq.answer: After training, candidates are commissioned as Lieutenant with salary ₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500 (Level 10) and approx. ₹17–18 LPA CTC.

faq.question: Q9. What is the training duration for TES-56?
faq.answer: The total training duration is 4 years (3 years at CTW + 1 year at IMA Dehradun), after which candidates receive an engineering degree.

faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official website joinindianarmy.nic.in by filling out the application form before the deadline.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies have been announced for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force has announced vacancies for Group C Civilian posts through Advertisement No. 01/2026 across various Air Force Stations/Units. The total number of vacancies is distributed across multiple commands: HQ Maintenance Command (14 posts), HQ Training Command (5 posts), HQ Central Air Command (3 posts), HQ Western Air Command (6 posts), HQ Eastern Air Command (6 posts), HQ South Western Air Command (5 posts), HQ Southern Air Command (1 post), and Independent Units (7 posts). The posts include Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD).

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: For Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Hindi Typist positions, candidates must have passed 12th Class from a recognised Board with typing speed of 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi on computer (corresponding to 10500 KDPH/9000 KDPH on an average of 5 key depressions for each word). For Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD), candidates must have Matriculation pass or equivalent qualification from a recognised board or university, hold a valid Civil Driving Licence for light and heavy vehicles, possess professional skill in driving and knowledge of motor mechanism, and have minimum two years' experience in driving motor vehicles.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for all posts is 18 to 25 years, calculated as on the last date of receipt of application. Age relaxation is provided for reserved categories: OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (up to 28 years), SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (up to 30 years), Persons with Disabilities (PwBD) get 10 years relaxation with additional 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC categories. Ex-Servicemen get relaxation for full period of continuous service in Armed Forces (minimum 6 months), and Departmental Employees can apply up to 40 years (45 years for SC/ST).

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned in the official notification for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026. However, candidates must attach a self-addressed envelope with stamps of Rs. 10/- pasted along with the application. The application process is completely offline and candidates need to send their applications by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: All the Group C Civilian posts including Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD) are placed at Level-2 as per Pay Matrix 7th CPC. This corresponds to a pay scale of Rs. 19,900 - Rs. 63,200 plus allowances as admissible. The exact salary depends on various allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other applicable benefits.

faq.question: Q6. How to apply for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is strictly offline. Candidates must download and fill the Application Form as per the prescribed Proforma (Annexure-I) given in the advertisement, affix a recent passport-size self-attested photograph, attach self-attested copies of all required documents (educational qualification, age, caste certificate, PwBD certificate if applicable, experience certificate), attach a self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamps, and send the complete application by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit's postal address. The envelope must be superscribed clearly with "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF AND CATEGORY ". Applications must reach within 30 days from the date of publication in Employment News (i.e., by approximately 01-06-2026).

faq.question: Q7. What is the last date to apply for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of application is 30 days from the date of publication of this advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar. Since the advertisement was published in Employment News dated 2-8 May 2026, the last date is approximately 01-06-2026. Applications received prior to the date of publication or after the closing date will not be entertained and will be rejected. IAF will not be held responsible for any postal delay.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process involves multiple stages: Stage 1 - Scrutiny of Applications based on age limit, minimum qualification, documents and certificates; Stage 2 - Written Test with 100% weightage (question cum answer paper in English and Hindi) covering General Intelligence, English Language, Numerical Aptitude & General Awareness for LDC/Hindi Typist, and additionally Trade/Post related questions for CMTD; Stage 3 - Skill/Practical/Physical Test which is qualifying in nature for shortlisted candidates based on merit in written test. Eligible candidates will be issued call letters for the written test, and there will be simultaneous conduct of test for all categories.

faq.question: How many vacancies have been announced for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force has announced vacancies for Group C Civilian posts through Advertisement No. 01/2026 across various Air Force Stations/Units. The total number of vacancies is distributed across multiple commands: HQ Maintenance Command (14 posts), HQ Training Command (5 posts), HQ Central Air Command (3 posts), HQ Western Air Command (6 posts), HQ Eastern Air Command (6 posts), HQ South Western Air Command (5 posts), HQ Southern Air Command (1 post), and Independent Units (7 posts). The posts include Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD).

faq.question: What is the educational qualification required for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: For Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Hindi Typist positions, candidates must have passed 12th Class from a recognised Board with typing speed of 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi on computer (corresponding to 10500 KDPH/9000 KDPH on an average of 5 key depressions for each word). For Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD), candidates must have Matriculation pass or equivalent qualification from a recognised board or university, hold a valid Civil Driving Licence for light and heavy vehicles, possess professional skill in driving and knowledge of motor mechanism, and have minimum two years' experience in driving motor vehicles.

faq.question: What is the age limit for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for all posts is 18 to 25 years, calculated as on the last date of receipt of application. Age relaxation is provided for reserved categories: OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (up to 28 years), SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (up to 30 years), Persons with Disabilities (PwBD) get 10 years relaxation with additional 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC categories. Ex-Servicemen get relaxation for full period of continuous service in Armed Forces (minimum 6 months), and Departmental Employees can apply up to 40 years (45 years for SC/ST).

faq.question: Is there any application fee for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned in the official notification for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026. However, candidates must attach a self-addressed envelope with stamps of Rs. 10/- pasted along with the application. The application process is completely offline and candidates need to send their applications by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit.

faq.question: What is the pay scale for IAF Group C Civilian posts?
faq.answer: All the Group C Civilian posts including Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Hindi Typist, and Civilian Mechanical Transport Driver - Ordinary Grade (CMTD) are placed at Level-2 as per Pay Matrix 7th CPC. This corresponds to a pay scale of Rs. 19,900 - Rs. 63,200 plus allowances as admissible. The exact salary depends on various allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other applicable benefits.

faq.question: How to apply for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is strictly offline. Candidates must download and fill the Application Form as per the prescribed Proforma (Annexure-I) given in the advertisement, affix a recent passport-size self-attested photograph, attach self-attested copies of all required documents (educational qualification, age, caste certificate, PwBD certificate if applicable, experience certificate), attach a self-addressed envelope with Rs. 10/- stamps, and send the complete application by post to the concerned Air Force Station/Unit's postal address. The envelope must be superscribed clearly with "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF AND CATEGORY ". Applications must reach within 30 days from the date of publication in Employment News (i.e., by approximately 01-06-2026).

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for IAF Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of application is 30 days from the date of publication of this advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar. Since the advertisement was published in Employment News dated 2-8 May 2026, the last date is approximately 01-06-2026. Applications received prior to the date of publication or after the closing date will not be entertained and will be rejected. IAF will not be held responsible for any postal delay.

faq.question: What is the selection process for Indian Air Force Group C Civilian Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process involves multiple stages: Stage 1 - Scrutiny of Applications based on age limit, minimum qualification, documents and certificates; Stage 2 - Written Test with 100% weightage (question cum answer paper in English and Hindi) covering General Intelligence, English Language, Numerical Aptitude & General Awareness for LDC/Hindi Typist, and additionally Trade/Post related questions for CMTD; Stage 3 - Skill/Practical/Physical Test which is qualifying in nature for shortlisted candidates based on merit in written test. Eligible candidates will be issued call letters for the written test, and there will be simultaneous conduct of test for all categories.

faq.question: Q6. What are the minimum qualifying marks in India Post WB Circle LDCE 2026?
faq.answer: Minimum qualifying marks in Paper‑I are 40% for General/OBC/EWS and 33% for SC/ST/PwBD, while in DEST (Paper‑III) candidates must secure 75% for General/OBC/EWS and 60% for SC/ST/PwBD, where applicable.

faq.question: Q7. How are Category A and Category B officials defined for India Post WB LDCE 2026?
faq.answer: Category A includes officials in Level‑3 with 3 years’ service, Level‑2 with 5 years’ service (including Level‑1) or Level‑1 with 5 years’ service. Category B includes officials in Level‑1/2/3 with 8 years’ total regular service including regular GDS service (5 years if regular GDS service existed on or before 17.06.2022) and is considered only if sufficient Category A candidates are not available.

suitable weightage will be given to this factor as well as the age of the candidates.
Selection Process Steps:

Selection Trials: Candidates possessing requisite qualifications will be called for selection trials to be conducted by the Sports Board of the Bank Interview: Candidates who qualify in selection trials for the discipline applied for will be called for interview at respective centres (Jammu and/or Srinagar) Merit List Preparation: Merit list of the candidates based on the marks obtained by them as per the selection criteria will be prepared in descending order for respective post and sport discipline Final Selection: Final selection will be made on the basis of merit list up to the number of vacancies
Selection Criteria:
Selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit

determined under selection criteria of the Bank Recruitment mainly based on sports qualifications with suitable weightage Age of candidates will also be considered Performance of candidates in the previous two years will be given preference In case of equal marks obtained by two or more candidates
the merit order will be on the basis of Date of Birth (candidate junior/younger in age will be placed higher in the merit list)
Interview Centers:
Jammu and/or Srinagar Selection process shall be conducted by Sports Board of the Bank
Important Notes:

The Bank shall reserve the right to restrict number of candidates to be called for interview Mere fulfilment of requisite qualifications will not confer any right on a candidate to be called for interview Candidates found suitable will be waitlisted Final selection will depend
inter-alia
on verification of character & antecedents
medical fitness and verification of genuineness of sports certificates to the Bank's satisfaction

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in J&K Bank Football Players Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: J&K Bank has notified 7 vacancies for Football Players under the Sports Quota in Assistant Banking Associate and Banking Attendant cadre for the J&K Bank Football Team.

faq.question: Q2. What are the application dates for J&K Bank Football Players Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window for J&K Bank Football Players Recruitment 2026 is open from 11 May 2026 to 25 May 2026, and candidates must apply through the official J&K Bank careers portal only.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for J&K Bank Football Players 2026?
faq.answer: For Assistant Banking Associate, candidates must have passed 12th standard from a recognised board, while for Banking Attendant, candidates must have passed 10th standard from a recognised board.

faq.question: Q4. Who can apply for J&K Bank Football Players Recruitment 2026 under Sports Quota?
faq.answer: Only domiciles of UT of J&K or Ladakh who have represented in football at international, national, state/UT, district or university level are eligible to apply under this sports quota recruitment.

faq.question: Q5. What are the age limits for J&K Bank Football Player posts in 2026?
faq.answer: As on 01 January 2026, the age limit is 18 to 30 years for Banking Attendant and 20 to 28 years for Assistant Banking Associate, with upper age relaxations for eligible sports persons as per rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary of J&K Bank Football Players in ABA and BAT cadre?
faq.answer: Selected football players will receive pay as regular staff: ABA in the pay scale Rs. 24,050 – 64,480 and BAT in the pay scale Rs. 19,500 – 37,815, plus other admissible perks of J&K Bank employees.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for J&K Bank Football Players Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process comprises selection trials conducted by the Sports Board of the Bank, followed by an interview at Jammu and/or Srinagar for candidates who qualify in the trials.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for J&K Bank Football Player online form 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must pay a non‑refundable application fee of Rs. 500/- (including GST) per application while submitting the online form on the J&K Bank website.

faq.answer: Candidates selected as Football Players under sports quota will be on an initial probation period of 2 years, after which they will be confirmed in the services of the Bank on successful completion.

basic_details.department: Bihar Home Guard Service Cadre, Home Department (Special Branch), Govt. of Bihar
basic_details.short_information: Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BPSSC recruitment notification 2026 for 122 Adhinayak Anudeshak (Home Guard Commandant Instructor) vacancies under the Bihar Home Guard Service Cadre, Home Department (Special Branch), Govt. of Bihar. The online application process starts on 1st May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 1st June 2026. Find BPSSC vacancies across Bihar on the official website bpssc.bihar.gov.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of “Upcoming BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026” notifications for fr

important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on BPSSC website

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for the post of Havildar Instructor are required to pay a non-refundable examination fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the BPSSC application portal via Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI. Bank charges for using Credit/Debit Card or Net Banking will be borne by the candidates. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Applications for which fee has been charged back by the bank will be rejected and legal action may also be taken.

age_limit.details: Age will be calculated as on 01-08-2025 based on Matriculation or equivalent certificate. Candidates from other states applying under reserved categories will be considered under Unreserved category with the age limit applicable to Unreserved candidates.

qualification.education_qualification: Details
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 01-08-2025. Indian citizenship is mandatory. Male, Female and Third Gender candidates can apply. The benefit of any reservation (including 35% women reservation) is admissible only to original residents (Mool Niwasi) of Bihar State. Candidates from other states belonging to reserved categories will be treated as Unreserved/General candidates. | Educational Qualification

The selection process for BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 (two) successive stages - a Written Competitive Examination of 100 marks (MCQ/Objective type

2 hours) and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET) of 100 marks. The written examination is only qualifying for the PET; the final merit list will be based solely on marks obtained in the Physical Efficiency Test.
Candidates scoring less than 30% marks in the written examination will be disqualified from the Physical Efficiency Test. Based on written exam marks
candidates equal to 5 (five) times the number of vacancies for each reservation category will be shortlisted for PET. The appointing authority for selected candidates is the Commandant
Bihar Home Guard
Patna.
Stage 1 - Written Competitive Examination
Each correct answer carries 1 mark. Minimum qualifying marks: 30%. The written exam is qualifying only - not the basis for final merit list.
If examination is held in multiple sessions/shifts
normalized marks will be calculated using Equi-Percentile Method. OMR answer sheets (carbon copy) will be retained for one year after final selection list is published.
Stage 2 - Physical Efficiency Test (PET) - Total 100 Marks
Physical Efficiency Test standards and process are the same as those prescribed for the post of Constable in Bihar Police. PET consists of three events - Running
Shot Put
and High Jump. Candidates must qualify each event separately. Only 3 chances are given for Shot Put and High Jump.
(A) Running - Maximum 50 Marks
Male candidates exceeding 6 minutes and Female candidates exceeding 5 minutes will be declared disqualified.
(B) Shot Put - Maximum 25 Marks
(C) High Jump - Maximum 25 Marks
Tie-Breaking Rules

physical_eligibility.details: No marks are awarded for Height, Chest, and Weight; however, candidates not meeting the prescribed standards will be declared disqualified. Physical standards for Kinnar/Kothi/Hijra/Transgender candidates will be the same as those for Backward Class candidates. | Height | Chest (Male Candidates Only) | Weight

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the BPSSC website. Hard copy of applications need not be sent to the Commission. Candidates should visit https://bpssc.bihar.gov.in and click on the "Home Guard" tab, then click the link related to Advt. No. 05/2026 to begin the application process. Only one online application can be submitted per candidate. If multiple applications are submitted by a candidate, all will be rejected and legal action may also be taken.

The online application is filled in two parts. In Part 1, candidates register with their name, nationality, mobile number, email ID, domicile, reservation category, gender, and date of birth, followed by fee payment. After successful payment, Registration ID and Password are sent via SMS and Email. In Part 2, candidates fill in father/husband name, mother's name, address, educational qualifications, upload photograph and signature, and finally submit the application.
Photo and Signature Upload Requirements:
Photograph: Colour, taken within last 2 months, white background, between 15-25 KB, .jpg/.jpeg/.gif format
Signature: In English and Hindi (black or blue ink), separate, white background, between 15-25 KB, .jpg/.jpeg/.gif format
Non-compliance with photo/signature instructions will result in rejection of application

Candidates must keep a valid and active mobile number and email ID (preferably registered in their name) as all important communications including hall tickets will be sent via SMS and Email throughout the recruitment process.
Apply Online at: https://bpssc.bihar.gov.in → "Home Guard" Tab → Advt. No. 05/2026
important_instructions: Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply. Male, Female and Third Gender candidates can apply. All reservations (including 35% women's reservation) are applicable only to original residents (Mool Niwasi) of Bihar State. Candidates from other states must apply as Unreserved/General. Married female candidates found pregnant at the time of Running/Physical Efficiency Test (as per medical report) will not be allowed to participate and will be declared disqualified. Married female candidates must submit a declaration that they are not pregnant. Candidates must ensure they fulfill all eligibility criteria before applying. False information in applications will lead to rejection and legal action. No hard copy of application to be sent to the Commission - online submission only. Examination fee is non-refundable; fee charged back from bank will result in rejection and legal action. Only one application per mobile number is allowed. Candidates must be physically present with mobile phone during registration as Registration ID and Password are sent by SMS. Date of Birth will be calculated strictly as per Matriculation or equivalent certificate. Married/divorced/separated female candidates applying under reserved category must submit caste certificate based on their father's caste (not husband's), as caste is determined by birth, not marriage. EWS certificate must be based on income and assets for financial year 2025-26 as per prescribed format from General Administration Department, Bihar. The selection process may take approximately 6 months. No extension will be granted for any reason (personal, medical, pregnancy, fracture, etc.) at any stage. Candidates are advised to regularly check the BPSSC website and keep their registered mobile/email active for all updates. All decisions of the Commission regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026 and what is the post name?
faq.answer: Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission has released 122 vacancies for the post of Adhinayak Anudeshak (Havildar Instructor), Level‑4 in the Bihar Home Guard Service Cadre under the Home Department (Special Branch), Government of Bihar.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates for Bihar BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window will be open from 01 May 2026 to 01 June 2026, and candidates must submit their forms and examination fee through the official BPSSC website bpssc.bihar.gov.in within this period; no offline applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q3. How are the 122 Havildar Instructor vacancies distributed category‑wise and what is the women’s reservation?
faq.answer: The 122 posts are distributed among UR, SC, ST, EBC, BC, EWS and BC‑Women categories, with 35% horizontal reservation for women within each category, applicable only to women who are original residents of Bihar. In addition, 2% (2 posts) are reserved horizontally for grandchildren/great‑grandchildren of State freedom fighters receiving pension from the Central Government.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for BPSSC Havildar Instructor 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Intermediate (10+2) or an equivalent examination from a recognised board on or before the cut‑off date 01 August 2025. The written examination standard will be of Matric/10th level, as per Bihar School Examination Board syllabus, even though the minimum qualification is 12th pass.

faq.question: Q5. What is the examination fee for BPSSC Havildar Instructor 2026 and how to pay it?
faq.answer: All candidates (male, female and third gender, all categories) must pay a non‑refundable examination fee of Rs. 100/-, which is payable online only through Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or UPI on the BPSSC application portal; any chargeback or unsuccessful fee payment will lead to rejection of the application and may invite legal action.

faq.question: Q6. How many candidates will be called for PET and what are the key PET benchmarks?
faq.answer: Candidates up to 5 times the number of vacancies in each category (based on written‑exam marks) will be called for PET. In the PET, male candidates must complete 1.6 km running within a maximum of 6 minutes (less than 5 minutes for 50 marks), while female candidates must run 1 km within a maximum of 5 minutes (less than 4 minutes for 50 marks), with graded marks for intermediate timings. Shot Put and High Jump also carry graded marks with three attempts per event, and failure to meet minimum distance/height or time leads to disqualification.

faq.question: Q7. How can candidates apply online for BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must visit bpssc.bihar.gov.in → “Home Guard” tab → Advt. No. 05/2026, complete Part‑1 registration (basic details and fee payment), then log in with the Registration ID and Password sent via SMS/Email and complete Part‑2 by filling personal and educational details and uploading a recent colour photograph and signatures in Hindi and English as per size and format specifications. Only one application per candidate is allowed; multiple applications or incorrect photo/signature uploads can lead to rejection and possible legal action, so applicants should carefully preview and print the final submitted form for future reference.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process and exam pattern for BPSSC Havildar Instructor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection has two stages: Written Competitive Examination – single OMR‑based paper of 100 objective questions for 100 marks, covering Hindi, English, Mathematics, Social Science, Science, General Knowledge and Current Affairs, of 2 hours duration. Candidates must score at least 30% marks to qualify for the PET; marks of the written exam are used only for shortlisting, not for final merit. Physical Efficiency Test (PET) – for 100 marks, including Running (50 marks), Shot Put (25 marks) and High Jump (25 marks), with different benchmarks for male and female candidates; final merit list is prepared solely on PET scores, subject to qualifying in all events and medical/document verification.

basic_details.job_title: Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026 Online Form - Apply Online for 344 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The CSC e-Governance Services Indian has officially released a recruitment notification for Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

important_dates.raw: Starting Date for Apply Online: 02-04-2026 Last Date for Apply Online: 20-05-2026

age_limit.details: Minimum Age Limit: 18 Years or above Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online only through the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website. Follow the steps given below to submit your application form.
Visit Official Website
Go to the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website: csc.gov.in.
On the homepage, navigate to the Recruitment/Careers/Latest Opening section.
Open Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Application Link
Find and click on the notification/link titled “CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026” or similar.
Click on the Apply Online button to proceed to the application page.
Registration (New Users)
Click on New Registration (if applying for the first time).
Enter basic details such as Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, Date of Birth, and create a password.
Submit the details to generate your Registration ID and Password (note these for future login).
Login and Fill Application Form
Login using your Registration ID and Password.
Fill in the online application form carefully with personal details, contact details, educational qualification, Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator certificate details, and preferred state/district.
Ensure that all details match your certificates and Aadhaar data.
Upload Documents

Upload scanned copies of the following documents in the prescribed format and size (as mentioned in the notification): Recent passport size photograph Signature 10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency Any other documents required as per notification
Recent passport size photograph
Signature
10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets
Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency
Any other documents required as per notification
Preview the uploaded documents and confirm that they are clear and readable.
Application Fee Payment (If Applicable)
Check the Application Fee section in the official notification.
If any fee is prescribed, pay it online through available modes such as Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.
After successful payment, download/print the fee receipt.
Final Submission
Carefully review all the details filled in the application form in the Preview section.
If required, make corrections before final submission.
Click on Final Submit to complete the application process.
Print Application Form
After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.
Keep the Registration ID, Password, and printout safe for further recruitment stages.

faq.question: Q1. What is CSC e‑Governance Services India Recruitment 2026 for Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator?
faq.answer: CSC e‑Governance Services India Recruitment 2026 is an online hiring drive for 53 posts of Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator in Bihar, Goa, Punjab and Uttar Pradesh, where eligible candidates will work at Aadhaar enrolment/update centres operated through CSCs.

faq.answer: Selected candidates will be paid at least the minimum wages of the respective state prescribed for semi‑skilled manpower; exact amount varies according to state wage notifications.

basic_details.job_title: SSC Stenographer Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 731 Grade C & D Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Stenographer Grade C (Group ‘B’ Non-Gazetted) Stenographer Grade D (Group ‘C’)
basic_details.short_information: Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Stenographer Grade ‘C’ & ‘D’ Examination 2026 Notification on 24th April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 731 tentative vacancies for Stenographer Grade C (Group B Non-Gazetted) & Grade D (Group C) posts in various Ministries/Departments/Organizations of Govt. of India. Candidates aged 18-30 years (Grade C) / 18-27 years (Grade D) as on 01.08.2026, with 12th standard pass & stenography skills, are eligible to apply online only at ssc.gov.in from 24 April to 15 May 2026

application_fee.details: For General / OBC (NCL) / EWS: ₹100/- For SC / ST / PwBD / ESM / Women: No fee (exempted) Pay online via BHIM UPI / Net Banking / Card

age_limit.age_details: 18-30 Yrs (Grade C) / 18-27 Yrs (Grade D) as on 01.08.2026
age_limit.details: Crucial date: 01.08.2026 (born not before 02.08.1996 & not later than 01.08.2008 for Grade C; adjust for Grade D).

qualification.details: Nationality: Indian citizen / Subject of Nepal/Bhutan / Tibetan refugee (before 01.01.1962) / Person of Indian origin from specified countries (eligibility certificate required). | Essential Educational Qualification (as on 01.08.2026)

salary_details.stenographer grade c (post selection): Pay Level-7 (₹44,900 - 1,42,400); approx. CTC ₹10-12 LPA (incl. DA, HRA, perks).
salary_details.stenographer grade d (post selection): Pay Level-4 (₹25,500 - 81,100); approx. CTC ₹6-8 LPA (incl. DA, HRA, perks).
salary_details.additional benefits: DA, HRA/quarters, TA, medical, PF, gratuity, pension, leave encashment etc. as per rules.

selection_process: Computer Based Test (CBT): 200 marks (objective; General Intelligence & Reasoning 50
GA 50
English 100); qualifying: 30% UR
25% OBC/EWS
20% others. Skill Test in Stenography (Qualifying): Grade C: 100 wpm (English/Hindi); Grade D: 80 wpm (English/Hindi) (10 min dictation; 40% UR/EWS
35% others pass marks). Document Verification (DV) by User Departments. Final Merit: Based on CBT marks (category-wise); All India Service Liability.
Pre-Employment Medical Exam (esp. for BRO posts with Physical Efficiency Test).
how_to_apply: Apply online only via ssc.gov.in → One Time Registration (OTR) → Stenographer 2026 section.
Capture live photo (webcam), upload signature (10-20 KB JPEG).
Fill details, pay fee (if applicable), submit.
Correction window: 20-21 May 2026 (charges apply).
Print application for records; active email/mobile required.
Last date: 15 May 2026 (23:59 Hrs)
important_instructions: Read notification carefully before applying. Ensure eligibility (esp. stenography skill, age/EQ cut-off 01.08.2026). Upload clear photo/signature; Aadhaar authentication recommended. Govt/PSU: NOC/undertaking required. Keep email/mobile active; no fee refund. SSC reserves right to modify/cancel; no waiting list (sliding mechanism for FRTA). Posts AISL; BRO posts need physical/medical standards (males only for BRO Grade D).

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for SSC Stenographer 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for SSC Stenographer 2026 is 15 May 2026 (23:00 Hrs). Candidates must complete registration and fee payment before the deadline. Applications submitted after the closing date will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in SSC Stenographer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: SSC has announced approximately 731 tentative vacancies for Grade C and Grade D posts. The exact category-wise vacancy details will be updated later. Candidates are advised to check the official website regularly for updates.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility qualification for SSC Stenographer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 12th standard or equivalent from a recognized board. In addition to educational qualification, stenography skill is mandatory. The skill test will be conducted during the selection process.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for SSC Stenographer Grade C & D 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for Grade C is 18 to 30 years and for Grade D is 18 to 27 years. The crucial date for calculating age is 01 August 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for SSC Stenographer 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹100 for General, OBC, and EWS candidates. Candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBD, ESM, and women categories are exempted. The fee can be paid through online modes like UPI, net banking, or debit/credit cards.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for SSC Stenographer 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Computer-Based Test followed by a stenography skill test. Candidates who qualify both stages will be called for document verification. Final selection is based on CBT marks.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for SSC Stenographer Grade C and D posts?
faq.answer: The salary for Grade C is under Pay Level-7 ranging from ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400. For Grade D, the salary falls under Pay Level-4 ranging from ₹25,500 to ₹81,100. Additional benefits like DA, HRA, and other allowances are also provided.

faq.question: Q8. When will the SSC Stenographer 2026 exam be conducted?
faq.answer: The SSC Stenographer 2026 exam is expected to be held in July–August 2026. The exact exam dates will be announced officially on the SSC website. Candidates should stay updated and prepare accordingly.

faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the official SSC website by completing One-Time Registration. After registration, they need to fill the application form and upload required documents. The form must be submitted before the deadline.

faq.answer: Yes, stenography skill is compulsory for both Grade C and Grade D posts. Candidates must qualify the skill test to be considered for final selection. Without passing the skill test, candidates will not be eligible for recruitment.

The recruitment drive aims to fill 91 Group-C positions including Library Information Assistant, Technical Assistant, Stenographer Grade II, Technician, LDC, Forest Guard, and MTS posts. Candidates within the age range as per post-wise eligibility criteria and holding qualifications such as 10th Pass, 12th Pass, ITI, Graduation, or Bachelor’s Degree are eligible to apply online at fri.icfre.gov.in. The application process will be active from 04 May 2026 to 03 June 2026, and candidates can easily access

Female, SC, ST, and PwD applicants are required to pay only Rs. 700/- as processing fee - they are exempted from payment of the application fee. The examination fee and processing fee once paid will not be refunded or re-adjusted under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: The crucial date for age calculation will be the last date of submission of online application form i.e. 03-06-2026. Age relaxation is admissible to SC/ST, OBC, PwD, Ex-Serviceman, Central Govt. Civilian Employees, Widows/Divorced Women, and ICFRE Permanent Employees as per Government of India norms. Once a candidate has chosen a reserved category, no request shall be entertained for change to other reserved categories.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (03-06-2026). The qualification prescribed should have been obtained from Govt. recognized Universities/Boards/Institutions. Essential qualification is mandatory for all posts. Candidates should fully satisfy themselves before applying that they possess the minimum qualification and experience as laid down in the advertisement. | Educational Qualification

The selection process for FRI Group C Recruitment 2026 consists of 3 successive stages - Stage-I: Computer Based Test (CBT)
Stage-II: Descriptive Test

and Stage-III: Skill/Trade Test (for specific posts only). Final merit list will be prepared based on marks secured in the CBT for candidates who qualify both the Descriptive Test and Skill/Trade Test (wherever applicable). No interview will be conducted. Shortlisted candidates (05 times the number of vacancies for each advertised category of post) will be called for the Descriptive Test based on their CBT scores.
Stage-I: Computer Based Test (CBT)
Stage-II: Descriptive Test (Qualifying Nature Only)

Descriptive Test will be of qualifying nature only and is intended to test elementary language skills required for Group-C posts. It will be held for all posts in Dehradun only in Pen and Paper Mode. In case of failure to attain minimum qualifying marks (50% of maximum marks)
the candidature will not be considered for final selection/merit. Candidates must not write any personal identity (name
roll number
mobile number
address etc.) inside the Answer Book - failure will be treated as Unfair Means (UFM) and will result in zero marks and rejection of candidature.
Stage-III: Skill/Trade Test (Qualifying Nature Only - For Specific Posts)

Skill/Trade Test will be of qualifying nature only and will be held for candidates who successfully pass the Descriptive Test. Final merit list will be prepared based on marks secured in CBT (with tie-breaking rules as applicable) for the candidates who qualify in the Descriptive and Skill/Trade Test (wherever applicable).
Tie-Breaking Rules for CBT
CBT Tentative Schedule

Note: FRI will not entertain any request for change in test date/time/Centre in case of any clash in the test date/time for any two or more posts. Candidates need to decide the test they would like to take.
how_to_apply: Online submission of application can only be made from the link provided on the official FRI website at https://fri.icfre.gov.in. Candidates should read the instructions carefully before filling up the online application form. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates who desire to apply for more than one post should apply online separately for each post. However, FRI will not be responsible if candidates get the exam centre at long distances or on the same date/time.

Candidates are advised to submit online applications well before the closing date to avoid the possibility of disconnection/inability or failure to login to the FRI website due to heavy load during the closing date. After final submission, no request with respect to change in any data submitted in the online application form will be entertained. In case of any corrigendum/addendum pertaining to this advertisement, the same shall be published on the Institute's website only.
Documents / Upload Requirements:
Recent passport size colour photograph (taken against a light-coloured, preferably white, background; face clearly visible)
Signature (signed on white paper with Black/Blue ink pen - must be of applicant only)
Left hand thumb impression (scanned digital image)
All relevant certificates (educational qualification, age proof, category certificate, etc.) as per instructions given during filling the online application form
Documents to be Produced at the Time of Document Verification (Originals + Copies):
Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (from 10th onwards as applicable)
Age proof certificate
SC/ST Certificate in the prescribed format (as applicable for appointment to posts under Govt. of India)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format valid for FY 2025-26 (issued before last date of application submission)
EWS Income & Asset Certificate in prescribed format from a Competent Authority
PwD Certificate in the prescribed format (as applicable for appointment to posts under Govt. of India)
Ex-Serviceman discharge/service certificate and undertaking (in prescribed format)
NOC from employer (for candidates working in Government Departments, Public Sector Undertakings and Government Funded Research Agencies) - uploaded at the time of form filling
All documents uploaded during online application must be produced at the time of document verification
Apply Online at: https://fri.icfre.gov.in
important_instructions: Online applications only - no other mode of application will be accepted; applications incomplete in any respect will be summarily rejected. Candidates must fully satisfy themselves about their eligibility before applying - if ineligibility is detected at any stage before or after appointment, candidature/appointment shall be cancelled/terminated immediately. Examination fee once paid will not be refunded or re-adjusted under any circumstances - application fee will be refunded only in the event of a post becoming zero. All posts are temporary in nature but likely to continue further; candidates will be confirmed after successfully completing probationary period as per rules. Candidates who desire to apply for more than one post must apply online separately for each post. FRI will not entertain any request for change in test date/time/Centre in case of any clash - candidates need to decide the test they would like to take. Candidates working in Government Departments, PSUs, and Government Funded Research Agencies must send application through proper channel and submit NOC at the time of document verification. For OBC-NCL candidates, the certificate must be valid for FY 2025-26 and issued before the last date of submission of application. Candidates must not write personal identity (name, roll number, mobile number, address etc.) inside the Answer Book during Descriptive Test - failure will be treated as Unfair Means (UFM). Photograph must be a recent passport size colour photograph taken against a light-coloured preferably white background; if photograph and signature are not clearly visible, candidature will be liable to be rejected. Canvassing in any form and/or bringing any influence, political or otherwise, will definitely be treated as a disqualification for the post applied for. All future correspondences regarding the selection process, shortlisting of candidates and important dates shall be uploaded on the Institute's website https://fri.icfre.gov.in - candidates are responsible to check the FRI website regularly. Appointment is subject to candidates being found medically fit as per the requirements of the Institute. Legal disputes, if any, shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the competent court in the state of Uttarakhand.

Employees, Nagar Sainik: As per Chhattisgarh Government rules | Physical Standards (For Male) | Height: Minimum 168 cm (ST candidates relaxation applicable) Chest: 81-86 cm (minimum 5 cm expansion) | Note: Female candidates are exempted from certain physical measurements.

100 Multiple Choice Questions No negative marking Subjects: Subject-specific knowledge + General Knowledge / Reasoning / Analytical Ability

important_instructions: Only candidates who qualified the Physical Efficiency Test are eligible to apply. Only one application per candidate is allowed. Junior Clerk cum Junior Security Assistant
