selection_process: Stage Description Applicable To
Stage 1 Merit-based Shortlisting - candidates shortlisted based on percentage of marks in qualifying examination by the Screening Committee All posts
Stage 2 Written Examination - objective multiple-choice type (MCQ); shortlisted candidates will be called for written exam All posts

Stage 3 Physical Efficiency / Endurance Test - physical fitness and endurance standards to be met Fire Department posts only (Fireman, Senior Fireman, Sub Fire Officer, Fireman Driver, Fire Technician)
Stage 4 Document Verification - candidates clearing written exam / physical test will be called for original document verification All shortlisted candidates

The selection process for BMC Recruitment 2026 consists of multiple stages as determined by the Selection Committee (Pasandgi Samiti). Candidates will first be shortlisted on the basis of academic merit
followed by a written examination
physical test (for fire department posts)
and document verification. Final selection will be merit-based. The Commissioner
Bhavnagar Municipal Corporation
reserves the right to modify the selection process as required.

faq.question: Q5. What are the basic educational qualifications required for different BMC Bhavnagar posts?

application_fee.sc_fee: As per Government Rules (usually exempted or reduced)

Candidates must be Indian citizens and fulfill local domicile requirements wherever applicable.

selection_process: Selection will be made on the basis of Written Examination (Objective MCQ type) conducted through OMR or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT).
Final selection will be based on marks obtained in the written test and document verification. The Board reserves the right to change the examination pattern/method.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the OJAS portal. No offline or hard copy applications will be accepted.
Step-by-Step Application Process:
Visit the Official Portal Go to https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in
One Time Registration (OTPR)
New users must complete One Time Profile Registration (OTPR) first.
Enter basic details like Name, Date of Birth, Mobile Number, Email ID, etc.
After successful registration, a User ID and Password will be generated and sent to your registered mobile and email.
Login and Fill Application Form
Login using your User ID and Password.
Select the desired post(s) you want to apply for (you can apply for multiple posts if eligible).
Carefully fill all the required details in the online application form such as personal information, educational qualifications, category, address, etc.
Upload Scanned Documents
Recent passport size Photograph (maximum 15 KB in JPG format)
Signature (maximum 15 KB in JPG format)
Ensure both are clear and as per specifications.
Pay Application Fee
General category candidates have to pay ₹100/- + service charges.
Pay the fee online through SBI e-Pay (Net Banking, Credit/Debit Card, or other available modes).
Reserved category candidates may be exempted as per rules.
Review and Submit
Preview the entire application form carefully.
Make corrections if needed (before final submission).
Click on “Submit” button.
After successful submission, note down the Confirmation Number / Application Number generated by the system.
Take Printout
Take a printout of the filled application form and payment receipt for future reference.
Important Note:
Candidates are not required to upload any certificates at the time of online application.
Original documents will be verified only after the written examination result is declared for shortlisted candidates.
Apply Online at: https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in
important_instructions: Read the full detailed advertisement carefully before filling the application. Only online applications will be accepted. Hard copies are not allowed. Candidates can apply for multiple posts if they fulfill the eligibility criteria for each. Application fee once paid will not be refunded. Upload correct photograph and signature as per specifications. Keep all original certificates ready for document verification. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited in the examination hall. Visit https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in and https://gpssb.gujarat.gov.in regularly for updates regarding exam date, admit card, and result.

faq.question: Q1. What is GPSSB Group‑3 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Gujarat Panchayat Service Selection Board (GPSSB), Gandhinagar has announced Group‑3 Recruitment 2026 for Laboratory Technician, MPHW (Male), Junior Pharmacist and Female Health Worker posts in the Panchayat Department. This recruitment aims to fill a total of 2640 Class‑III vacancies through a written examination conducted via OMR or Computer Based Recruitment Test on the OJAS Gujarat portal.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under GPSSB Group‑3 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 2640 vacancies have been notified for GPSSB Group‑3 posts in 2026. Out of these, 108 posts are for Laboratory Technician, 420 for Multi Purpose Health Worker (Male), 164 for Junior Pharmacist and 1948 for Female Health Worker, with district‑wise and category‑wise break‑up given in the respective advertisements.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for GPSSB Group‑3 Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: The short and detailed advertisements for GPSSB Group‑3 posts are dated April 2026. The online application process on the OJAS portal starts on 29 April 2026 at 13:00 hrs and will continue till 20 May 2026 up to 23:59 hrs, which is also the last date for online fee payment.

faq.question: Q4. For which posts has GPSSB issued Group‑3 notifications in 2026?
faq.answer: GPSSB has issued multiple Group‑3 advertisements for 2026, including Laboratory Technician (Advt. No. 04/2026‑27), Multi Purpose Health Worker (Male) (Advt. No. 05/2026‑27), Junior Pharmacist (Advt. No. 07/2026‑27) and Female Health Worker (Advt. No. 08/2026‑27). All these posts fall under the Gujarat Panchayat Service Class‑III cadre and are to be filled through direct recruitment.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility criteria for GPSSB Group‑3 posts (education and age limit)?
faq.answer: For Laboratory Technician, candidates generally need a Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry/Microbiology/Biochemistry/Biotechnology plus a Diploma in Laboratory Technology or equivalent as per the notification. For MPHW (Male), Junior Pharmacist and Female Health Worker, the minimum qualification includes 12th Science with relevant MPHW/FHW training certificates or a Bachelor’s degree in Pharmacy along with basic computer knowledge, and age must be between 18 and 36 years with relaxation for reserved categories as per Gujarat Government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for GPSSB Group‑3 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for all GPSSB Group‑3 posts consists of a written examination in objective MCQ format conducted either through OMR sheet or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT). Final selection will be based on marks obtained in the written exam followed by document verification, and the Board reserves the right to modify the exam pattern if required.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for GPSSB Group‑3 online form 2026?
faq.answer: As per recent GPSSB Class‑III notifications, General category candidates are required to pay an application fee of ₹100 plus applicable service charges. Reserved category candidates such as SC/ST/SEBC/EWS and PH are usually exempted or charged a reduced fee, which must be confirmed from the detailed advertisement and OJAS payment instructions.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply online for GPSSB Group‑3 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online only through the OJAS Gujarat portal by visiting ojas.gujarat.gov.in and registering with One Time Registration (OTR) before filling the form. After login, they need to select the relevant GPSSB advertisement, fill personal and educational details, upload a photo and signature within the prescribed size limits, pay the application fee through SBI e‑Pay, and finally submit and print the application form.

faq.question: Q9. Can I apply for more than one GPSSB Group‑3 post in this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates who meet the eligibility conditions for more than one post (for example, Laboratory Technician and Junior Pharmacist) can apply for multiple advertisements separately. However, they must submit separate applications and fees for each post and ensure that exam dates and timings do not clash as per the exam schedule released by GPSSB.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the GPSSB Group‑3 2026 Notification PDF and get latest updates?
faq.answer: The detailed notification PDFs for each Group‑3 post are available on the official websites gpssb.gujarat.gov.in and the OJAS portal under “Current Advertisements”. Candidates should regularly check these sites for corrigenda, exam dates, admit card downloads, answer keys and result updates, as all official information is published online only.

basic_details.department: Bal Vikas Seva Evam Pushtahar, Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow
basic_details.short_information: UP Anganwadi Bharti Lucknow Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026: Get the latest UP Anganwadi recruitment notification 2026 for 198 Anganwadi Karyakartri vacancies under the Bal Vikas Seva Evam Pushtahar, Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow. The online application process starts soon and the last date to apply online is 16th May 2026. Find UP Anganwadi vacancies across Lucknow on the official website upanganwadibharti.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of “Upcoming UP Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026” notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for UP Anganwadi

age_limit.details: The age of the applicant must be minimum 18 years and maximum 35 years as on the cut-off date of 01 January 2026. The date of birth as mentioned in the High School (10th) Marksheet and Certificate will be the only accepted proof for age verification. No other age proof document will be entertained. | Age relaxation as per Government of Uttar Pradesh rules will be applicable to SC/ST/OBC/PwD/Ex-Servicemen and other eligible reserved category candidates as per prevailing government orders.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (16-05-2026). The recruitment is governed by Government Order Shासनादेश संख्या-2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September, 2025. Candidates are advised to go through the said Government Order thoroughly before applying. The complete recruitment process will be conducted as per the said order and full details are available on the official website http://upanganwadibharti.in.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official website http://upanganwadibharti.in. Hard copy applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The last date for online application submission is 16-05-2026 up to 12:00 AM midnight. Candidates are advised to apply well before the last date to avoid last-minute technical issues.

The complete selection process will be conducted as per the Government Order Shासनादेश संख्या-2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September, 2025. Candidates should visit the official website regularly for updates on all recruitment steps.
Apply Online at: http://upanganwadibharti.in
important_instructions: Only bonafide residents of Uttar Pradesh are eligible to apply. Candidates must possess the prescribed minimum qualification on the closing date of application. No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only. The number of vacancies is subject to increase or decrease - नोट: रिक्तियों की संख्या घट-बढ़ सकती है। Village/ward-wise vacancy and reservation details are available on the notice boards of Collectorate, Vikas Bhawan, Tehsil, and Vikas Khand offices. The complete recruitment process will be conducted as per Shासनादेश संख्या-2025/3313/58-1-2025 (1917687) dated 17 September, 2025. Candidates are advised to visit http://upanganwadibharti.in regularly for updates on recruitment steps. All decisions of the Commission regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. What is UP Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026 for Lucknow?
faq.answer: UP Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026 for Lucknow is a district‑level recruitment drive for 198 Anganwadi Karyakartri (Anganwadi Worker) posts under Bal Vikas Seva Evam Pushtahar, Lucknow. The process is being conducted by the Office of the District Programme Officer (Jila Karyakram Adhikari), Lucknow as per the Government Order dated 17 September 2025.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released for Lucknow Anganwadi Worker 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 198 vacancies have been notified for Anganwadi Karyakartri in Lucknow district. These posts are distributed project‑wise across Alam Nagar, Aliganj, Bakshi Ka Talab, Chinhat, Gosainganj, Kakori, Malihabad, Mall, Mohanlalganj and Sarojini Nagar projects, and the exact village/ward‑wise breakup is displayed on official notice boards.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for UP Anganwadi Worker Lucknow Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: The advertisement for Lucknow Anganwadi Worker Recruitment 2026 is dated 25 April 2026. The last date to submit the online application form for the 198 posts in Lucknow is 16 May 2026 up to 12:00 AM (midnight), while the schedule for merit list and further selection steps will be notified on the official portal.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility (age and education) for Lucknow Anganwadi Worker posts?
faq.answer: Female candidates must be at least 18 years and not more than 35 years of age as on 01 January 2026, with relaxations for OBC/SC/ST and other reserved categories as per Uttar Pradesh government rules. The minimum educational qualification for Anganwadi Worker in the ongoing 2026 cycle is generally Intermediate (10+2) or equivalent from a recognized board, as reflected in district‑wise UP Anganwadi notifications including Lucknow.

faq.question: Q5. Who can apply – is it only for residents of Uttar Pradesh?
faq.answer: Only bonafide female residents of Uttar Pradesh belonging to the notified village/ward or local area of the concerned Anganwadi centre are eligible to apply, as per UP Anganwadi norms. Candidates must also satisfy all conditions mentioned in Government Order Shासनादेश संख्या‑2025/3313/58‑1‑2025 (1917687) dated 17 September 2025 and any district‑specific instructions issued by the District Programme Officer.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for UP Anganwadi Worker in Lucknow?
faq.answer: For the current UP Anganwadi Worker cycle, selection is typically done through a departmental process based primarily on academic merit and document verification, without any written exam or interview in many districts. However, candidates should follow the exact procedure given in the Lucknow notification and on upanganwadibharti.in, as district‑wise instructions may specify additional steps such as scrutiny and local‑level approval.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale or honorarium for Anganwadi Workers in Lucknow?
faq.answer: Anganwadi Workers in Uttar Pradesh are engaged on a contractual/honorarium basis with a fixed monthly amount as per the latest Government of Uttar Pradesh orders for ICDS Anganwadi centres. The exact honorarium payable in Lucknow for 2026 will be as per government norms and may be mentioned in the detailed district notification or related government orders referenced therein.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for UP Anganwadi Worker Lucknow Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: For the 2026 UP Anganwadi Worker recruitment cycle across districts, there is generally no application fee for any category, and candidates can submit forms free of cost through upanganwadibharti.in. Candidates should nevertheless check the Lucknow notice and online form instructions to confirm whether any district‑specific fee or charge has been prescribed.

basic_details.job_title: Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 93 Peon, Steno and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Stenographer Grade-III, Process Server, Peon, Copyist
basic_details.short_information: Principal District & Sessions Court, Mysuru has released the official Stenographer Grade-III, Process Server, Peon, Copyist Notification 2026 on 28th April 2026 at mysuru.dcourts.gov.in. A total of 93 vacancies have been announced under the Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 45 years (relaxation for reserved categories) with a qualification of SSC/12th Pass (depending on the post) can apply online from 28th April 2026 to 31st May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

application_fee.details: For Process Server Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 150/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Peon Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 200/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 100/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Copyist Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category-1 Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online | For Stenographer Grade-III Posts | For General Candidates: Rs. 300/- For Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B Candidates: Rs. 150/- For SC/ ST, Category-1, PH Candidates: Nil Mode of Payment: Online

age_limit.details: Age Limit (as on the last date of application): | Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: General: 40 years Cat-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B: 43 years SC/ST/Category-1: 45 years | Age Relaxation: Additional 5 years relaxation (one-time measure) as per Government Order & High Court directions (valid up to 31.12.2027). Further relaxations applicable for Ex-Servicemen, PWD, Widows, etc.

qualification.details: Educational Qualification: | Stenographer Grade-III: PUC / 12th or equivalent + Senior Grade Typewriting & Shorthand (Kannada & English) or 3 years Diploma in Commercial Practice. Copyist: PUC / 12th or equivalent + Junior Grade Typewriting (Kannada & English). Process Server & Peon: SSLC / 10th or equivalent. | Computer Knowledge: Knowledge of computer operation is essential for all posts.

selection_process: Selection will be made as per Karnataka Subordinate Courts (Recruitment) Rules.
Stenographer Grade-III & Copyist: Skill Test (Shorthand/Typing) + Computer Test + Interview (5% weightage). Process Server & Peon: Merit based on SSLC marks + Interview (10 marks).
Selected candidates will be appointed under New Pension Scheme (NPS) and will be on 2 years probation.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted **online only**. The step-by-step process is given below:
Visit the official website: https://mysuru.dcourts.gov.in/ or go to Online Recruitment section.
Click on the respective recruitment notification link and online application link.
Register and fill the online application form with correct details.
Upload required scanned documents and photograph.
Pay the prescribed application fee through State Bank Collect (SBI).
Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.
important_instructions: Only online applications will be accepted. Candidates must possess valid documents/certificates as on the last date of application. Application fee is non-refundable. Age and qualifications will be calculated as on the last date of application. Selected candidates will be subject to verification of documents and medical fitness. For detailed information, refer to the official notifications.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026 is 31 May 2026 (up to 11:59 PM). The online application portal opened on 28 April 2026, giving candidates approximately one month to complete their registration through the official website mysuru.dcourts.gov.in. The last date for payment of application fee is 02 June 2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 93 vacancies have been announced under the Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026 by the Principal District and Sessions Court, Mysuru. The post-wise distribution includes: Peon - 75 posts, Process Server - 11 posts, Stenographer Grade-III - 4 posts, and Copyist - 3 posts. These are Group-C posts in the Mysuru District Judicial Unit. The vacancies are distributed across various categories including General, SC, ST, Category-1, and OBC (2A, 2B, 3A, 3B).

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Mysuru District Court posts?
faq.answer: The educational qualification varies by post. For Stenographer Grade-III, candidates must have PUC/12th or equivalent plus Senior Grade Typewriting & Shorthand (Kannada & English) or a 3-year Diploma in Commercial Practice. For Copyist, candidates need PUC/12th or equivalent plus Junior Grade Typewriting (Kannada & English). For Process Server and Peon posts, candidates must have passed SSLC/10th or equivalent. Knowledge of computer operation is essential for all posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years, and the maximum age varies by category as on the last date of application (31 May 2026). For General category candidates, the maximum age is 40 years; for Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates, it is 43 years; and for SC/ST/Category-1 candidates, it is 45 years. Additionally, there is a one-time age relaxation of 5 years (as per Government Order and High Court directions, valid up to 31.12.2027). Further age relaxations are applicable for Ex-Servicemen, PWD, Widows, and other categories as per rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for different posts in Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee varies by post and category. For Stenographer Grade-III: General candidates - Rs. 300/-, Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B - Rs. 150/-, SC/ST/Category-1/PH - Nil. For Process Server: General - Rs. 300/-, Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B - Rs. 150/-, SC/ST/Category-1/PH - Nil. For Peon: General - Rs. 200/-, Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B - Rs. 100/-, SC/ST/Category-1/PH - Nil. For Copyist: General - Rs. 300/-, Category-1 - Nil. The fee must be paid online through State Bank Collect (SBI), and it is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q6. What is the pay scale for Mysuru District Court posts?
faq.answer: The pay scales for different posts are as follows: Stenographer Grade-III - Rs. 44,425 – 83,700/-, Process Server - Rs. 31,775 – 61,300/-, Copyist - Rs. 34,100 – 67,600/-, and Peon - Rs. 27,000 – 46,675/-. These pay scales are as per the Karnataka Government pay structure. Selected candidates will be appointed under the New Pension Scheme (NPS) and will be on probation for 2 years.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process varies by post as per Karnataka Subordinate Courts (Recruitment) Rules. For Stenographer Grade-III and Copyist, selection is based on Skill Test (Shorthand/Typing) + Computer Test + Interview (5% weightage). For Process Server and Peon, selection is merit-based on SSLC marks plus Interview (10 marks weightage). Selected candidates will be subject to verification of documents and medical fitness before final appointment.

faq.question: Q8. Is knowledge of Kannada language mandatory for Mysuru District Court posts?
faq.answer: Yes, knowledge of Kannada language is essential for all posts under Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026. For skill-based posts like Stenographer Grade-III, candidates must possess Senior Grade Typewriting & Shorthand in both Kannada and English. For Copyist, Junior Grade Typewriting in Kannada and English is required. Since the posts are in Karnataka state courts, proficiency in Kannada is desirable and may be tested during the selection process.

faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Mysuru District Court Recruitment 2026 is 31 May 2026 (up to 11:59 PM). basic_details.job_title: Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 271 Forester, Forest Guard Posts
basic_details.short_information: Tripura Forest Department has released the official Forest Guard & Forester Notification 2026 on 28th April 2026 at forest.tripura.gov.in. A total of 271 vacancies have been announced under the Tripura Forest Guard & Forester Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 40 years with a qualification of Madhyamik (Class 10) for Forest Guard and Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) for Forester can apply online from 1st May 2026 to 31st May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

age_limit.details: Age Limit: 18-40 years as on 01 May 2026. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is admissible for SC/ST candidates and Government employees.

qualification.details: Before applying, candidates must ensure they fulfill the eligibility criteria. Applicants must possess Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC). | Educational Qualification: | Forest Guard: Madhyamik (Class 10) passed or equivalent. Forester: Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) passed or equivalent. | Desirable: Knowledge in Bengali or Kok-borok.

salary_details.pay_scale: Forest Guard: Level-6 (Rs. 5700-24000 + GP 2100) Forester: Level-7 (Rs. 5700-24000 + GP 2200)

selection_process: Selection will be done through:
Written Examination (85% marks) Interview (15% marks)
Only candidates securing cut-off marks in the written test will be called for interview. Final selection is subject to medical fitness.
Written Test Syllabus:
For Forest Guard: Mathematics (20)
English (30)
General Knowledge (120)
Interview (30). Bengali/Kokborok is qualifying in nature (Min 40%).
For Forester: Mathematics (20)
English (20)
General Knowledge (100)
Science (30)

physical_eligibility.details: Candidates should possess good physique and sound health. Persons with disabilities are not eligible. | Minimum Physical Standards: | Physical Test (Qualifying in nature): | Distance to be covered on foot in 4 hours: | Male: 25 Km Female: 16 Km | Only candidates who qualify in the Physical Test will proceed further.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted in online mode only through the official portal. The step-by-step process is as follows:
Visit the official recruitment portal: https://forestrecruitment.tripura.gov.in
Register and fill the online application form carefully.
Upload required documents and submit the form.
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.
Candidates applying for both Forest Guard and Forester posts need not appear twice for Physical Test.
important_instructions: Only online applications will be accepted. No physical applications are entertained. Candidates must have Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC). Women candidates must submit a self-declaration that they are not pregnant on the date of Physical Test. No TA/DA will be paid for attending any stage of recruitment. The vacancies are subject to increase or decrease. Candidates who have applied for both posts are not required to appear for physical test twice. For detailed syllabus and selection rules, refer to the official notification and Annexures.

faq.question: Q1. hat is the last date to apply for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026 is 31 May 2026 (up to 5:30 PM). The online application portal will open on 01 May 2026, giving candidates a full month to complete their registration through the official recruitment portal forestrecruitment.tripura.gov.in. All applications must be submitted online only - no physical applications will be accepted.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: A total of 271 vacancies have been announced under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Out of these, 167 posts are for Forest Guard (Group-C, Non-Gazetted) and 104 posts are for Forester (Group-C, Non-Gazetted). The category-wise distribution for Forest Guard includes UR-80 (including Ex-SM-02), SC-21, and ST-66 (including Ex-SM-01), while Forester posts include UR-39 (including Ex-SM-01), SC-21, and ST-44 (including Ex-SM-01). Additionally, 33% horizontal reservation for women is provided across both posts.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: For Forest Guard posts, candidates must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10) or equivalent examination. For Forester posts, candidates must have passed Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) or equivalent examination. Knowledge in Bengali or Kok-borok is desirable for both positions. Candidates must possess the prescribed educational qualification on or before the closing date of application.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 to 40 years of age as on 01 May 2026. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is admissible for SC/ST candidates and Government employees. The age calculation date is 01 May 2026 for all applicants. Candidates should verify their age eligibility carefully before applying, as this is a mandatory criterion.

faq.question: Q5. Is Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) mandatory for application?
faq.answer: Yes, possession of a valid Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) is absolutely mandatory for all applicants. Candidates without a valid PRTC are not eligible to apply for these posts under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Only bonafide residents of Tripura State with valid PRTC documentation can submit applications for Forest Guard and Forester positions.

faq.question: Q6. What are the physical standards required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: The minimum physical standards vary by category and gender. For Male (UR & SC): minimum height 163 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Male (ST): minimum height 152 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (UR & SC): minimum height 150 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (ST): minimum height 145 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion. Candidates should possess good physique and sound health, and persons with disabilities are not eligible.

faq.question: Q7. What is the Physical Efficiency Test for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Physical Efficiency Test (PET) is qualifying in nature and requires candidates to cover a specified distance on foot within 4 hours. Male candidates must cover 25 km within 4 hours, while female candidates must cover 16 km within 4 hours. Only candidates who qualify in the Physical Test will proceed to the next stage of selection. Women candidates must submit a self-declaration that they are not pregnant on the date of the Physical Test.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: Selection will be conducted through a Written Examination (carrying 85% marks weightage) followed by an Interview (carrying 15% marks weightage). Only candidates securing cut-off marks in the written test will be called for the interview. Final selection is subject to medical fitness. For Forest Guard, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (30 marks), General Knowledge (120 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok being qualifying in nature (minimum 40% required), plus Interview (30 marks). For Forester, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (20 marks), General Knowledge (100 marks), Science (30 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok qualifying (minimum 40%), plus Interview (30 marks).

faq.question: hat is the last date to apply for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026 is 31 May 2026 (up to 5:30 PM). The online application portal will open on 01 May 2026, giving candidates a full month to complete their registration through the official recruitment portal forestrecruitment.tripura.gov.in. All applications must be submitted online only - no physical applications will be accepted.

faq.question: How many vacancies are announced for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: A total of 271 vacancies have been announced under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Out of these, 167 posts are for Forest Guard (Group-C, Non-Gazetted) and 104 posts are for Forester (Group-C, Non-Gazetted). The category-wise distribution for Forest Guard includes UR-80 (including Ex-SM-02), SC-21, and ST-66 (including Ex-SM-01), while Forester posts include UR-39 (including Ex-SM-01), SC-21, and ST-44 (including Ex-SM-01). Additionally, 33% horizontal reservation for women is provided across both posts.

faq.question: What is the educational qualification required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: For Forest Guard posts, candidates must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10) or equivalent examination. For Forester posts, candidates must have passed Higher Secondary (+2 Stage) or equivalent examination. Knowledge in Bengali or Kok-borok is desirable for both positions. Candidates must possess the prescribed educational qualification on or before the closing date of application.

faq.question: What is the age limit for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 to 40 years of age as on 01 May 2026. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is admissible for SC/ST candidates and Government employees. The age calculation date is 01 May 2026 for all applicants. Candidates should verify their age eligibility carefully before applying, as this is a mandatory criterion.

faq.question: Is Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) mandatory for application?
faq.answer: Yes, possession of a valid Permanent Resident Certificate of Tripura (PRTC) is absolutely mandatory for all applicants. Candidates without a valid PRTC are not eligible to apply for these posts under the Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026. Only bonafide residents of Tripura State with valid PRTC documentation can submit applications for Forest Guard and Forester positions.

faq.question: What are the physical standards required for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: The minimum physical standards vary by category and gender. For Male (UR & SC): minimum height 163 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Male (ST): minimum height 152 cm, chest girth 79 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (UR & SC): minimum height 150 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion; For Female (ST): minimum height 145 cm, chest girth 74 cm (normal) with 5 cm expansion. Candidates should possess good physique and sound health, and persons with disabilities are not eligible.

faq.question: What is the Physical Efficiency Test for Tripura Forest Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Physical Efficiency Test (PET) is qualifying in nature and requires candidates to cover a specified distance on foot within 4 hours. Male candidates must cover 25 km within 4 hours, while female candidates must cover 16 km within 4 hours. Only candidates who qualify in the Physical Test will proceed to the next stage of selection. Women candidates must submit a self-declaration that they are not pregnant on the date of the Physical Test.

faq.question: What is the selection process for Tripura Forest Guard and Forester posts?
faq.answer: Selection will be conducted through a Written Examination (carrying 85% marks weightage) followed by an Interview (carrying 15% marks weightage). Only candidates securing cut-off marks in the written test will be called for the interview. Final selection is subject to medical fitness. For Forest Guard, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (30 marks), General Knowledge (120 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok being qualifying in nature (minimum 40% required), plus Interview (30 marks). For Forester, the written test includes Mathematics (20 marks), English (20 marks), General Knowledge (100 marks), Science (30 marks), with Bengali/Kokborok qualifying (minimum 40%), plus Interview (30 marks).

basic_details.job_title: NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 29 LDC, UDC and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: National Investigation Agency (NIA), Ministry of Home Affairs
basic_details.post_name: Section Officer/Office Superintendent, Assistant, Stenographer Grade-I, UDC, LDC
basic_details.short_information: National Investigation Agency (NIA) Ministerial Staff Notification 2026 has been released on 18 March 2026 on the official website nia.gov.in. A total of 29 vacancies have been announced through the NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026 on Deputation Basis.

important_dates.last_date_to_apply: Within 02 months from date of publication in Employment News

salary_details.details: In addition, all posts carry DA, HRA, TPT and other allowances as admissible under Central Government orders, plus a Special Security Allowance of 20% of basic pay.

how_to_apply: This recruitment is through deputation basis only and not through direct online application. The process is as follows:
Step 1 – Eligible officers should obtain the Bio-data/CV proforma (Annexure-II) available on the NIA website at nia.gov.in/recruitment-notice.htm.
Step 2 – Fill the Bio-data proforma duly countersigned by the competent authority.
Step 3 – The nominating Ministry / Department / Organization should forward the nomination through proper channel to the DIG (Admin), NIA Hqrs, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003.
Step 4 – Ensure all required documents listed below are attached before dispatching.
Step 5 – Applications must reach NIA Headquarters within 02 months from the date of publication of this advertisement in the Employment News.
Bio-data in the prescribed proforma (Annexure-II), duly countersigned by the competent authority (available at nia.gov.in/recruitment-notice.htm)
Photocopies of APARs dossier from the year 2020–2021 to 2024–25, duly attested
Departmental Enquiry / Vigilance clearance certificate
Integrity Certificate
Details of Major / Minor penalties imposed on the official during the last 10 years
Applications received after the last date or incomplete applications will not be considered
Candidates who apply will not be allowed to withdraw their candidature subsequently
The eligibility criteria, application form, and recruitment rules are also available on the NIA website at nia.gov.in/recruitment-notice.htm
The advertised number of posts is subject to variation

faq.question: Q1. What is NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026 on deputation basis?
faq.answer: NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026 is a deputation-based recruitment by the National Investigation Agency (NIA) to fill 29 posts of Section Officer/Office Superintendent, Assistant, Stenographer Grade‑I, Upper Division Clerk (UDC) and Lower Division Clerk (LDC) from eligible serving Central/State/UT Government employees.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced under NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 29 vacancies have been notified, including 3 posts of Section Officer/Office Superintendent, 3 of Assistant, 14 of Stenographer Grade‑I, 3 of UDC and 6 of LDC, and the number of posts is subject to variation depending on requirement.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of nominations is within two months from the date of publication of the advertisement in Employment News, and applications reaching NIA Headquarters after this period will not be considered.

faq.question: Q4. Who can apply for NIA Section Officer/Office Superintendent posts on deputation?
faq.answer: Officers of Central/State Governments or Union Territories holding analogous posts on regular basis or with at least five years’ regular service in Level‑6, possessing a Bachelor’s Degree and certified computer proficiency in word processing, spreadsheets and presentations, and not exceeding 56 years of age on the closing date are eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What are the eligibility criteria for NIA Assistant and Stenographer Grade‑I posts?
faq.answer: For Assistant, officers must hold analogous posts or have six years’ service in Level‑5, possess a Bachelor’s Degree and have passed the prescribed computer proficiency test; Stenographer Grade‑I requires officers from the stenographers’ cadre holding analogous posts or with prescribed regular service in Level‑5/Level‑4, a graduate degree, computer proficiency and stenography skill at 100 wpm with specified transcription timings.

faq.question: Q6. What are the eligibility requirements for NIA UDC and LDC posts on deputation?
faq.answer: UDC requires officers holding analogous posts or having eight years’ regular service in Level‑2 and at least 12th pass qualification; LDC requires officers already working as LDC (analogous post) on regular basis with 12th pass or equivalent from a recognized board, and in both cases the maximum age is 56 years on the closing date.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale and allowances for NIA Ministerial Staff posts?
faq.answer: The pay matrix is Level‑7 (Rs 44,900–1,42,400) for Section Officer/Office Superintendent, Level‑6 (Rs 35,400–1,12,400) for Assistant and Stenographer Grade‑I, Level‑4 (Rs 25,500–81,100) for UDC and Level‑2 (Rs 19,900–63,200) for LDC, along with DA, HRA, transport and other admissible allowances plus a Special Security Allowance of 20 percent of basic pay.

faq.question: Q8. What is the period of deputation for NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The period of deputation for all advertised posts shall ordinarily not exceed three years, including any prior deputation in another ex‑cadre post, and deputationists will not be considered for promotion in NIA’s direct line of promotion.

faq.question: Q9. How can eligible officers apply for NIA Ministerial Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible officers must fill the prescribed bio‑data proforma (Annexure‑II) available on the NIA website, get it duly countersigned by the competent authority, and have their parent Ministry/Department/Organization forward the complete nomination through proper channel to the DIG (Admin), NIA Headquarters, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003 within the stipulated time.

faq.question: Q10. Which documents are required to be sent along with the NIA deputation application?
faq.answer: The nominating authority must forward the bio‑data proforma along with duly attested photocopies of APARs from 2020‑21 to 2024‑25, vigilance/disciplinary clearance, integrity certificate, and details of any major or minor penalties imposed in the last ten years; incomplete applications or those lacking required enclosures are liable to be rejected.

basic_details.job_title: Konkan Railway Recruitment 2026 Notification Out – Walk in for 132 Helper, Technician and Other Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL), A Government of India Undertaking under Ministry of Railways
basic_details.post_name: VDPD Workshop, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh (Selected candidates can be posted and transferred anywhere to KRCL establishment during contract period)
basic_details.short_information: Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL) Recruitment 2026: Get the latest KRCL recruitment notification 2026 for 132 vacancies across 9 posts in the Mechanical/Project Department on a fixed term contract basis under Notification No. CO/P-R/7C/2026. The Walk-in Interview process starts on 8th June 2026 and the last walk-in interview date is 12th June 2026 at Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. Find KRCL vacancies on the official website www.konkanrailway.com. You can also get Free Job Alert of “Upcoming KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026” notifications for freshers and experienced candidat

age_limit.age_details: Fixed Term Contract – Initial period of 1 year, extendable up to 5 years based on satisfactory performance and requirement of KRCL
age_limit.details: The upper age limit is 50 years for all posts as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is provided to reserved category candidates as per Government guidelines. Age relaxation for Ex-servicemen is discretionary.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 01-05-2026. Candidates must ensure they fulfill the criteria before attending the walk-in interview for a particular post. Incomplete or vague educational qualification will be invalid. Post-wise eligibility details are as follows: | Educational Qualification & Experience (Post-wise)

salary_details.details: The consolidated monthly remuneration is inclusive of Basic, DA, HRA, Conveyance, and Mobile Allowance. Remuneration is classified as per A Class, B Class, and C Class cities. Annual remuneration will increase by 4% of the consolidated remuneration after completion of every year, subject to requirement of KRCL and at the discretion of KRCL. No accommodation and no Food/Mess Allowance will be provided by KRCL. | Other Allowances/Facilities: Insurance, Payment of Gratuity, and Bonus as per provisions of New Labour Code-2026 (as applicable). TA as per extant KRCL policy for official travel beyond the project area. Complementary Railway Pass for travel on duty as per extant KRCL policy. Rest House of KRCL if available while on duty; otherwise hotel charge reimbursement as per KRCL rates on production of vouchers. 15 days fully earned leave on completion of every 6 months (2.5 leaves per month); 8 days Casual Leave during the year. Weekly off and public holidays as applicable at place of posting.

selection_process: Stage Details
Preliminary Screening Verification of applications and documents submitted in person. Only provisionally eligible candidates allowed to proceed.
Elimination Round (if required) Group Discussion (GD) or Written Test may be introduced depending on number of candidates.
Interview By nominated Committee of KRCL. Merit list drawn based on performance in interview, qualification, and post-qualification experience.

The selection process for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026 is through Walk-in Interview. Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria may report directly for the walk-in interview at the specified venue with all original certificates and one copy of application in prescribed format (Annexure-A) along with one set of self-attested copies of all required certificates. Registration will be done between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs only on the date of walk-in interview.
After preliminary screening of applications submitted in person

only provisionally eligible candidates will be interviewed. Group Discussion (GD) or any other elimination round like written test may be introduced depending on the number of candidates; only those who clear the elimination round will be called for further selection. The nominated Committee’s decision shall be final and binding. No train/bus fare/TA/DA shall be payable by KRCL for attending the walk-in interview. Candidates should come prepared to stay for a minimum of 2 days if required
at their own cost.
how_to_apply: Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria may report directly for the walk-in interview at the venue on the specified date and time for their respective post. Candidates must fill and bring one copy of the application in the prescribed format (Annexure-A attached with the notification), available on the KRCL website at www.konkanrailway.com under Recruitment > Current Notifications. No online or offline application submission by post/courier is required – candidates must appear in person.

Registration time is strictly 09:00 hrs to 12:00 hrs only on the date of walk-in interview. Applications not conforming to the prescribed format or having illegible/ambiguous certificates or without certificates or incomplete will be summarily rejected. Any kind of canvassing will result in disqualification of the candidate at any stage of selection without assigning any reason.
Documents (Self-attested copies) to be attached with the filled application form:
Copies of certificate in proof of qualification (as per the qualification specified in the Notification Annexure)
Copy of proof of Date of Birth (SSLC/SSC Certificate/Birth Certificate)
Copy of Caste certificate in support of claims, if any (OBC-NCL certificate in prescribed Central Government format; certificate not indicating validity will not be accepted)
Copy of service certificate in support of claims for Ex-servicemen, if any
Experience Certificate issued by previous employer
Two passport size recent photographs
Character certificate from Gazetted Officer/Executive Officer certifying good moral character

All original certificates must also be brought for verification at the time of the walk-in interview. Selected candidates will be required to pass a prescribed medical examination before the contract is entered into. Selected candidates must be able to join within a maximum of 30 days after issuance of offer of appointment. Selected candidates will be required to execute an agreement and indemnity bond of Rs. 50,000/- in favour of KRCL on stamp paper of Rs. 100/- or more.
Walk-in Interview Venue: Administrative Building, Wagon Repair Workshop, Autonagar, Vadlapudi, Gajuwaka, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
important_instructions: Candidates must ensure they fulfill the eligibility criteria before attending the walk-in interview for a particular post. Registration time is strictly 09:00 hrs to 12:00 hrs only on the date of the walk-in interview; late arrivals will not be registered. Candidate can appear for interview at this one venue only; no TA/DA will be paid for attending the walk-in interview. This engagement is on a fixed term contract basis and shall not result in regular/permanent absorption in KRCL service. Candidates must give an undertaking in writing to KRCL to this effect. Selected candidate can be posted and transferred at any time, anywhere to the KRCL establishment during the contract period. Contract may be terminated by giving one month’s notice from either side or payment in lieu thereof. Contract can be terminated without notice if performance is not satisfactory. No accommodation will be provided and no Food/Mess Allowance will be paid by KRCL. Even after contractual agreement, if any document/certificate/information is found incorrect or false, contractual service will be immediately terminated without prior notice, besides legal action. Candidates who are already working in KRCL on contract basis and selected against this notification do not require to follow the condition of notice period for joining the new post; however, a relieving letter from the concerned Konkan Railway authority will be required at the time of joining. The Corporation reserves the right to cancel/restrict/curtail/enlarge the contract engagement process without any prior notice and without assigning any reason. Any kind of canvassing will result in disqualification of the candidate at any stage of selection without assigning any reason. Statutory Income Tax, Professional Tax, and other applicable taxes will be deducted at source. The information on KRCL’s website www.konkanrailway.com at its link Recruitment > Current Notifications should be checked for updates.

faq.question: Q1. What is KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026 is a fixed term contract recruitment by Konkan Railway Corporation Limited to engage 132 candidates across 9 posts in the Mechanical/Project Department for VDPD Workshop, Visakhapatnam, under Notification No. CO/P‑R/7C/2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under KRCL Recruitment 2026 and for which posts?
faq.answer: A total of 132 vacancies are announced for Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical, Quality Inspector, Technician (Welder), Technician (Fitter), Technician (EOT Operator), Vehicle Driver, Lister/Forklift Operator, Hydra Operator, and Office Boy/Helper/Khalasi posts in the Mechanical/Project Department.

faq.question: Q3. What are the walk‑in interview dates and venue for KRCL Mechanical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Walk‑in interviews will be held from 08 June 2026 to 12 June 2026 (post‑wise scheduled dates) between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs at the Administrative Building, Wagon Repair Workshop, Autonagar, Vadlapudi, Gajuwaka, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.

faq.question: Q4. What is the nature and duration of engagement in KRCL Mechanical Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The engagement is purely on fixed term contract for an initial period of 1 year, which can be extended up to a maximum of 5 years based on satisfactory performance, conduct, and the requirement of KRCL, without any claim for regular absorption.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility and educational qualification for Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical in KRCL?
faq.answer: For Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical, candidates must have a full‑time Degree or Diploma in Mechanical Engineering or related branches (Production & Industrial, Automobile, Manufacturing, Mechatronic, Material Sciences, Metallurgical) from an AICTE‑recognized institute, along with at least 1 year post‑qualification experience, preferably in repair workshops or manufacturing units.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For all posts, the upper age limit is 50 years as on 01 May 2026, with relaxations for OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer) and SC/ST candidates as per government rules, while Ex‑servicemen age relaxation is as per government guidelines.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary structure for KRCL Mechanical contract posts?
faq.answer: KRCL offers consolidated monthly remuneration varying by post and city class; for example, Junior Technical Assistant/Mechanical gets around Rs. 47,220 in A‑Class cities (including Basic, DA, HRA, conveyance and mobile allowance), with slightly lower consolidated pay for B and C class city postings, and annual 4% increment on completion of each year, subject to KRCL’s discretion.

faq.question: Q8. Is accommodation or food provided to KRCL Mechanical Department contract staff?
faq.answer: No, KRCL does not provide accommodation or food/mess allowance to contract employees; however, certain benefits like insurance, gratuity and bonus as per applicable labour codes, TA for duty travel, railway pass for official journeys, and leave benefits are provided as per KRCL rules.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a walk‑in process involving preliminary screening of documents, and based on the number of applicants, an elimination round such as Group Discussion or written test may be conducted, followed by an interview by a nominated committee that prepares a merit list considering qualification and post‑qualification experience.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for KRCL Mechanical Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no online application; eligible candidates must download and fill the prescribed application form (Annexure‑A) from the KRCL website, bring the duly filled form along with original certificates and self‑attested photocopies to the walk‑in venue on the specified date and time for their post, and register between 09:00 hrs and 12:00 hrs only.

basic_details.job_title: GESCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 275 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company Limited (GESCOM), Kalaburagi
basic_details.post_name: Apprentice - BE (Electrical & Electronics) Electrical Roster and Diploma (Electrical) Electrical Roster
basic_details.department: Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company Limited (GESCOM) - Karnataka Government Undertaking
basic_details.short_information: Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company Limited (GESCOM) has released the official Apprentice - BE (Electrical & Electronics) Electrical Roster and Diploma (Electrical) Electrical Roster Notification 2026 on 12 May 2026 at gescom.karnataka.gov.in. A total of 275 vacancies have been announced under the GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 18 and 40 years (for UR candidates) and 18 and 45 years (for SC/ST candidates) with a qualification of BE in Electrical/Electronics or a 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engineering can apply offline from 13 May 2026 to 05 June 2026

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of application. Only candidates who have passed their BE / Diploma in Electrical or Electronics Engineering after January 2022 are eligible to apply for 2026–27 apprenticeship training. Candidates who completed their BE in Electrical and Electronics Engineering or 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engineering after January 2022 from a State/Central Government recognized or Government-aided institution are eligible. Candidates must be able to read and write Kannada. Priority will be given to Karnataka state candidates. Physically fit candidates will be given preference; differently-abled candidates are not eligible to apply. Candidates undergoing apprenticeship training in any other organization or currently employed elsewhere are not eligible. Candidates who have already completed their apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 are not eligible. | Educational Qualification

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 9000/- per month (Engineering Degree holders) | Rs. 8000/- per month (Diploma in Engineering holders)
salary_details.details: Note: Apprentices will not be entitled to food, accommodation, or any other allowances during the training period. The training organization (GESCOM) has no obligation to provide employment after the training period, nor is there any right to claim employment.

selection_process: Candidates will be shortlisted for apprenticeship training on the basis of merit derived from their marks in the qualifying examination (BE / Diploma). The application must clearly state "For 2026–27 Apprenticeship Training - BE / Diploma holder" at the top. Candidates will be selected purely on merit based on qualifying examination marks. No written examination or interview is mentioned in the notification.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted offline only - either in person or by post. Online applications are not accepted. Completed application forms (self-written or typed) along with all required documents in self-attested copies must be submitted to the Principal Managing Officer (HR & Admin), GESCOM Corporate Office, Station Road, Jestaon, Kalaburagi – 585102, on or before 05-06-2026 by 5:00 PM. Applications submitted by post must reach the above address within the stipulated date and time. Incomplete applications or applications without required documents will be rejected. No correspondence of any kind (letters or phone calls) will be entertained. Candidates are advised to write "For 2026–27 Apprenticeship Training - BE / Diploma holder" clearly on the envelope.
Documents to be Attached with Application:
SSC (10th) marks card attested by Gazetted Officer (Xerox copy)
PUC (12th) marks card attested by Gazetted Officer (Xerox copy)
All semester marks cards attested by Gazetted Officer (Xerox copies)
All semester Diploma marks cards attested by Gazetted Officer (Xerox copies)
Degree Certificate / Provisional Degree Certificate (PDC) / Diploma Certificate (Xerox copies)
Caste certificate - Xerox copy (attested by Gazetted Officer)
Women candidates - Xerox copy of Government Hospital District Surgeon's medical fitness certificate attested by Gazetted Officer
Character certificate from two Gazetted Officers (Xerox copy)
Three recent passport-size photographs (one to be attached with the application)
A self-addressed envelope with Rs. 25/- stamp affixed, 4x9 inch size - one envelope to be submitted along with the application
Application to be submitted to: The Principal Managing Officer (HR & Admin), GESCOM Corporate Office, Station Road, Jestaon, Kalaburagi – 585102
important_instructions: Only candidates who have passed BE (Electrical / Electronics) or Diploma (Electrical) after January 2022 are eligible to apply for 2026–27 apprenticeship training. Candidates must be able to read and write Kannada; preference will be given to Karnataka state candidates. Differently-abled candidates are not eligible to apply. Candidates currently undergoing apprenticeship training in any other organization or currently employed are not eligible. Candidates who have already completed apprenticeship under the Apprentices Act, 1961 are not eligible. Candidates who applied for apprenticeship in any other organization under the Apprentices Act, 1961, or who are currently undergoing such training, are ineligible (applications can be rejected at any stage). Applications must clearly mention "For 2026–27 Apprenticeship Training - BE / Diploma holder" on the envelope. Incomplete applications or applications without all required documents will be summarily rejected. No correspondence (letters or phone calls) of any kind will be entertained after submission. Candidates will not be entitled to food, accommodation, or any other benefits during training. Completion of apprenticeship training does not confer any right to employment in GESCOM. Apprentices-in-training cannot raise any demand for rights or regularization of employment at any stage. Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official GESCOM website gescom.karnataka.gov.in for updates.

faq.question: Q1. What is GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 is a one‑year apprenticeship training program under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme for 275 Engineering Degree and Diploma holders in Electrical/Electronics with Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company Limited (GESCOM), a Karnataka Government Undertaking, for the training year 2026‑27.

faq.question: Q2. How many apprenticeship seats are available in GESCOM Apprenticeship 2026–27?
faq.answer: A total of 275 apprenticeship seats are notified, including 175 positions for BE (Electrical & Electronics) Electrical Roster apprentices and 100 positions for Diploma (Electrical) Electrical Roster apprentices, with separate counts for general and women candidates in each stream.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The apprenticeship notification is dated 12 May 2026, and the last date for receipt of offline applications at the GESCOM Corporate Office, Kalaburagi is 05 June 2026 up to 5:00 PM for the 2026–27 training year.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility and educational qualification for GESCOM BE & Diploma Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed BE/B.Tech in Electrical or Electronics Engineering or a 3‑year Diploma in Electrical Engineering from State/Central Government recognized or Government‑aided institutions after January 2022, and must be able to read and write Kannada, with preference given to Karnataka state candidates.

faq.question: Q5. Are candidates who have already done apprenticeship or are currently employed eligible for GESCOM Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: No, candidates who have already completed apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961, those currently undergoing apprenticeship in any other organization, or those presently employed elsewhere are not eligible to apply for GESCOM Apprenticeship 2026–27.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend for GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selected Engineering Degree apprentices will receive a stipend of Rs. 9,000 per month, while Diploma apprentices will receive Rs. 8,000 per month during the one‑year apprenticeship training under GESCOM.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any job guarantee in GESCOM after completion of apprenticeship training?
faq.answer: No, GESCOM clearly states that apprentices will not be entitled to employment after completion of the one‑year apprenticeship, and they cannot claim any right to regular appointment or other benefits based on this training.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for GESCOM BE & Diploma Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: The selection of candidates is purely merit‑based, prepared from the marks obtained in the qualifying examination (BE/Diploma), and the notification does not mention any separate written exam or interview for the apprenticeship selection.

faq.question: Q9. How to apply for GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 and where to send the application?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted offline only by writing or typing the application, attaching self‑attested copies of all required certificates and documents, and sending or submitting it in person to the Principal Managing Officer (HR & Admin), GESCOM Corporate Office, Station Road, Jestaon, Kalaburagi – 585102, clearly superscribing “For 2026–27 Apprenticeship Training – BE/Diploma holder” on the envelope.

faq.question: Q10. Which documents are required to be attached with the GESCOM Apprenticeship application form?
faq.answer: Applicants must attach self‑attested copies of SSLC and PUC marks cards, all semester marks cards for Degree/Diploma, Degree/Provisional Degree or Diploma certificate, caste certificate (if applicable), medical fitness certificate for women, two character certificates from Gazetted Officers, three recent passport photos, and a self‑addressed stamped envelope of 4x9 inch size along with the application.

faq.question: What is GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026 is a one‑year apprenticeship training program under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme for 275 Engineering Degree and Diploma holders in Electrical/Electronics with Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company Limited (GESCOM), a Karnataka Government Undertaking, for the training year 2026‑27.

faq.question: How many apprenticeship seats are available in GESCOM Apprenticeship 2026–27?
faq.answer: A total of 275 apprenticeship seats are notified, including 175 positions for BE (Electrical & Electronics) Electrical Roster apprentices and 100 positions for Diploma (Electrical) Electrical Roster apprentices, with separate counts for general and women candidates in each stream.

faq.question: What are the important dates for GESCOM Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The apprenticeship notification is dated 12 May 2026, and the last date for receipt of offline applications at the GESCOM Corporate Office, Kalaburagi is 05 June 2026 up to 5:00 PM for the 2026–27 training year.

faq.question: What is the eligibility and educational qualification for GESCOM BE & Diploma Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed BE/B.Tech in Electrical or Electronics Engineering or a 3‑year Diploma in Electrical Engineering from State/Central Government recognized or Government‑aided institutions after January 2022, and must be able to read and write Kannada, with preference given to Karnataka state candidates.

faq.answer: No, GESCOM clearly states that apprentices will not be entitled to employment after completion of the one‑year apprenticeship, and they cannot claim any right to regular appointment or other benefits based on this training.

basic_details.job_title: RRB ALP (CEN 01/2026) Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 11,127 Assistant Loco Pilot Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), Ministry of Railways, Government of India
basic_details.department: Ministry of Railways, Government of India (Various Zonal Railways)
basic_details.official_website: Refer RRB-wise websites listed below (e.g., www.rrbald.gov.in, www.rrbmumbai.gov.in, etc.)
basic_details.short_information: The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) have officially announced the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) Recruitment 2026 with 11,127 openings. The detailed notification was published on 14 May 2026. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 30 years and hold Matriculation + ITI or Diploma/Degree in Engineering can now submit their online applications from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

important_dates.cbt-1 / cbt-2 / cbat date: To be notified on official RRB websites

application_fee.sc_fee: Rs. 250/- (Rupees Two Hundred and Fifty only)
application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the non-refundable recruitment fee online. Fee can be paid only through online mode - internet banking, debit/credit cards, or UPI. Applications received without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Candidates who appear in CBT-1 will receive a refund of their examination fee (after deducting applicable bank charges) credited to their Aadhaar-seeded bank account. Candidates whose applications are rejected, or who do not appear in CBT-1, will not receive any refund.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age as on 01-07-2026. Age relaxation is available for various categories as detailed below. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation on more than one ground, only the maximum relaxation will be granted (not cumulative). SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR vacancies will not get any age relaxation. There is no fixed limit on the number of attempts - candidates can appear as long as they are within the prescribed upper age limit.

qualification.required_degree: Matriculation / SSLC plus 3-year Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Engineering (or combination of these streams) from a recognised institution in lieu of ITI. Note: Degree in the above Engineering disciplines will also be acceptable in lieu of Diploma.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (14-06-2026). Educational/Technical qualification certificates must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute. Candidates awaiting results of their final examination for the prescribed qualification are NOT eligible to apply. The post of ALP is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD). Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for this post. | Educational Qualification

salary_details.pay_scale: Level-2 in 7th CPC Pay Matrix; Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-

selection_process: Details Particulars
Duration 60 Minutes
Total Questions / Marks 75 Questions / 75 Marks (1 mark per question)
Type Objective MCQ - Computer Based Test
Negative Marking 1/3rd mark deducted per wrong answer
Minimum Qualifying Marks UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Shortlisting for CBT-2 15 times the vacancies notified per RRB (based on CBT-1 merit, RRB-wise)
Syllabus Mathematics, Mental Ability (Reasoning), General Science (10th standard level)
Part Duration Questions Nature Minimum Qualifying Marks
Part-A 90 Minutes 100 Questions / 100 Marks Competitive - marks counted for merit UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Part-B 60 Minutes 75 Questions / 75 Marks Qualifying only - trade-specific 35% for all candidates irrespective of category
Total Duration 175 Questions / 2.5 Hours
Sl. No. Qualification of Candidate Trade / Subject of Choice for Part-B
1 Electrical Engineering (and combinations) Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Wireman, Armature & Coil Winder, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
2 Electronics Engineering (and combinations) Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV)

3 Mechanical Engineering (and combinations) Fitter, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic, Heat Engine, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic
4 Automobile Engineering (and combinations) Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
Shortlisting for CBAT 8 times the ALP vacancies per community/category per RRB (based on Part-A marks of CBT-2)
Qualifying Marks Minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately (all candidates irrespective of category)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking
1st Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of given name (not surname)
The selection process for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 consists of five successive stages - First Stage CBT (CBT-1)
Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)
Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)
Document Verification (DV)

and Medical Examination (ME). CBT-1 is a screening exam only and its marks are not counted in the final panel. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of combined marks from Part-A of CBT-2 (50% weightage) and CBAT scores (50% weightage). Negative marking of 1/3rd mark per wrong answer applies in both CBT-1 and CBT-2. There is no negative marking in CBAT.
Stage 1 - First Stage CBT (CBT-1) - Screening Only
Stage 2 - Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)
Part-A Syllabus (CBT-2): Mathematics
General Intelligence and Reasoning
Basic Science and Engineering (Engineering Drawing
Units
Measurements
Basic Electricity
Levers
IT Literacy
etc.)
Part-B Syllabus (CBT-2) - Trade-wise Qualifying Test:
Stage 3 - Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)
Stage 4 & 5 - Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (ME)
Final merit list is prepared RRB-wise from candidates qualifying in CBAT
with 50% weightage to Part-A of CBT-2 marks and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are shortlisted for DV and Medical Examination. In case of a tie
the older candidate is preferred; if age is also equal
alphabetical order of given name (A to Z) is applied.
Tie-Breaking Rules
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the official websites of participating RRBs (listed at Para 13.7 of the CEN). Hard copy / offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal is active from 15-05-2026 to 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs.). Candidates must first create an account on the RRB portal. Those who created an account for CENs notified in 2024/2025 should use the same credentials. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Candidates can apply to only one RRB and submit only one application - multiple applications to the same or different RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and debarment from future RRB/RRC exams. Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication is strongly advised during account creation for a smoother recruitment process. A live photograph is captured during the online application; pre-existing photographs are not accepted. No documents need to be uploaded at application stage (except signature and SC/ST certificate for free travel pass, if applicable).
Documents to be Produced at Document Verification (on being called):
Age proof - Matriculation / SSLC / 10th class certificate
Educational qualifications (Matriculation onwards) - marksheets and certificates
ITI / Diploma / Degree certificate in prescribed trade / discipline
SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / Ex-Servicemen certificate (if applicable), in prescribed format and issued by competent authority
Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A & VIII B) - to be submitted on CBAT day
NOC from present employer (if applicable, for Government servants)

Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official RRB website for their chosen RRB for updates on all stages. Helpline for technical queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 0172-565-3333 and 9592001188 (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).
Apply Online at: Visit the official website of your chosen RRB (see list below)
important_instructions: Only one application per candidate is accepted - multiple applications to one or more RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and permanent debarment from RRB/RRC exams. Candidates awaiting results of their final prescribed qualification examination are NOT eligible to apply. Candidates who have undergone Lasik or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP. Selection of RRB, once submitted, is final and cannot be changed at any later date. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Examination fee is refundable (after deducting bank charges) only to candidates who appear in CBT-1; rejected or absent candidates are not eligible for refund. Refund will be credited only to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account; candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account. Mobile phones, electronic gadgets, watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, calculators, books, pen, food items, and all other listed banned items are strictly prohibited inside the test centre. Examination centre, date, and shift allotted in the e-call letter are final - no requests for change will be entertained under any circumstances. Candidates must maintain identical signatures throughout all stages of the recruitment process. No BLOCK/CAPITAL/DISJOINTED letter signatures are acceptable. Free Sleeper Class Railway Pass is available for SC/ST candidates for CBT/CBAT/DV/Medical Examination stages (if opted in application). Candidates are advised to visit only official RRB websites and beware of fake websites, unscrupulous agents, and touts claiming to provide Railway jobs through influence or unfair means. RRBs never appoint agents or coaching centres for recruitment.

age_limit.details: All age calculations are as on the cut-off date 13.05.2026. No age relaxation is available for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates applying for FTE positions.
